Lambda cultivates intersectional dialogue among students, faculty, administration, and the greater Nashville community and seeks to provide a voice for all forms of diversity. Our main core principle ultimately resides in our continued fight for human rights everywhere.
summary: The Vanderbilt Lambda Association is Vanderbilt’s undergraduate Gender and Sexuality Alliance. Lambda serves and supports LGBTQ+ students, in addition to providing a voice on LGBTQ+ matters on campus and within the greater Nashville community.

name: Owen Latin Business Association
description:
The Owen Latin American Business Association (LABA) is a student-run organization that brings together members who are passionate about Latin America. It is more than a club. It's a community that’s impactful, empowering and unforgettable for all, fostering cultural pride, while also driving initiatives that amplify our presence and visibility on campus. It’s not just about representing our culture, but rather about celebrating it in a way that leaves a lasting impression too.

LABA's mission is to create awareness about Latin American culture, connect students with the Latin business community, and promote discussion about the unique business opportunities for Latin American students. 

To accomplish its mission, LABA engages the student community in cultural and academic events, such as:

- Latin American Entrepeneur Series - Lunch & Learn

- Cafecito Social Mornings

- Latin American Entrepreneur Series 

- Networking opportunities with the LATAM business community in Nashville

- LABA Cultural Closing Bell

- Dancing lessons, Party Bus and more!
summary: The Owen Latin American Business Association (LABA) is a student-run organization that brings together a community who is passionate about Latin America.

name: Malaysian Student Association at Vanderbilt
description: The Malaysian Student Association at Vanderbilt (MSAV) represents the voice of the Malaysian community at Vanderbilt. We hope to promote a healthy intercultural exchange between us, international, and local students. We also support cultural diversity on campus through our members and events.
summary: The Malaysian Student Association at Vanderbilt (MSAV) represents the voice of the Malaysian community at Vanderbilt to promote a healthy intercultural exchange between us and other students and to support cultural diversity on campus.

name: Minority Association of Pre-Medical Students
description:
The purpose of MAPS is to provide underrepresented pre-medical students with the knowledge, skills, and experience that are both prerequisite & concomitant to professional participation in health care fields, improve minority student matriculation into all professional health-related programs, with an emphasis on medicine, and encourage culturally conscious physicians by increasing awareness of issues that underrepresented communities face.

 

Find us on Instagram @vanderbiltmaps
summary: The Vanderbilt MAPS Chapter is an undergraduate chapter that extends the mission of the Student National Medical Association(SNMA) to foster the needs of minorities students interested in the medical health fields.

name: South Asian Cultural Exchange
description:
SACE is an organization made up of over 500 Vanderbilt undergraduate and graduate students dedicated to representing, celebrating, and sharing South Asian culture at Vanderbilt and in the greater Nashville area. Throughout the year, we host various cultural, social, and educational events, several of which are highlighted below.

 

Garba

​Garba is a traditional folk dance associated with the Indian festival, Navaratri, meaning "nine nights” that has transpired into a profound cultural celebration. In India, this dance is performed in massive group circles revolving around one another, embracing all students. To honor this celebration and share this rich aspect of Indian culture and dance with the Vanderbilt community, SACE hosts a Garba every fall to teach students the Garba dance and celebrate as a community with food, music, dance, and performances.

 

The Dances of South Asia

The Dances of South Asia (TDS) is SACE's largest and most popular event serving to showcase the myriad of dance styles present within South Asia, as well as guest acts from the entire Vanderbilt community. Every year, over 200 students perform in the showcase in dances such as Classical, Raas, Nepali, Pakistani Pop, and Bollywood Fusion segments. In addition to dance performances, TDS presents a fashion show that highlights traditional attire from many different regions within South Asia and a skit centered around each year’s specific theme. TDS is Vanderbilt's largest cultural showcase, attracting over 1000 guests from the Vanderbilt and surrounding Nashville community.

 

Mela

Mela, SACE's annual semiformal event, is a night of celebration. Mela programming includes music, dancing, food, celebration, and cultural fun! The community comes together dressed in their most stylish South Asian (or semiformal) attire. 

 

Holi

This festival of colors, rooted in Indian and Nepalese tradition, never fails to cover Alumni Lawn in a rainbow of colors. Holi is an outdoors festival that welcomes the spring and harvests to come with colored powder (gulal), music, food, and friends. 

 

Chaand Raat

Chaand Raat is a night festival that celebrates the sighting of the moon, marking the end of the month of Ramadan and the beginning of Eid-al-Fitr. SACE celebrates Chaand Raat in collaboration with other cultural orgs to present a night full of of games, food, and fun!

 

The House System & Big/Little

To foster community within SACE, our house system seeks to sort SACE members into one of four houses: Earth, Fire, Air, or Water. Each house is led by 2-3 House Heads that create and promote programming (movie nights, dinners, and social gatherings) within and between the houses, creating opportunities for SACE members to mingle and form meaningful connections within our organization. Additionally, SACE offers a Big/Little mentorship program where underclassmen and upperclassmen students are matched together based on their interests to encourage interclass bonding and exchange of ideas and advice.

 

SACE Speaks

SACE Speaks is SACE's discussion-based cultural education and current event programs, designed to promote awareness of the South Asian experience and diaspora through educational conversations and speaker events. Through independent and collaborative events that highlight complex components and intersectionalities of the South Asian identity, SACE Speaks strives to empower South Asian students at Vanderbilt & provide them with a platform to discuss important issues within the community.
summary: SACE is an organization made up of over 500 Vanderbilt undergraduate and graduate students dedicated to representing, celebrating, and sharing South Asian culture at Vanderbilt and in the greater Nashville area.

name: Middle Eastern and North African Student Association (Formerly MESA)
description: The Middle Eastern and North African Student Association (MENA) at Vanderbilt University seeks to foster a welcoming space for students of MENA heritage and all those interested in our diverse cultures. We are dedicated to sharing and celebrating the rich traditions, histories, and perspectives of the Middle East and North Africa through education, dialogue, and cultural programming. By engaging our peers in meaningful ways, we aim to strengthen community on campus, build cross-cultural understanding, and create opportunities for service that positively impact the greater Nashville community. Through these efforts, we aspire to cultivate knowledge, inclusivity, and lasting connections within and beyond Vanderbilt.
summary: The Middle Eastern and North African Students Association (MENA) is an undergraduate, student-run organization that provides a common ground for Middle Eastern and North African students to come together...

name: National Black Law Student Association
description:
National History:  

The National Black Law Students Association (NBLSA), founded in 1968, is a nationwide organization formed to articulate and promote the needs and goals of Black law students and effectuate change in the legal community. As the largest student-run organization in the country with over 6,000 members, NBLSA includes chapters or affiliates in six different countries, including The Bahamas, Nigeria, and South Africa. NBLSA encourages the development of talented, social conscious lawyers of tomorrow.

Vanderbilt University Undergraduate History: 

The National Black Law Student Association was established on Vanderbilt University's campus in 2005. Our undergraduate chapter is part of the Southern Region sector of the national organization. As an organization, we encourage our members as they embark on the pre-law journey here at Vanderbilt University. As a member, you are given opportunities to personally interact with law school admission representatives, visit law schools, network with legal professionals, receive help in preparation for law school, and connect to pre-law resources offered academically.
summary: The National Black Law Student Association (NBLSA) exists to articulate and promote the educational, professional, political, and social needs and goals of Black Pre-Law students; as well as, foster a community amongst Black pre-law individuals.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
summary: The mission of NSBE is to increase the number of culturally responsible black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community.

name: Organization of Black Graduate and Professional Students
description: The Organization of Black Graduate and Professional Students (OBGAPS) dedicates itself to enhancing the experience of Black graduate and professional students at Vanderbilt University, to advancing the scholarly activities of the Black graduate students and ensuring their successful matriculation though Vanderbilt. OBGAPS strives to facilitate positive outcomes for students across the spectrum of the graduate experience which includes facilitating the transition into post baccalaureate programs, giving academic and professional support, as well as providing opportunities for social networking, cultural exchanges, and service opportunities for its membership. The organization also strives to actively promote the interests of Black graduate students by working to bring the problems, concerns, and needs of the Black graduate and professional students to the attention of the university. In addition to these efforts, OBGAPS promotes the recruitment and retention of Black students with the goal of increasing minority representation among students, staff, and faculty. OBGAPS also seeks to share elements of the Black culture with the larger Vanderbilt University community. Lastly, OBGAPS aims to foster cohesion and collaboration among the various Black graduate and professional student organizations in order to fulfill the aforementioned goals.
summary: Graduate and professional student organization.

name: Owen Black Students Association
description:
OBSA's purpose is to meet the distinct needs of the Black students at OGSM. We seek to create an environment that values diversity and fosters the advancement of students of African, West Indian, and Caribbean descent. OBSA, in its mission, seeks to:

  1. Provide vehicles for the attainment of success for black students during and after the Owen experience,
  2. create and sustain awareness within the Owen community of unique Black contributions, and to
  3. provide leadership and assistance to the Black community.
summary: OBSA strives to promote diversity and inclusion, while addressing the unique needs of black students at Owen.

name: Peabody Coalition of Black Graduates
description: The Peabody Coalition of Black Graduates is an organization geared toward Black students that seek social, academic, and professional support. PCBG also plays an integral role in the recruitment and retention of Black, Indigenous, and students of color on Peabody's campus. The organization holds several educational and social events throughout the year that are open to the greater Vanderbilt campus.
summary: The Peabody Coalition of Black Graduates is an organization geared toward Black students that seeks to provide social support to its members. PCBG also plays an integral in the recruitment and retention of Black students on Peabody's campus.

name: Serving, Helping, Affirming the Divinity in Every Sista'
description: S.H.A.D.E.S. is a student organization at Vanderbilt Divinity School open to those who desire to take seriously the struggles, challenges, and triumphs of Black women in theory and praxis. Influenced by the very last line in Ntozake Shange’s For Colored Girls Who Have Considered Suicide When the Rainbow Is Enuf, “ I found God in myself and I loved her…I loved her fiercely,” S.H.A.D.E.S. stands for Serving, Helping, Affirming the Divinity in Every Sista’. Black women have remarkable virtue; likewise, the term S.H.A.D.E.S affirms our united strength and celebrates our unique and varied complexions, personalities, identities, and experiences.
summary: S.H.A.D.E.S. aims to create covenant community and space for Black women’s experiences, stories, and issues to be shared and discussed. We will utilize womanism as a means to break the yoke of silence for Black women and confront tripartite oppression.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
description:
1. To unite Hispanic and Non-Hispanic engineering students at Vanderbilt and connect them with the Vanderbilt Engineering administration, other university organizations, and the Nashville community.

2. To promote Hispanic advancement in engineering, science and math fields by providing educational and tutorial programs in these fields.

3. To implement programs for the awareness of higher education, career advancements, and community development.

4. To increase the recruitment, registration, and the establishment of scholarships for Hispanic engineering students at Vanderbilt and other Universities.

 

Join now! Visit the SHPE registration site to become a registered member of SHPE to gain access to a huge community of minority professionals, academics, and to networking and career opportunities.

Visit the national organization's website at: http://www.shpe.org/
summary: To implement programs for the advancement of Hispanic engineers in the University and in society.

name: South Asian Law Students Association
description: The mission of SALSA is to provide a venue for law students of South Asian descent and all others that are interested, to increase awareness of South Asian issues, and to develop cultural competencies.  We also hope to educate others within the law school community about our heritage and to learn more about the impact of South Asians within the legal community.
summary: The South Asian Law Students Association provides networking opportunities, scholarly programming, and social events for South Asian law students. To find out more or to join SALSA, contact any officer or the Office of Student Affairs.

name: Turkish Student Association
description: The Turkish Student Association (TUSA) at Vanderbilt University is a dynamic and inclusive club dedicated to celebrating and sharing the rich cultural heritage of Turkey. Our mission is to promote a deeper understanding of Turkish traditions while providing a supportive network for Turkish and Turkish-American students. We achieve this through a diverse range of activities, including cultural festivals, food tastings, film screenings, and educational workshops. By hosting these events, we aim to showcase the beauty of Turkish culture and foster connections among students. In addition, to support and assist Turkish freshmen to adapt to college life and feel welcomed at their new home. Whether you're of Turkish descent or simply interested in learning more about Turkey, the TUSA invites you to join our community and contribute to enriching Vanderbilt with the warmth and vibrancy of Turkish heritage.
summary: An association for Turkish students and scholars at Vanderbilt; while everybody who is interested in the Turkish culture is welcome.

name: Vanderbilt Undergraduate Chinese Association
description: Founded in 2007, the mission of VUCA is to promote Chinese culture and facilitate Chinese language learning across the Vanderbilt community. VUCA welcomes everyone who is interested in Chinese language or culture. We host events to welcome students to Chinese culture and to engage them through meaningful activities. Our events are a great opportunity for participants to enjoy not only the culture, but also each other's company. Participants at our events learn something new about the country, the language, and the culture each time they come.
summary: VUCA seeks to broaden the understanding and appreciation of Chinese culture and current events as well as facilitate Chinese language learning through educational and recreational events.

name: Association of Latin American Students
description:
Our mission is to establish a sense of community at Vanderbilt through intentional programming that celebrates Latinx and Hispanic culture, educates through meaningful discussions, and serves and advocates for Latinx communities.

Our newsletter goes out every Monday. If you would like something included, please send a blurb and optional graphic to our Secretary by Sunday at noon.
summary: Association of Latin American Students is an organization at Vanderbilt University responsible for celebrating and promoting Latinx culture for all students. It is an actively involved organization on campus and works to make a lasting difference.

name: Office of LGBTQIA+ Concerns, Vanderbilt Divinity School
description: To create a "safe space" for the facilitation of conversations to include a diversity of sexualities and gender expressions. To support the LGBTQIA+ community and their allies in the Vanderbilt Divinity School and the Graduate Department of Religion. To provide opportunities for discussion, advocacy, education, and community-building regarding issues that concern the greater LGBTQIA+ community. To promote an open and affirming environment in classroom settings, academic gatherings, curriculum, and all other VDS related activities. To advance ministries that affirm the gifts and contributions of the LGBTQIA+ community. To affirm the sexualities and gender expressions of individuals in our community in a manner that promotes fellowship and gaiety.
summary: GABLE is for students, alumni, faculty, staff, and other community members who are concerned about LGBTQIA+ issues, particularly in relation to faith communities.

name: Blair Student Council
description:
Our mission is to represent the students of the Blair School of Music to faculty, staff, and administration and to the greater Vanderbilt community. We exist to aid and empower all Blair students through the following measures:

  • Collaborate with administration to address our students’ concerns with creative, effective solutions,
  • Foster a strong sense of community in the Blair School of Music, Vanderbilt, and the greater Nashville community,
  • Advocate for all Blair Students by ensuring access to campus resources, quality of life related needs, and other resources,
  • And enrich the experience of the student body we represent by strengthening efforts of diversity, equity, and inclusion.
summary: Blair Council is the student organization in charge of planning and running Blair student events throughout the year.

name: Engineering Council
description:
Goal: To ensure Vanderbilt University School of Engineering (VUSE) students are receiving the best education possible by:

  1. Serving as advocates of the VUSE and voicing the concerns of students to administrators, faculty, and staff
  2. Serving as intermediaries between students and faculty/administrators, promoting effective communication throughout the VUSE organization
  3. Planning and promoting events for the VUSE, encouraging  student involvement in activities of the Engineering School, Engineering Council, and other related organizations
  4. Encouraging effective student organizations including professional societies, and coordinating their activities with those of the Engineering Council for the benefit of the VUSE community.
summary: The Engineering Council serves as the governing body of the School of Engineering.

name: Graduate Student Council
description:
We coordinate academic, social, and other activities significant to the graduate community. We strive to promote community amongst graduate students, as well as graduate student integration into the university community.

Click here to nominate yourself or a colleague to the executive board.

Click here to nominate yourself or a colleague for a Graduate Leadership Anchor Award.

Click here to volunteer.

Click here to apply for a co-sponsorship.

Click here to apply for a travel grant.
summary: We coordinate academic, social, and other activities significant to the graduate community. We strive to promote community amongst graduate students, as well as graduate student integration into the university community.

name: Interfraternity Council
description: The Interfraternity Council (IFC) at Vanderbilt takes charge of and preserves the high standards of its 9 member fraternities on this campus. The Interfraternity Council promotes the interests of fraternities in general, and insures cooperation among the different fraternities on campus. The purpose of this organization shall be to develop and maintain fraternity life and interfraternity relationships, and in doing so to: promote excellence in scholarship; act in conjunction with member fraternities and the University to promote and maintain high social and moral standards; provide programs and services to member fraternities; act as a liaison between member fraternities and other student organizations, the student body, the faculty, and the administration of Vanderbilt University; foster the development of a positive relationship between the community and member fraternities; institute policies and rules that will further the mission of Greek Life at Vanderbilt University; work collaboratively with the Intercultural Greek, National Pan-Hellenic and Panhellenic Councils on programs, activities, and events.
summary: The Interfraternity Council promotes the interests of fraternities in general, and insures cooperation among the different fraternities on campus.

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council
description:
The National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC) is a collaborative organization of nine historically African American, international Greek lettered fraternities and sororities. The nine NPHC organizations are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Divine Nine". 

The council promotes interaction through forums, meetings and other mediums for the exchange of information and engages in cooperative programming and initiatives through various activities and functions.

Each constituent member organization determines its own strategic direction and program agenda. Today, the primary purpose and focus of member organizations remains camaraderie and academic excellence for its members and service to the communities they serve. Each promotes community awareness and action through educational, economic, and cultural service activities.
summary: The National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC) is a collaborative organization of nine historically African American, international Greek lettered fraternities and sororities.

name: Panhellenic Council
description:
The Panhellenic Council at Vanderbilt takes charge of and preserves the high standards of its member sororities on this campus. Panhellenic consist of nine chapters who are members of the National Panhellenic Conference (NPC).The Panhellenic Council at Vanderbilt develops and maintains fraternity/sorority life and interfraternity relations at a high level of accomplishment, and in doing so:

  • Considers the goals and ideals of member groups as continually applicable to campus and personal life;
  • Promotes superior scholarship as basic to intellectual achievement;
  • Cooperates with member fraternities and the university administration in concern for and maintenance of high social and moral standards;
  • Acts in accordance with National Panhellenic Conference Unanimous Agreements and policies;
  • Acts in accordance with such rules established by Panhellenic Association as to not violate the sovereignty, rights and privileges of member fraternities;
  • Oversees and maintain all actions of member sororities of the University, adhering to all rules and regulations set forth by the National Panhellenic Conference, this University Panhellenic Council, as well as those determined by the University itself;
  • Contributes to the excellence of the University and the outside community through service and academic achievement;
  • Enhances and maintain standards of individual members of this University Panhellenic Association through inter-sorority and intercommunity relations and high emphasis on academic achievement, all in cooperation with University administration and faculty.
summary: The Panhellenic Council at Vanderbilt takes charge of and preserves the high standards of its member sororities on this campus. The Panhellenic Council at Vanderbilt develops and maintains fraternity/sorority life and interfraternity relations.

name: Anthropology Graduate Student Association (VAGSA)
description: The Vanderbilt Anthropology Graduate Student Association (VAGSA) was formed to represent the interests of graduate students at Vanderbilt and to promote our educational growth. We hold periodic meetings and social events and also sponsor events for Vanderbilt professors and graduate students to present their research.
summary: Organization for Graduate Students in the Department of Anthropology

name: English Graduate Students Association
summary: A professionalization and service organization for the graduate students in the English department.

name: German Graduate Student Association
description: The German Graduate Student Association (GGSA) has been in existence since Fall 2010. All graduate students in Department of Germanic and Slavic Languages are members. The GGSA seeks to  enrich the graduate student experience within the Vanderbilt German Department, as well as to promote German Studies at all levels in our campus community and beyond. In addition to coordinating activities for our members, we organize a weekly Kaffeestunde for undergraduate language learners and everyone who is interested in learning German. We also part of other outreach events (such as the Halloween party and an ice cream social that happen every year).
summary: The GGSA seeks to enrich the graduate student experience within the Vanderbilt German Department, as well as to promote German Studies at all levels in our campus community and beyond.

name: Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Association
description: The mission of the Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Association (MEGA) is to promote social and professional interaction among students within the mechanical engineering department while also serving to facilitate feedback between students and faculty.
summary: The mission of the Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Association is to promote social and professional interaction among students within the mechanical engineering department while also serving to facilitate feedback between students and faculty.

name: Global Health Alliance
description:
Our mission is to foster interdisciplinary awareness and understanding of global health within the Vanderbilt community by:

1. Serving as a medium of communication between the Vanderbilt student body and Vanderbilt Institute for Global Health

2. Promoting service, research, and educational opportunities

3. Actively facilitating collaboration and action

Click here to learn more about us.
summary: The Global Health Alliance (formerly VIGH Student Advisory Council) brings together undergraduate and graduate students from all departments interested in global health, with the goal of fostering cross-disciplinary student networking and collaboration.

name: Phi Delta Phi
description:
Phi Delta Phi provides students with an opportunity to meet and interact with members of the law school community through various social events. In addition, the international legal honor society makes available numerous services and benefits provided by the national organization. Members of the local Inn of Phi Delta Phi may become more involved in the society by participating in the national convention and other national programs.

PDP also engages in a variety of service and academic events. The organization sponsors guest speakers who present on topics such as ethics in the practice of law. Through its events, PDP attempts to live up to the values of the organization and promote the ethical practice of law.
summary:
Phi Delta Phi is an international legal honors fraternity that is among the oldest legal organizations in North America.

Mission: Phi Delta Phi  promotes the highest standard of ethics and professionalism in law schools and the legal profession.

name: Psi Chi International Psychology Honors Society
description:
(From the National Website) Psi Chi is the International Honor Society in Psychology, founded in 1929 for the purposes of encouraging, stimulating, and maintaining excellence in scholarship, and advancing the science of psychology. Psi Chi is dedicated to promoting excellence among high-achieving psychology students and connecting them with one another. Benefits of membership include:

  • Networking and career opportunities
  • Research opportunities
  • Internationally recognized organization to list on resume
  • Connections with other Vanderbilt Psychology students
  • Opportunities for scholarships, awards, and grants

Vanderbilt’s chapter will be having monthly/bimonthly virtual events including but not limited to:

  • Psychology movie nights and podcast discussions
  • Guest speakers
  • Service events
  • Psi Chi resource and info sessions

We hold fall and spring recruitment events. Membership is open to current juniors and seniors at Vanderbilt University who are majoring or minoring in Psychology, Child Development, or Cognitive Studies. In order to qualify, you must:

  • Have taken at least 9 hours of psychology courses
  • Have a GPA that falls in the top 35% of your class
  • Maintain at least a 3.0 GPA overall and in psychology courses
  • Have successfully completed 3 semesters of full-time university coursework
summary: Psi Chi is the International Honor Society for Psychology majors. Membership is open to second-semester sophomores and up studying Psychology in both Peabody and the College of Arts and Sciences.

name: Tau Beta Sigma
description:
Tau Beta Sigma is a co-educational national honorary band sorority dedicated to serving college and band programs. The sorority operates primarily as a student service and leadership recognition society whose chief aim is to assist the Director of Bands in developing the leadership and enthusiasm that they require of their band. Our goals are not only to provide the band with organized and concentrated service activities, but to give our membership valid and wholesome experiences in organization, leadership, and social contacts.

The Eta Phi chapter is affiliated with the Vanderbilt Spirit of Gold Marching Band.
summary: Tau Beta Sigma is a national honorary band service organization. The Eta Phi chapter is affiliated with the Vanderbilt Spirit of Gold Marching Band.

name: American Constitution Society
description: ACS is a nationwide progressive legal organization that promotes the U.S. Constitutional values of individual rights and liberties, genuine equality, access to justice, democracy and the rule of law. This chapter allows students to connect with other progressive legal minds in the student body, Nashville, and nationwide. https://youtu.be/eH24CPGo_I0
summary: ACS is a nationwide progressive legal organization that promotes the U.S. Constitution's values of individual rights and liberties, genuine equality, access to justice, democracy and the rule of law.

name: The Owen Cork & Barrel Society
description: The Owen Cork & Barrel Society is a group of Owen students that appreciate thirst, most prevalently through education about wine, beer and spirits. Through educational tastings, seminars and “spirited” events, Cork & Barrel offers business school students the opportunity to regale in the wine, beer and spirits’ industry firsthand, learning from experts, industry titans, and local connoisseurs.
summary: Through tastings, seminars and “spirited” events, The Owen Cork & Barrel Society offers business school students the opportunity to learn about and appreciate libations with classmates firsthand.

name: Criminal Law Association at Vanderbilt Law School
description:
The Criminal Law Association (CLA) is dedicated to the principles of criminal justice.  Its purpose is to afford law students knowledge and insight into law enforcement, corrections, prosecution, and defense while providing opportunities for students interested in the criminal justice system.

Our hope is to allow Vanderbilt Law students the opportunity to become involved with an organization that unites students with a common interest consistent with the Law School's collegiate atmosphere. CLA seeks to explore the criminal justice system on a state, federal, and international level.

Along with sponsoring speakers, debates, and forums, CLA affords students opportunities to become involved in the community.  Whether through the RISE (Riverbend Insider Support and Education) program, student job panels, or community based programs in conjecture with other clubs, CLA seeks to allow Law Students to receive a meaningful experience.
summary: The Criminal Law Association (CLA) seeks to promote both the study and the practice of criminal law. CLA sponsors lectures, forums, and debates on issues of current concern. Speakers include faculty members at the Law School, local and national attorneys

name: Entertainment and Sports Law Society
description: The Mission of the Entertainment and Sports Law Society is to educate Vanderbilt law students about the current events and problems facing the sports and entertainment industries today and to facilitate lasting connections between Vanderbilt law students and professionals in those fields.
summary: The purpose of the Entertainment and Sports Law Society is to educate and inform students about the entertainment and sports law industries.

name: Federalist Society at Vanderbilt Law School
description: The Federalist Society promotes the intellectual exchange of views concerning current legal and public policy interests. The Society is open to all students and is founded on the principles that the state exists to preserve freedom, that the separation of governmental powers is central to our constitution, and that it is emphatically the province and duty of the judiciary to say what the law is, not what it should be. The society seeks to promote an awareness of these principles and to further their application while maintaining an equal awareness of other points of view. Leading scholars from all legal, political, and social perspectives are regularly invited to speak on a variety of issues in both lectures and symposia.
summary: The Federalist Society promotes the intellectual exchange of views concerning current legal and public policy interests, focusing on conservative and libertarian political philosophy, particularly as applied to the judiciary.

name: Health Law Society
description:
The Health Law Society (HLS) is a student organization in the Vanderbilt Law School that focuses on current issues in healthcare law, health policy, and the healthcare industry.

Nashville is one of the nation’s premier healthcare hubs and is home to more than 300 healthcare companies that generate over $70 billion of revenue and employ more than 400,000 people. Accordingly, the Health Law Society helps law students learn about the many growing opportunities in health law and meet attorneys currently practicing in this diverse field.

 

Past speakers have included:

- William V. "Bill" Corr (VLS '73; Deputy Secretary of the US Department of Health and Human Services)

- Douglas I. Friedman (VLS '75; Founder and Shareholder, Friedman Law Firm; nationally-known speaker and author on disability issues relevant for insurers and disease-specific advocacy groups)

- Chasity Goodner (Senior Litigation Counsel for HCA)

- Jon R. "Jay" Harris (VLS '84; Partner, King & Spalding)

- Andrea Huddleston (Deputy General Counsel for Tennessee Department of Health)

- Daniel R. Kuninsky (VLS '10; Attorney at Bass, Berry & Sims; member of AHLA Advisory Council on Diversity)

- J. Eric Miles (Partner, North, Pursell & Ramos)

- Marc Overlock (VLS '87; General Counsel for Metropolitan Nashville Hospital Authority)
summary: The Health Law Society (HLS) is a student organization in the Vanderbilt Law School that focuses on current issues in health law, health policy, and the healthcare industry.

name: Law and Business Society of Vanderbilt Law School
summary: Mission Statement:  Vanderbilt Law and Business Society is committed to exposing students to scholarship and professionals at the intersection of business and the law, aiming to supplement the litigation-focused first-year law school curriculum.

name: Law Students for Social Justice
description:
Law Students for Social Justice (LSSJ) supports the mission of the Social Justice Program at Vanderbilt Law School through student-driven programming, projects, and events related to exploring the role of law in creating, perpetuating, and eradicating hierarchies of power and privilege in our society. Some students involved with LSSJ are thinking of careers in public interest, but most LSSJ students come together from a variety of fields. Everyone is welcome to attend any of our events!   

  • Panels & Speakers: LSSJ brings in speakers on social justice issues, often partnering with other student organizations. Past speakers have included Constance Slaughter-Harvey, a civil rights activist and the first African-American woman to receive a law degree from the University of Mississippi Law School and Elliot Ozment, a local immigration lawyer in Nashville.
  • Community Involvement: Opportunities to get involved in the local community with volunteer and pro-bono projects.
  • 1L Representatives: For those looking to get more involved in the organization’s executive board, we have 1L Rep positions, elections held in early Fall.
summary: Supports the mission of the Social Justice Program at Vanderbilt Law School through student-driven programming and events related to exploring the role of law in creating, perpetuating, and eradicating hierarchies of power and privilege in our society.

name: Law Students for Veterans Affairs
description:
Mission Statement

LSVA aims to promote awareness of the complex legal issues surrounding veterans and their families to provide a framework for advocacy and support. We will actively engage our law school peers, the Vanderbilt community, and the Middle Tennessee area through legal advocacy, service projects, topical awareness events, and fun team-building activities. LSVA also provides a supportive community for active and transitioning service members, both inside and out of the university to help strengthen civil-military relations in our community.
summary: LSVA promotes awareness of the complex legal issues surrounding veterans and their families by empowering law students to learn about these topics and provide advocacy and support for the larger veteran community in Middle Tennessee.

name: Vanderbilt/Fisk Universities Materials Research Society
description: The Vanderbilt University Chapter of the Materials Research Society (part of the National Materials Research Society) is for students interested in materials science or researching materials. Fostering communication among fellow students and our community through social events, intellectual discussions, outreach opportunities (work in progress due to POM constraints) are some of our goals.
summary: The Vanderbilt/Fisk Universities Chapter of the Materials Research Society (part of the national Materials Research Society) is for students interested in materials science or researching materials.

name: outLaw
description:
The purpose of outLaw shall be to utilize the collective resources of its members, including lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, questioning, intersex, asexual and ally (“LGBTQQIAA”) law students, and the community in order to:

  1. Foster and encourage academic success and professional competence of LGBTQQIAA law students;
  2. Develop the role of the LGBTQQIAA attorney within the greater legal structure; and
  3. Influence meaningful change by promoting a more informed awareness of the needs of the LGBTQQIAA community.

Membership is open to all Vanderbilt Law School students.
summary: The purpose of outLaw is to utilize the collective resources of its members, including LGBTQQIAA law students, and the community in order to foster and encourage their academic success and professional competence.

name: Intellectual Property Association
description: The Vanderbilt Intellectual Property Association is the central student organization for Law students interested in Intellectual Property. VIPA seeks to provide opportunities for students to learn about the breadth of careers in the field, whether in Patent Law, Trademarks, Copyright, or Transactional IP. We aim to provide these opportunities in the form of speaker events, workshops, and informational sessions. VIPA is a resource to any student, whether they are fully committed to pursing a career in IP or just interested in learning more.
summary: The Vanderbilt Intellectual Property Association supports the intellectual property community at the Vanderbilt University Law School. It organizes educational events, networking opportunities and social gatherings for students interested in IP.

name: Ambassadors at Vanderbilt Law School
description: A Law School Organization dedicated to the recruitment of future students, the creation of the Obiter Dictum, and the facilitation of long-lasting relationships with VLS Alumni.
summary: A Law School Organization dedicated to the recruitment of future students, the creation of the Obiter Dictum, and the facilitation of long-lasting relationships with VLS Alumni.

name: Transfer Student Association at Vanderbilt Law School
description: The Transfer Student Association provides transition support to incoming transfers and community for 2L and 3L transfers.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to represent the interests of transfer law students and facilitate their smooth transition into Vanderbilt Law School.

name: Legal Aid Society at Vanderbilt Law School
description:
Vanderbilt's Legal Aid Society facilitates opportunities for law students to give back to traditionally underrepresented groups through community service, pro bono, and special projects. The Legal Aid Society strives to promote a culture where service and public interest are recognized as vital parts of a legal education and an important component of a legal career.

One way this mission is supported is through administering the Public Interest Stipend Fund. These stipends provide funding for students who dedicate their summers to otherwise unpaid public interest work. Students are recognized for their commitment to volunteer work and public interest through the Pro Bono Pledge. The opportunities organized by the Legal Aid Society not only allow students to serve the Nashville community but also create a platform for students to expand their legal networks by making connections in the public interest space.

The Legal Aid Society also facilitates a number of pro bono and community service projects, including the Medical Legal Partnership and Street Law. More information about these opportunities will be published closer to the beginning of the fall semester.

 

If you have questions about the Legal Aid Society, feel free to email our Co-Executive Directors, Annakate Blanks and Anna Smith (annakate.c.blanks@vanderbilt.edu & anna.a.smith@vanderbilt.edu).
summary: The Legal Aid Society facilitates opportunities for law students to give back to underrepresented groups while promoting a culture of service among the law school student body.

name: Vanderbilt Mock Trial Organization
description:
Vanderbilt Mock Trial gives students an opportunity to learn about the legal process and compete in mock trial tournaments around the country. Students fill the role of attorneys or witnesses as the team competes against other schools.

The program includes 3 competitive teams. In the past, Vanderbilt has been enormously successful on the national stage. In the 2021-2022 season, the program placed at numerous invitationals, the Regional competition, and sent a team to the National Championship Series. In the 2022-2023 season, the program won the St. Louis Regional Tournament and took Honorable Mention at the National Championship Series.
summary: Vanderbilt Mock Trial gives students an opportunity to learn about the legal process and compete in mock trial tournaments around the country. Students fill the roles of attorneys and witnesses as the team competes against other schools.

name: Moot Court Board at Vanderbilt Law School
description: The Vanderbilt Moot Court Board promotes the practice of effective appellate advocacy by leading all appellate advocacy activities at Vanderbilt Law School. Each year, the Board produces a unique Constitutional Law moot court problem and runs the Bass Berry & Sims Intramural Moot Court Competition, which serves as the selection process for new board members. The Board also holds tryouts for students to compete in traveling teams that participate in various moot court competitions around the country. Finally, the Board assists the law school's Legal Writing Department in organizing and implementing the first-year oral arguments program, a required component of the Legal Writing curriculum.
summary: The Moot Court Board promotes the practice of effective appellate advocacy by leading all appellate advocacy activities at Vanderbilt Law School, including the Intramural Moot Court competition, traveling competitions, and the 1L oral argument program.

name: Women Law Students Association
summary: WLSA was organized to promote gender equality in the law, in legal education, and in the legal profession. The organization sponsors speakers, panels, and discussion groups, and encourages all members to get involved in community outreach.

name: Student National Medical Association
description:
The Student National Medical Association (SNMA) was founded in 1964 on the campuses of Meharry Medical College (Nashville, TN) and Howard University College of Medicine (Washington D.C.). It is the nation’s oldest and largest organization focused on the needs and concerns of medical students of color. Currently, the organization has over 190 chapters at allopathic and osteopathic medical schools across the nation.

SNMA is committed to supporting current and future underrepresented minority medical students, addressing the needs of underserved communities, and increasing the number of clinically excellent, culturally competent, and socially conscious physicians.

Helpful Resources

Event Calendar

Community Service Opportunities
summary: Vanderbilt SNMA is committed to building a community and nurturing environment for current and future underrepresented minority students at VUSM, fostering connections between VUSM and the Nashville community, and addressing issues of health equity.

name: Headnotes: A Cappella
description:
We are Vanderbilt Law School's A Cappella Group! In order to escape the stresses of law school life, we are a group of people who get together every week to sing! Students, faculty, and advisors/staff alike come together over our love of music and we encourage all who are interested to get involved!

Mission Statement:  Our goal is to provide a creative outlet for law students with similar interests in music and performing, while building relationships through networking opportunities.

Facebook Page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/headnotes/
summary: Vanderbilt Law School's A Cappella Group

name: Momentum Dance Company
description: Momentum Dance Company is Vanderbilt’s first student-run dance performance group and includes members coming from a variety of artistic backgrounds, ranging from classical ballet studios to competition troupes. Momentum produces two full-length shows each year containing contemporary, lyrical, ballet, pointe, jazz, and hip-hop pieces choreographed, directed, and performed by undergraduate company members. The company also engages in a variety of community outreach projects and performs at various campus and philanthropic events. Momentum works to make dance more accessible to the general public in an effort to provide a challenging and exciting experience for its dancers as well as a fulfilling and high quality performance for its audiences. The company fosters a sense of genuine emotionality, innovative storytelling, and raw passion for movement, striving to further enrich Vanderbilt and Nashville with meaningful art.
summary: Momentum Dance Company is Vanderbilt's first student-run dance performance group and produces two full-length shows each year containing pieces choreographed, directed, and performed by undergraduate company members.

name: Swingin' Dores
description: Swingin' Dores is Vanderbilt University's premiere all-female a cappella group, consisting of 10-15 talented young women who audition for their spot in our ensemble. We create powerful arrangements from an eclectic mix of genres - from pop and hip-hop, to country and oldies - which we perform at events on campus and around Nashville. Our mission is to represent Vanderbilt’s fierce spirit through dynamic vocals, captivating arrangements, and tight harmonies, all while having loads of fun along the way.
summary: Swingin' Dores is Vanderbilt University's premiere all-female a cappella group, consisting of 10-15 talented young women who audition for their spot in our ensemble. We create and perform powerful arrangements at events on campus and around Nashville.

name: The Original Cast
description:
The Original Cast, one of Vanderbilt's oldest performing arts organizations, produces two original shows each year by pulling songs from a wide variety of musicals, making it the only musical theatre revue troupe on campus. Since 1975, this entirely-student run group has maintained distinctly smaller cast sizes in contrast to other organizations in order to ensure that all performers have a featured role in each show and to guarantee close-knit casts.

The Original Cast holds auditions each semester for those interested in joining. Additionally, the group hosts social events throughout the school year for past/current performers and alumni. If you want to sing, dance, and act with some of Vanderbilt’s most talented performers, you could find a new home in The Original Cast!
summary: The Original Cast is Vanderbilt's Musical Theatre Revue Troupe.

name: The Slant
description:
According to recent scientific studies, The Slant wins at everything. We are the #1 humor publication at Vanderbilt University (and the world) and a three-time winner of the Nobel Prize (Literature, 2002; Literature 2005; Physics, 2008). Started in 1886, two years before our arch-nemesis The Hustler, The Slant has satirized everything that needs to be satirized.

If you are a Vanderbilt student or and you’re interested in joining this Justice League of Humor, then feel free to give us a holla, shoutout or whatnot.

We are currently looking for writers, artists, funny people, kind of funny people and mischief makers. There is no need to have any experience, either on a newspaper staff or as a comedy writer. In fact, experience is discouraged; many Slant staffers are even illiterate!

The Slant staff meets once per week to brainstorm and generate story ideas. One thing's for sure—none of your achievements in college make Mom prouder than getting published in a paper that can swear whenever the hell it wants to. So send us an email to learn more about joining, and double the value of your education!
summary: The Slant is Vanderbilt's premier humor and satire publication since 1886.

name: The Vanderbilt Melodores
description: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OT-ZAZ1or4s&pp=ygUJbWVsb2RvcmVz
summary: The Vanderbilt Melodores exist to provide for its members a community of musicality and brotherly fellowship; to provide for the Vanderbilt community rousing musical performances; and to afford to the greater community service and enjoyment.

name: Tongue 'N' Cheek
description: We are Vanderbilt's Improv Comedy Troupe! We put on monthly shows for the Vanderbilt community which feature short form improv and long form improv.
summary: We are Vanderbilt's Improv Comedy Troupe! We put on monthly shows for the Vanderbilt community which feature short form improv and long form improv.

name: Vanderbilt Off-Broadway
description: Vanderbilt Off-Broadway (VOB) is Vanderbilt University’s only student-run theatre company that produces full-length musicals. Since our founding, VOB has produced over 30 different musical productions. Our casts, crews, orchestras, and boards consist solely of students — we run the show, literally. Auditions and crew/orchestra sign-ups are held at the beginning of each semester, and elections for our Executive Board occur every January-February. Whether you’re a veteran of the stage or a newcomer to musical theatre, anyone is welcome to become a member of VOB. Interested in joining the VOB family? Come out and see how we’ve entertained the ‘Dores since 1998!
summary: Vanderbilt Off-Broadway (VOB) is Vanderbilt University's home for student-produced, full-length musicals. We've been entertaining the 'Dores since 1998. #VOBIsSoFun

name: Vanderbilt Performing Arts Community
description: The Vanderbilt Performing Arts Community (VPAC) is the umbrella organization that oversees all performing arts groups on Vanderbilt's campus. VPAC hosts Spotlight (fall) and VPACFest (spring), two showcases that feature all performing arts groups and have audiences up to 1,000. We also plan smaller-scale, community events that allow the Vanderbilt student body to stay immersed with the arts scene on campus.
summary: VPAC aims to bring together members of the performing arts community at Vanderbilt in order to support all groups’ events, foster collaboration, and have a unified voice in advocating for the performing arts.

name: Vanderbilt Spoken Word
description: Vanderbilt Spoken Word (VSW) is a performance poetry group committed to cultivating a diversity of thoughts, ideas, and perspectives through the spoken word art form. VSW endeavors to promote self expression, challenge our prejudices, and create an environment where diverse cultures can thrive and learn from one another. We believe that spoken word can bring people together in the ethic of service and community, and we strive to realize that vision in our poems and in our actions.
summary: Vanderbilt Spoken Word (VSW) is a performance poetry group committed to cultivating a diversity of thoughts, ideas, and perspectives through the spoken word art form.

name: Vanderbilt Tango Club
description:
Mondays in March & April: Keep building those tango basics by attending the Tango Technique classes every Monday 7:00-8:00pm at Vanderbilt Tango Club at Rand Dining Center Dance Floor! Stay for practica after class, 8:00-9:00pm. Free for students, $10 for public.   

Workshop with Jeremías Fors - Guest Master Tango Instructor
Date: March 2, 2026, 7-8 pm class, 8-9 pm práctica
Knowledge of tango basics is required for this class.
All our other classes on Mondays are open to all levels.

Spring Break Week: No classes

Free for students, $20 for community members. No partner needed!

Free Parking Directions:

Park at West End United Methodist Church or Pinnacle Financial.

To Rand Dining Center Dance Floor:
Walk down 23rd Ave N toward campus (south). Pass the circle and continue on the sidewalk past Alumni Field to Rand Hall/Sarratt Building. Keep walking straight. Go up the stairs and enter the last door on the right.

If it's after 7:00 PM and the door is locked, try the previous door or wait for a student to let you in.

Follow us on Instagram & Facebook: @VanderbiltTangoClub
summary: Celebrating Argentine tango on the Vanderbilt campus through classes, practicas, workshops, and social dancing. No partner is required!  We meet weekly on Monday nights to learn and dance tango together. Location: Rand Dance Floor.

name: Vanderbilt University Concert Choir
description:
Founded in 1895, VUCC is the oldest performing arts group, the only extracurricular choral group on campus, and the official keepers of the Vanderbilt alma mater!

Our choir is comprised of students from both undergraduate and graduate schools. We have two concerts a year and sing everything from Handel to Morten Lauridsen, Broadway show tunes, and even Lady Gaga! We also perform at various school and off campus functions throughout the year, such as Fall for the Arts during Family Weekend, athletic games, nursing homes, etc. Each spring break, we go on an optional, partly-paid-for tour to sing to high schoolers, church congregations, and alumni.

We are a student-led organization with lots of opportunity for leadership. Our director, C. Brian Warford, will be starting his fourth year with this choir (as of Fall 2025) but has many years of choral directing experiences. We rehearse twice a week, from 4:30-6:00 PM on Mondays and Wednesdays at our historic Music Rehearsal Hall. Our mission is to provide a space in which all music lovers feel welcomed and comfortable to sing and make friends. As our unofficial motto states, Concert Choristers "come for the music and stay for the people."
summary: The Concert Choir is the oldest performing group on campus.  Our membership consists of students of all majors who love to sing.  We perform 2-3 concerts per year and go on tour over spring break to sing and recruit. Come be a part of VUCC!

name: Vanderbilt University Theatre
description:
Vanderbilt University Theatre (VUTheatre) is a student organization affiliated with the Vanderbilt University Department of Theatre. Membership is open to any undergraduate or graduate student at Vanderbilt University!

VUTheatre's season consists of three to four main-stage productions per year that are directed and designed by our professional faculty or guest artists with student actors and crews, in addition to several studio productions that are entirely student-run. VUTheatre productions are staged in Neely Auditorium next to Alumni Lawn.

Membership in our student organization includes incredibly exciting social events, including a holiday party and an end of the year banquet!
summary: Vanderbilt University Theatre (VUTheatre) is a student organization affiliated with the Vanderbilt University Department of Theatre. Membership is open to any undergraduate or graduate student at Vanderbilt University!

name: Vanderbilt Variations
description: Founded in 1997, Variations is Vanderbilt's premier co-ed a cappella group! We usually have 12-15 undergraduate students and have a wide repertoire ranging from Lake Street Dive to Hozier to Paramore. We hold concerts at the end of each semester, in addition to performing at various campus events throughout the year. We audition new members at the beginning of each semester.
summary: Vanderbilt University's premier co-ed a cappella group! We are we're always looking for members who can add a little "variety"!

name: Spectrum A Cappella
description: Spectrum A Cappella is a competitive fusion a cappella team that promotes diversity of culture by blending pop music from around the globe. The team has won numerous awards in both the A3 and ICCA Circuits and has traveled across the nation to compete in Los Angeles, Pittsburgh, Atlanta, and Austin. The team also collaborates with other organizations to showcase its annual Concerts to perform and involve students across campus. Through potlucks, photoshoots, and annual retreats, Spectrum fosters some of the closest friendships and a very tight-knit community for members. Come out and audition to join the family!
summary: Vanderbilt's Award-Winning Co-Ed Competitive Fusion A Cappella Group

name: VIBE
description: VIBE is Vanderbilt's premier hip hop dance organization. The student-led organization is dedicated to promoting hip hop's deeply-rooted culture on Vanderbilt's campus and throughout greater Nashville. Members of VIBE pride themselves upon the ability to create diverse and unique styles of choreography in order to tell a story through the art of dance.
summary: VIBE is Vanderbilt's premiere hip hop dance organization!

name: VIDA
description: VIDA is one of Vanderbilt’s Latin dance groups committed to choreographing, performing, and teaching various styles of Latin dance. Through guest performances, our own showcase, and collaboration with the local Nashville social dancing scene, VIDA looks to foster a positive and enthusiastic environment. VIDA prioritizes accessibility for our members, education, and fun above all else in our performances, choreography, and clinics so that anyone’s experience with Latin dance will be one to remember. Come dance with us and join our VIDA community! Insta: @vandy_vida
summary: VIDA is Vanderbilt’s dance group committed to choreographing, performing, and teaching various styles of Latin dance.

name: VOCE A Cappella
description: VOCE A Cappella is one of Vanderbilt’s soprano-alto a cappella groups. We pride ourselves in our diverse repertoire and our original arrangements – we sing everything from Ariana Grande to Fleetwood Mac to the Jackson 5, and our collaborative creative process allows everyone’s individual voice to be heard. At our most recent concert, we performed a set list of hits including "Back to Black," "Landslide," and a Taylor Swift medley. We update our setlist with a new collection of songs every semester which keeps our rehearsals and performances fresh and energetic. Voce is also a place where we have found friendship and support – we are a small, close-knit ensemble of women who have built strong connections with each other that go beyond just music and rehearsal time, and we deeply value this aspect of the group. We often appear as guest performers at campus events and we also host our own concert each semester to showcase all of our hard work. We highly encourage anyone passionate about music to audition for VOCE A Cappella!
summary: VOCE is an all-girls a cappella group at Vandy! We're a close-knit group that loves to sing and perform together. From ABBA and The Backstreet Boys to Ariana Grande and Clairo, we arrange our own music and create fun mashups and medleys.

name: Vanderbilt College Republicans
description: Vanderbilt College Republicans (VCR) is the bedrock for campus conservativism at Vanderbilt University with regular guest speakers, social events, debates, and more. VCR is a part of the statewide Tennessee College Republicans conference.
summary: Vanderbilt College Republicans (VCR) is the bedrock for campus conservativism at Vanderbilt University with regular guest speakers, social events, debates, and more.

name: ANCHORTHON
description: ANCHORTHON is the largest (and most fun) philanthropic student organization on campus. In our 24 years, we have raised over $3 million for Monroe Carell Jr. Children’s Hospital at Vanderbilt. Throughout the year, we form relationships with patient ambassadors, or Miracle Kids, and help them spread their stories and awareness about pediatric health. At the end of the year, we celebrate all our hard work at our Dance Marathon fundraiser, where we cater food from all around Nashville and dance all day long (although dancing is not necessary- we just want to have a good time)!!!!
summary: We are a student-run philanthropic organization, supporting pediatric patients and their families at Monroe Carell Jr. Children’s Hospital at Vanderbilt. Throughout the year, we spend time with our "miracle kids,” as well as fundraise for the hospital.

name: VPB CityVU
description:
CityVU is Vanderbilt's small but mighty all-student organization of true Nashville lovers! A subcommittee of the Vanderbilt Programming Board (VPB), we work hard all year long to plan, organize, and pay for exciting events held throughout the year, all tailored to be totally accessible for you and your friends to enjoy!

We believe that there are so many amazing opportunities to be had in and around our town, from local street food to the world-famous Country Music Awards. CityVU has a blast discovering all the best-kept secrets and the up-and-coming hot spots to share with the Vanderbilt community, as well as those timeless Nashville traditions we never forget to bring back, year after year.
summary: VPB's City VU seeks to expose the Vanderbilt community to Nashville's many wonders by offering events year-round, both on and off campus, all of which are open to everyone, and are entirely free of charge!

name: VPB Music Group
description: The purpose of the Music Group is to expose Vanderbilt students and also the Greater Nashville community to a diverse musical experience. The Music Group is dedicated to maintaining a balance between showcasing new talents and representing Vanderbilt students’ musical tastes. The Music Group is a student-run, democratic concert group which plans, publicizes and produces several concerts a year for Vanderbilt students and the greater Nashville community.  Concerts include Commodore Quake in the fall, and Rites of Spring in April, as well as additional events as the Music Group sees fit.
summary: The Music Group is a student concert committee that plans, publicizes and produces several concerts a year for students of Vanderbilt and the Nashville community. Our annual events are Commodore Quake and Rites of Spring.

name: VPB Speakers Committee
description:
The Vanderbilt University Speakers Committee has the responsibility of enhancing the quality of student life and the Vanderbilt educational experience by attracting and presenting distinguished speakers to the University. The programs of the Speakers Committee seek to encourage thoughtful reflection and dialogue on contemporary issues in order to promote and strengthen the intellectual environment at Vanderbilt. We achieve this by inviting speakers throughout the year who are entertaining, diverse, and accomplished in their fields. One of our most important events is the IMPACT Symposium, which has been a tradition at Vanderbilt since 1964. This is an annual multi-day symposium in which speakers are invited to discuss current events and topics of a controversial nature.

 

Learn more here.
summary: The Vanderbilt University Speakers Committee has the responsibility of enhancing the quality of student life and the Vanderbilt educational experience by attracting and presenting distinguished speakers to the University.

name: VPB Vandy Fanatics
description: Vandy Fanatics is the student organization dedicated to promoting school spirit by sponsoring events for the Vanderbilt community. Activities encourage attendance at athletic events, support student-athletes, and uphold Vanderbilt sports traditions.
summary: Vandy Fanatics is the student organization dedicated to promoting school spirit by sponsoring events for the Vanderbilt community. Activities encourage attendance at athletic events, support student-athletes, and uphold Vanderbilt sports traditions.

name: VPB's Late Night Programming Committee
description:
The mission of VPB's Late Night Programming Committee is to provide unique, interactive, and fun alternative programming events in a late-night and weekend setting. Programming will be offered to all students and the Vanderbilt community for free or at a reduced rate. Our goal is to serve as the vehicle to facilitate campus connections and creative programming that align with our mission.

Originally named VenUe, but renamed Late Night Programming in 2023, was created during the 2006-2007 school year as a merger between Sarratt Live and Films. In the past decade, Late Night Programming has grown dramatically and succeeded in gaining campus-wide support for events. Late Night Programming offers on average, 3-4 events each semester along with various cosponsorships with other Vanderbilt organizations. In 2023, VenUe was renamed as the Late Night Programming Committee. 

Please feel welcome to contact Co-Chairs Alberto Fernandez (alberto.fernandez@vanderbilt.edu) and Doris Jiang (doris.x.jiang@vanderbilt.edu) with any questions or comments you have!

We look forward to seeing you at our next event!
summary: VPB's Late Night Programming Committee (formerly VenUe) offers late-night programming for Vanderbilt students in unique and enjoyable ways. Our goal is to provide entertainment otherwise unavailable to students as a part of the Vanderbilt nightlife.

name: Every Nation Campus
description:
Every Nation Campus is a Christian organization that acts to spread the Gospel of Jesus Christ. We are a group that values devotion to Jesus, diversity within our community, and helping others be disciples of Jesus. We do this by engaging the campus with the Gospel, establishing people in community and in their faith, equipping members to share their faith and use their gifts within the body of Christ, and empowering members to make new disciples through evangelism.

Affiliated with the global ministry: Every Nation
summary: Every Nation Campus is a global movement that acts to impact every nation and every campus with the Gospel. We are a diverse community that exists to honor God and make disciples of every nation starting with students at Vanderbilt University.

name: Owen Jewish Business Association
description: The Jewish Business Association is a tool for networking, a method of educational and cultural outreach, a source of humor and a connection to the outstanding active Jewish community that resides in Nashville.
summary: The Jewish Business Association is a tool for networking, a method of educational and cultural outreach, a source of humor and a connection to the outstanding active Jewish community that resides in Nashville.

name: Jewish Law Student Association
description: The purposes of the Jewish Law Students Association (“JLSA”) are to serve as a sounding board and advocacy group for the Jewish students of Vanderbilt University Law School; to arrange events for Jewish holidays; to raise awareness of Jewish life and culture and help foster a diverse community at the Vanderbilt University Law School; and to serve as a liaison between students and the Vanderbilt University Law School student body, student organizations, staff, and faculty, other Jewish groups at Vanderbilt University and the Nashville Jewish community.
summary: The purposes of the Jewish Law Students Association (“JLSA”) are to serve as a sounding board and advocacy group for the Jewish students of Vanderbilt University Law School, and provide Jewish students with avenues to connect to their Jewish identity.

name: Latter Day Saint Student Association
description:
The Latter-day Saint Student Association (LDSSA) at Vanderbilt University is a student-led organization that brings together students who affirm faith in Jesus Christ and seek a balanced secular and spiritual college experience. LDSSA provides a supportive and inclusive community where Latter-day Saint students can practice their faith, build meaningful connections, and feel at home on a secular campus.

At the same time, LDSSA is committed to promoting interfaith understanding across Vanderbilt. Through open dialogue, shared activities, and mutual respect, the organization aims to build bridges between LDS and non-LDS students, fostering learning, inclusion, and genuine community for students of all backgrounds.
summary: LDSSA at Vanderbilt is a student-led community that supports LDS students in balancing faith and academics while fostering connection, inclusion, and interfaith understanding across campus.

name: Muslim Students Association
description: MSA is a religious and service organization representing the Muslim student body at Vanderbilt. Our goal is to create an environment where Muslims can interact with their fellow Muslims, while educating the Vanderbilt community about Muslims on campus.
summary: MSA is a religious and service organization representing the Muslim student body at Vanderbilt. Our goal is to create an environment where Muslims can interact with their fellow Muslims, while educating the Vanderbilt community about Muslims on campus.

name: Orthodox Christian Campus Ministries (OCCM)
description: Orthodox Christian Campus Ministries (OCCM) connects college students in faith, practice, community, and values with the ancient faith of the Orthodox church throughout their collegiate journey. OCCM aims to be an integral part of students’ spiritual formation by nurturing them spiritually, scholastically, socially, and even emotionally.
summary: Orthodox Christian Campus Ministries (OCCM) connects college students in faith, practice, community, and values with the ancient faith of the Orthodox church throughout their collegiate journey.

name: UKIRK Nashville
description: UKirk Nashville is a campus ministry supported by local Presbyterian Churches, but you don't have to be Presbyterian to join. We welcome all: Baptists, Catholics, Socialists, Libertarians, and party foulers. Come for the worship, the free food, or both! Regardless of belief, doubt, gender identity, sexuality, race, social class, disability, university affiliation or anything else, YOU are welcome to join us as we study scripture, ask hard questions, break bread, play, pray, laugh together, and love our neighbors.
summary: UKIRK Nashville exists to provide area college students a place for Christian community and spiritual growth by:  deepening relationships through fellowship, experiencing God through worship, and encouraging discipleship.

name: Reformed University Fellowship
description:
RUF is a student organization that seeks to spread the "gospel" (the good news of Jesus Christ) through ourselves and into the campus by word, deed, and lifestyle. We believe the gospel is what brings about personal change, social healing, and cultural renewal.

Everyone is welcome regardless of personal or religious background. RUF is here for the "convinced" and the "unconvinced," the lost, the found, the burned, bored, cynical, or spiritual. We invite you no matter where you are in the process.
summary: RUF is a student organization that seeks to spread the "gospel" (good news of Jesus) through ourselves and into the campus by word, deed, and lifestyle. We believe the gospel is what brings about personal change, social healing, and cultural renewal.

name: Vanderbilt Chabad Jewish Student Group
description: Vanderbilt Chabad is a Jewish home away from home for Vanderbilt students who wish to belong to a Jewish community, meet other students and learn about and celebrate Judaism. Vandy Chabad offers classes and discussions, weekly Friday night diners, Holiday celebrations and social events. Vandy Chabad is a warm, non judgemental place where everyone is welcome regardless of background and affiliation.
summary: Vanderbilt Chabad is a home away from home for students who wish to belong to a Jewish community, meet other students, and learn about and celebrate Judaism.

name: Vanderbilt Hillel
summary: The Vanderbilt Hillel prides itself in offering Jewish college students different ways to express their Jewishness such as through creative holiday programs and cultural events. Students are empowered to take responsibility for their Jewish identity.

name: Vandy Karma
description: Vandy Karma provides for the spiritual needs of Hindu students and works to increase understanding of Hinduism amongst the larger Vanderbilt community by organizing temple visits, scriptural study, screenings, ceremonies, and other spiritual events. Among these, Vandy Karma organizes a Diwali Dinner, Holi Event, and will now start working on a 9-day Navratri event. We interact with the Hindu community in Nashville through frequent trips to the Sri Ganesha Temple and collaborate with other Vanderbilt organizations on joint endeavors to create an environment of spiritual exchange.
summary: Vandy Karma provides for the spiritual needs of Hindu students and works to increase understanding of Hinduism among the larger Vanderbilt community by organizing temple visits, scriptural study, screenings, ceremonies, and other spiritual events.

name: Voices of Praise
description:
Don’t let anyone look down on you because you are young, but set an example for the believers in speech, in conduct, in love, in faith, and in purity.

1 Timothy 4:12

 

Vanderbilt's Voices of Praise is a gospel ministry that has historically been comprised of five separate ministries: Choir, Dance, Mime, Chosen Step, and Spoken Word. Voices of Praise was founded in 1992 when God gave three young women, Kimberly Parmer, LaTrice McBee, and Stephanie Grier, the amazing vision of this ministry, which has grown and continues to grow with the Lord's blessing.

We welcome you to fellowship, worship, and serve with us as we continue to grow in our relationship with Christ. For more information on joining this great ministry, please email the president daelyn.d.allen@vanderbilt.edu or visit our Instagram page @vandy_vop.
summary: Voices of Praise is a gospel ministry on campus dedicated to continuously build our relationships with God as well as share and grow together through gaining a better understanding of the Word through weekly Bible Study, worship, fellowship and service.

name: Canterbury CIRCLE (Christians of Interdenominational faith Realizing a Community of Love and Equality)
description: Canterbury CIRCLE is an interdenominational, Christ-centered community focused on love, inclusion, and equality.  We strive to foster the spiritual and social growth of all students, both undergraduate and graduate, through liturgy, worship, fellowship, and service opportunities.  Canterbury CIRCLE is supported by the Episcopal Diocese of Tennessee and St. Augustine’s Chapel.
summary: Canterbury CIRCLE at Vanderbilt is a Christian interdenominational student ministry open to ALL regardless of denominational background. Canterbury CIRCLE is here to serve you and, in turn, to help you serve others.

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description: Alpha Phi Omega is the nation’s largest service fraternity (co-educational). Our purpose is to make an impact on society by providing young people the opportunities to serve their local and national community. It is through the performance of service that we believe members of Alpha Phi Omega will learn our organization’s key principles: leadership, friendship, and service.
