name: Vanderbilt University Advanced Degree Consulting Club
description:
The VUADCC is the comprehensive resource for Vanderbilt APD candidates and postdoctoral fellows who wish to explore consulting as a career.

Our two-fold mission is as follows:

1. Provide comprehensive training and resources to our members in order to prepare them for a career in management consulting.

2. Promote awareness of consulting as a career option to APD candidates and postdocs at Vanderbilt.

We help our members break into the world of management consulting by providing numerous services as listed below:

1. Extensive Case and Fit interview prep resources

2. Information and Networking sessions with top consulting firms

3. Student consulting opportunities with business and non profit organizations

4. Educational resources and seminars to learn about management consulting and the principles involved

Please visit our website as well and don’t forget to sign up for our private group! We look forward to helping you launch your career in management consulting!
summary: .The Vanderbilt University Advanced Degree Consulting Club (VUADCC) is led by PhD, MD and JD candidates at Vanderbilt University. We serve the Vanderbilt APD candidates and postdoctoral fellows who wish to explore consulting as a career.

name: Humphrey Fellowship Program
description:
The Hubert H. Humphrey Fellowship Program was initiated in 1978 to honor the late senator and vice president and his life-long commitment to international cooperation and public service. The program brings accomplished mid-career leaders from developing nations and emerging democracies to the United States for an academic year to study, gain professional experience and foster cultural exchange.

Humphrey Fellows are selected based on their potential for leadership and their commitment to public service in either the public or the private sector. The Humphrey Fellowship Program's mission is to foster a mutual exchange of knowledge and understanding about issues of common concern in the United States and the Fellows' home countries while offering Fellows valuable opportunities for leadership development and professional engagement with Americans and their counterparts from many nations.

Program

  • About Our Program
  • Get Involved
  • Meet Our Fellows
Vanderbilt's Humphrey Fellows

During the 2012-2013 school year, Vanderbilt University will be hosting 10 educational leaders. The Fellows are leaders in the field of education in their home countries. Humphrey Fellows for 2012-2013 are from the following countries: Bahrain, Brazil, India, Liberia, Maldives, Myanmar (2), Niger, Nigeria, and Pakistan.

The Humphrey Fellowship Program at Vanderbilt has many components and required activities. Fellows will complete and participate a variety of professional development activities and projects during their Fellowship year. Some of the activities include

  • Designing an Individual Program Plan that outlines their goals and objective for their Humphrey year and well as a plan for impact once they return home.
  • Participating in the Humphrey Seminar Series on Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday mornings to enhance professional and leadership skills.
  • Auditing Academic Courses at Peabody.
  • Engaging in Professional Affiliations (similar to a high level internship).
  • Contributing to the Nashville community through Service.
  • Taking part in Cultural Exchange.

Funding & Support

The Humphrey Program is a Fulbright exchange activity. The U.S. Congress through the Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs (ECA) of the U.S. Department of State provides its primary funding. Co-sponsors include other governmental agencies, multinational organizations, and private donors. The Institute of International Education (IIE) assists the U.S. Department of State in administering the Humphrey Fellowship Program.


Community Service Partnership: Peace Dragon

Overview of the Peace Dragon Project:

The Peace Dragon Project is a not-for-profit program designed to synthesize personal stories, peace education, and art in order to inspire people to focus on peace and conflict resolution.

It opens the global doorway to participate in an inclusive environment of artistic expression with a focus on creating peaceful stories and works of art.

Description of our Partnership:
The Humphrey Program works in collaboration with The Peace Dragon Project to help design and construct a peace curriculum that utilizes an international collection of stories. The inaugural stories by the class of 2011 were complied by the 13 Vanderbilt University Humphrey Fellows from eleven different countries. They have worked to bring their unique personal stories to life, focusing on positive character traits, and creating personalized tools for each fellow to utilize in his/her home country. The tenets of Conflict Management course from Lipscomb University act as the experiential keys for the project as well. The goals of the curriculum are to do the following:

  1. Vanquish the Myth: No myth-taking, seek truth
  2. Right Voice: Speak, listen and ask
  3. Right Choice: Empower, pause and implement

Associate Campus Partnership: Volunteer State Community College:

Overview:
Volunteer State Community College is a public, two-year, college offering associate degrees, certificates, continuing education, and service to northern Middle Tennessee. It is located in Gallatin, Tennessee, 30 miles northeast of Nashville.

Volunteer State Community College is accredited by the Commission on Colleges of the Southern Association of Colleges and Schools to award the Associate Degree and holds membership in the American Association of Community and Junior College Association of Junior Colleges, and the Tennessee College Association.

Popularly known as Vol State, the college is home to more than 70 programs in five grand divisions:

Humanities, Social Science and Education, Allied Health, Business, and Math and Science. Vol State is committed to providing quality innovative educational programs that strengthen community and workforce partnerships, promote diversity and cultural exchange, inspire lifelong learning, and prepare students for successful careers, university transfer, and meaningful civic participation.

Description of our Partnership:
The Hubert Humphrey Fellowship Program at Vanderbilt aims to facilitate a multi-faceted approach towards professional development and cultural exchange. To diversify the professional and cultural collaborations between the Humphrey Fellows and their American counterparts, Volunteer State Community College has partnered with Vanderbilt to be an official Associate Campus for the Humphrey Program. Through this partnership, Vol State is able to broaden its international exposure, while exposing more of the Fellows to a community-centric, rurally located institution of higher education.

The goals of the collaboration with Vol State are the following:

  1. Foster cultural understanding for Humphrey Fellows and the Vol State community.
  2. Develop intercultural communication skills for Humphrey Fellows and the Vol State community.
  3. Advance global awareness for Humphrey Fellows and the Vol State community.
  4. Exchange of professional expertise for the Humphrey Fellows and the Vol State community.
summary: The Hubert H. Humphrey Fellowship Program was initiated in 1978 to honor the late senator and vice president and his life-long commitment to international cooperation and public service.

name: Vanderbilt Kennedy Center
description:
What We Do

Researchers work together to solve the mysteries of developmental disabilities and human development. Our approach to research is interdisciplinary, forming partnerships between researchers and clinicians in behavior, education, genetics and neuroscience in order to make breakthroughs in prevention and treatment.

Services are offered to people with disabilities, families, educators and healthcare and other service providers. Our programs apply the latest knowledge obtained through disabilities research and provide service models.

Training allows our progress to benefit communities.Clinicians, students and researchers, nationally and internationally, come to learn the Vanderbilt Kennedy Center's latest innovations.

Impacting communities. We reach out to the community for opportunities to learn more about the science of developmental disabilities and the needs and strengths of the people affected by them. As a result, our research, training and services are focused and effective, having a strong impact on communities.

 

Who We Are

History

The Vanderbilt Kennedy Center for Research on Education and Human Development was one of twelve original members of a national network of research centers on intellectual and developmental disabilities created by the Kennedy administration in 1963.

Mission

Our mission is to facilitate discoveries and best practices that make positive differences in the lives of persons with developmental disabilities and their families. We support and apply scientific research to bring better services and training to the community.

Networks and Affiliates

Vanderbilt Kennedy Center includes a University Center for Excellence in Developmental Disabilities (UCEDD), a Eunice Kennedy Shriver Intellectual and Developmental Disabilities Research Center (EKSIDDRC), the Treatment and Research Institute for Autism Spectrum Disorders, and a Leadership Education in Neurodevelopmental Disabilities training program. It collaborates with and is made up of organizations on national, state and institutional levels.

Policies, Procedures and Facilities

Policies and procedure archives for the Vanderbilt Kennedy Center includes information on anything from parking, to media relations, to privacy policies and more.

Quick Facts

A quick view of the Vanderbilt Kennedy Center includes total number of investigators, affiliated schools, programs, research projects, grants, and more.

Here is a printable Quick Guide with key contacts and phone numbers.

 

What is a developmental disability?

A developmental disability is a condition that is significant and ongoing, begins before age 22, and substantially limits functioning in daily activities of living.

Examples of developmental disabilities include autism, brain injury, cerebral palsy, Down syndrome, fetal alcohol syndrome, intellectual disabilities (mental retardation), Prader-Willi syndrome, spina bifida, and Williams syndrome.

(The Vanderbilt Kennedy Center serves persons with developmental disabilities and their families, as well as persons with all types of disabilities, including those whose disabilities occur after age 22.)
summary: The Vanderbilt Kennedy Center (VKC) mission is to facilitate discoveries and best practices that make positive differences in the lives of persons with developmental disabilities and their families

name: Active Minds at Vanderbilt
description: Active Minds strives to educate the community about mental health in order to decrease stigma and to raise awareness about current topics. The group will participate in a variety activities including discussions, community outreach, and fundraising, all in an attempt to better understand and raise awareness about mental health.
summary: Active Minds is a mental health awareness group that strives to educate the community about the problem of the stigma that surrounds mental health. We also organize a variety of activities to provide support to the Vanderbilt community.

name: Vanderbilt Recreation and Wellness Center
description:
About Us

The Recreation and Wellness Center

The Vanderbilt University Recreation and Wellness Center is a state-of-the-art facility. It includes 290,000 square feet of indoor facilities and seven acres of outdoor facility space.

These facilities include 3 basketball courts, 1 auxiliary gym for basketball and volleyball, 6 racquetball courts, 2 squash courts, an indoor elevated track and a fieldhouse featuring a turf field and 300-meter track. The Rec Center has two fitness centers, one on the first floor and the other on the second floor. The 14,000 square foot fitness center on the first floor has selecterize weight machines, a wide variety of cardio equipment including rowing machines, treadmills, ellipticals, and more, a TRX training area, a power room with ply metric stations, a motion cage and more. The 4,500-sq. ft. fitness loft,overlooking athletics fields is on the second floor. There is a wide variety of cardio equipment including bikes, treadmills, ellipticals, and more. There is a functional trainer and fitness assessment area as well. Our outdoor facilities included a sand volleyball court and 4 lighted multipurepose fields including 1 artificial turf field.
summary: The Vanderbilt University Recreation and Wellness Center is a state-of-the-art facility. It includes 290,000 square feet of indoor facilities and seven acres of outdoor facility space.

name: Department of Communication Studies
description:
Department of Communication Studies 

Courses offered in the Department of Communication Studies include surveys of the rhetorical tradition, studies of the history of oratory, analytical courses designed to improve students' critical skills and appreciation of mediated communication, investigation of classical means of persuasion, argumentation and debate, coupled with their modern applications, and courses which seek to develop understanding of contemporary theories of communication. Special topics courses are offered every year, including occasional Maymester classes. Courses of independent study and research are also available.

The National Communication Association's Honor Society, Lambda Pi Eta has a chapter at Vanderbilt. Students who meet certain academic standard are chosen for membership in their senior year.

The Vanderbilt Varsity Debate Program participates in national collegiate competition with consisten success. All Vanderbilt undergraduate students are eligible for membership on the squad.

Twice a year, students of public speaking classes compete for cash prizes in the Franklin K. Houston Speech Contest.

Graduates from this department follow a variety of careers. Many go to law school or schools of management, or do graduate work in communication, history, journalism, or political science. Careers of past graduates include finance, consulting in human resources, admissions officers, development officers, banking, policy analysis, teaching, media directors, systems design and software consulting, public relations, marketing, account managing, video journalism, law, political lobbying, copywriting and free lance writing, news production, brokerage, and real estate development.

An interdisciplinary major, the Communication of Science and Technology, is now available for enrollment. The new program was developed to address a pressing national issue - the need to communicate the results of scientific and engineering research to the American public. The course of study provides a balance between communication/ humanities and science/engineering. Career opportunities for students majoring in the Communication of Science, Engineering and Technology include science journalism, high tech management, public relations, management consulting, engineering and pharmaceutical sales, science policy, public health, and leadership positions in high technology industries. Graduates will also be prepared to enter graduate or professional schools in fields such as medicine, law, journalism, and business.
summary: Department of Communication Studies for the Vanderbilt College of Arts and Science

name: Study Abroad
description:
Welcome to Study Abroad at Vanderbilt! 
 
Study Abroad facilitates the process of Vanderbilt undergraduate students going abroad by providing resources and support for students before, during, and after the study abroad experience.  Studying abroad is an integral part of the Vanderbilt undergraduate experience, and planning is key. Study Abroad encourages all students, regardless of major, to pursue a study abroad experience that fits their academic interests, personal and professional goals. 
 
Interested in learning more about how study abroad works at Vanderbilt? Begin by attending a Study Abroad OverVU  session for an overview of programs, opportunities and processes. Attend other thematic events listed here as you plan for your study abroad experience. And remember, your financial aid travels with you abroad during the academic year. 
 
Follow us on Instagram @vanderbiltstudyabroad to sneak peeks of VU students' lives abroad, get updates on events, application deadlines, and more!
summary: Learn more and apply for more than 100 study abroad programs and scholarships.

name: American Association of Physicians of Indian Origin
description:
AAPIO is a national organization which promotes the professional, political, and social goals of Indian-American medical students and resident-physicians. This chapter at Vanderbilt attempts to promote representation and appreciation of South Asian culture by organizing events and developing a space for students and allies. Membership is open to all School of Medicine students.

This organization is only open to current VUSM MD Students.
summary: AAPIO is a national organization which promotes the professional, political, and social goals of Indian-American medical students and resident-physicians. This Vanderbilt chapter attempts to promote representation & appreciation of South Asian culture.

name: Health Professions Advisory Office
description:
GENERAL INFORMATION

The HPAO office is here to assist Vanderbilt undergraduate students interested in the health care professions. The HPAO office meets with students interested in medicine (both allopathic and osteopathic), dentistry, veterinary medicine, pharmacy, physical therapy, podiatric, etc. We are here to answer questions that you might have during the coming year.

 

Please visit our website for general information. To book an appointment, visit Handshake

 

or view the walk-in hours. Join our Brightspace page (found under "Self Registration") to see materials related to obtaining shadowing experience at VUMC, applying to professional programs in the health field, and more. If you are currently not on our ListServ email distribution list, please request to be added so you may receive valuable information by sending an email to hpao@vanderbilt.edu. 

 

Students interested in an advanced career in nursing are encouraged to contact the School of Nursing for advice and counseling (Pre-Nursing Handbook).
summary: The HPAO office is here to assist Vanderbilt undergraduate students interested in the health care professions.

name: Chemical and Physical Biology Graduate Student Association
description:
The purpose of the CPB GSA is to promote professional and social interaction among graduate students within the Chemical and Physical Biology program while also serving to facilitate feedback between students and faculty members. The CPB GSA will also appoint a committee responsible for the organization of the annual scientific retreat. Usually all members of the council are part of this committee. Furthermore, the CPB GSA will assist students in their preparation for their qualifying exams or committee meetings, which has been done in the form of mock exams.

Membership is open to all Vanderbilt University graduate students within the Chemical and Physical Biology program and those interested in joining the program who have the motivation to participate.
summary: The purpose of the CPB GSA is to promote professional and social interaction among graduate students within the Chemical and Physical Biology program while also serving to facilitate feedback between students and faculty members.

name: Vanderbilt Chess Club
description: We meet every Thursday at 7 PM to play chess and improve. Location: Rand Top Floor Dining Area. All skill levels welcome! We use GroupMe, so make sure to join our chat: https://web.groupme.com/join_group/70068006/5LpN9b61
summary: The Vanderbilt chess club is a community of commodores who enjoy playing chess. All skill levels are welcome. Email austin.g.powell@vanderbilt.edu for any questions. Join our GroupMe: https://web.groupme.com/join_group/70068006/5LpN9b61

name: Student Organization Funding  Committee (Formerly SSF)
description: The Student Organization Funding Committee (formerly SSF) is made up of the Student Organization Funding Chair, the Student Organization Funding Comptroller, the Student Organization Funding Subcommittee Chairs, and representatives from the Dean of Students Office. On an annual basis, they review applications for student organizational funding and allocate a portion of the Student Services Fee paid by each student. They also review applications to the Student Organization Funding Contingency Fund (Collaboration and Innovation Fund) on a rolling basis.
summary: The Student Organization Fund Committee allocates funding to student organizations on an annual basis.

name: Vanderbilt Law School
description:
Among the nation's leading law schools, Vanderbilt stands out for its pathbreaking approaches to preparing lawyers for the 21st century, connecting outstanding theoretical training to real-world strategies and experiences that provide immediate advantages to graduates. With about 195 students in each entering JD class and an accessible and distinguished faculty, Vanderbilt offers a challenging and collegial intellectual environment. An extensive network of more than 8,500 alumni in 49 states, DC, three US territories, and 32 foreign nations reflects the school’s national and international reach.

Drawn from across the nation and around the world, VLS students have remarkably varied backgrounds and professional goals. But a surprising number have something important in common: a campus visit convinced them that Vanderbilt is where they want to be.
summary: Among the nation's leading law schools, Vanderbilt stands out for its pathbreaking approaches to preparing lawyers for the 21st century.

name: Vandy Tap That!
description: Vandy Tap That is a tap dance organization for tappers of all levels, from those who have never tried on tap shoes to former competition dancers. There are beginner, intermediate, and advanced weekly classes as well as a company performance group. Our goal is to provide a space for the Vanderbilt community to learn about and enjoy tap dancing!
summary: Vanderbilt's one and only tap dancing organization where dancers of all levels of experience can join and have a great time learning to tap!

name: Phi Alpha Delta, Pre-Law Fraternity
description:
Mission

Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International is a professional law fraternity advancing integrity, compassion and courage through service to the student, the school, the profession and the community.

Vision

We are the preeminent law fraternity promoting the bonds of fraternalism and we are the leader in the development and advancement of professional ideals.

Core Values

Bound together by tradition and our common interest in the law, we share these core values: Integrity, Compassion, Courage, Professionalism, Service, Diversity, and Innovation.

Declaration of Purpose

The purpose of this Fraternity shall be to form a strong bond uniting students and teachers of the law with members of the Bench and Bar in a fraternal fellowship designed to advance the ideals of liberty and equal justice under law; to stimulate excellence in scholarship; to inspire the virtues of compassion and courage; to foster integrity and professional competence; to promote the welfare of its members; and to encourage their moral, intellectual, and cultural advancement; so that each member may enjoy a lifetime of honorable professional and public service.
summary: Phi Alpha Delta is a pre-professional law fraternity dedicated to helping those who will be the future of the law. The Pre-Law branch is devoted to teaching undergraduate students about law school and helping students prepare for law school.

name: Office of Conferences
description:
Summer Conference Coordinator
Click Here for Application


Welcome to Vanderbilt University!

Experience a world to discover in the heart of Nashville, Tennessee. Vanderbilt’s gorgeous 330-acre campus is a national arboretum and home to a mixture of historic and LEED certified buildings to hold your summer conference, camp or retreat. We are a comprehensive research university, providing innovative programs, state-of-the-art facilities, award winning dining services and a supportive environment for interdisciplinary inquiry. No matter the size or complexity of your event, the one stop shop at Vanderbilt’s Office of Conferences has the resources to help you achieve your goals and host a successful event.

Last summer, we housed over 70 unique groups such as the Tennessee Special Olympics, Game Breaker Lacrosse, iD Tech Camps, National Cheerleading Association, Vanderbilt Football Passing Camp, Youth About Business Nashville Summer Business Camp, Vanderbilt Owen Accelerator Summer Business Institute , Vanderbilt Summer Academy, Sigma Pi International Fraternity, Hillvue Heights Church Mission X and many more.

Vanderbilt’s Office of Conferences will provide assistance to your group and accommodate all your needs by offering the following services:

  • Courteous, customer-oriented professionals ready to serve you.
  • You will be assigned a key contact person to assist with all aspects of your stay, from pre-planning to your arrival on campus; from beginning to end.
  • Secure, clean residence hall accommodations.
  • 24/7 on-call staff and reception desk services.
  • Registration for checking in to and out of the residence hall.
  • Full service meeting space in technology-enriched classrooms, auditoriums, ballrooms, athletic and recreational facilities.
  • Rental of audio/visual equipment.
  • Arrangements for conference dining.
  • Catering arrangements.
  • Linen services, as arranged in advance.
  • Transportation needs.
  • Access to University libraries for paying guests.
  • Access to the Student Recreation Center for paying guests.

 

Facilities

Vanderbilt’s Office of Conferences provides secure, clean residence hall accommodations with 24/7 on-call staff and reception desk services.

You’ll enjoy full service meeting spaces in technology-enriched classrooms, auditoriums, ballrooms, athletic and recreational facilities. Rental of audio/visual equipment and catering arrangements can all be facilitated by our staff.

See the Facility Map to learn more about residential, dining, and meeting spaces on Vanderbilt’s campus.

Photo: The Martha Rivers Ingram Commons Center on the Ingram Commons/Vanderbilt Peabody College Campus.

 

 

Residential

Stambaugh House offers a private balcony to view the Nashville skyline and housing for over 150 guests.

Vanderbilt University offers a variety of residential accommodations including traditional rooms (singles and doubles), as well as suites, lodges, and apartments (efficiencies, one-bedrooms, and two-bedrooms).

All accommodations are furnished, air conditioned, and equipped with Ethernet data ports and cable television ports. Many spaces are fully accessible. Amenities unique to each residence hall include lounges, kitchens, ATM machines, laundry facilities, music practice rooms and markets. Several residence halls have small seminar rooms available at no cost. Room rates are available with linen or without linen. In addition, groups can choose to pay a nominal fee for more deluxe housekeeping services including towel exchanges, beds made and trash removal.

The Commons

Inspired by Thomas Jefferson’s plan for the University of Virginia, the Peabody campus has been a registered Historic Landmark since 1966. The classic symmetry of the Peabody architecture and generous green spaces present an interesting counterpoint to Vanderbilt’s more informal, park-like original campus. The Commons has ten houses grouped around four quads. Each residential facility has a mixture of double and single occupancy rooms, a technology enriched seminar room, laundry facilities, abundant light and common spaces. West Hall houses up to 115 guests, East Hall – 105, Gillette Hall – 230, Memorial Hall – 85, Crawford Hall – 155, Ingram Hall – 301, Murray Hall – 155, Stambaugh Hall – 165, and Sutherland Hall – 169, for a total of 1663 spaces for guest accommodations. The Martha Rivers Ingram Commons Center is conveniently located near all the Commons residence halls and is home to the Commons Dining Gallery and our very own coffee shop and market, Common Grounds.

Branscomb Quadrangle

Located near central campus, Branscomb Quadrangle offers easy access to classroom and athletic facilities, as well as to the Recreation Center. Four connecting halls, Lupton, Scales, Stapleton and Vaughn, make up the quadrangle, which can accommodate up to 772 guests in double rooms. There are several meeting space options available within Branscomb. In addition, Branscomb connects to the Student Life Center which features approximately 10,000 square feet of meeting space, including beautiful ballrooms, classrooms and the Board of Trust room overlooking the lawn and courtyard leading to the E. Bronson Ingram Studio Arts building and the University Club.

 

Meeting

:: Map of Meeting Accommodations


You can easily walk across the campus within 15 minutes. All of our buildings stand shoulder-to-shoulder, evidence that Vanderbilt is a common enterprise, a varied but cohesive community.

Many options are available on campus for meetings. Some of the most popular include the recently renovated Wilson Hall. It contains two lecture halls that can seat 296 and 129, and several classrooms with seating for 20 to 60 people, most with a/v already built in. In addition, there is a lobby area for breaks and a terrace and lawn for outdoor functions.

Furman is a beautifully appointed building that contains a lecture hall to accommodate 214 and several classrooms with seating for 12 to 50 people. As with all meeting space on campus, audio/visual equipment is either available in the room or it can be rented from an outside source. Langford Auditorium is a state-of-the-art facility adjacent to the medical center. It seats up to 1,154 people and includes a 28′ x 60′ stage. A truly impressive meeting site, it’s ideal for speakers, general sessions, and performances.

Vanderbilt University Law School has completed a four-year renovation process adding 70,000 square feet of new space to the building. Rooms are available in various sizes and seating styles to suit almost any need. There are rooms that reflect classroom/lecture hall style seating. Others include conference and meeting room styles all of which can seat anywhere from 12-245 people. Some of the featured and more popular rooms include the Flynn Auditorium, Faculty Conference Room, and the Alumni Room. All of our rooms can be equipped with the latest technology. Lobby and courtyard space are also available for receptions, lunches, dinner parties, and more. In addition, the Law School has video conferencing capabilities.

 

Catering and Dining Facilities

Awaiting you on campus is Vanderbilt University’s award-winning dining services. Conference meals will be served in either the Ingram Commons Dining Center, or the Rand Dining Center located in the Sarratt Student Center on the south end of Alumni Lawn.

Commons Food Gallery

The state-of-the-art dining facility at The Commons Center includes a salad bar, a wide variety of meal options, grill, and vegetarian fare.

The center is also the first gold-certified (certification for its environmentally friendly construction) building in Nashville.

Rand Dining Center

Rand Dining Center is the focal point of the campus community. Its central location, great food, and comfortable atmosphere provide our conference attendees an opportunity to gather, socialize, and interact.

Conference guests must purchase a conference meal plan, which consists of breakfast, lunch and dinner for each night they stay at Vanderbilt. Other dining options include catered meals and breaks, and meals delivered to alternate locations such as a residence hall or other university facilities.
summary: No matter the size or complexity of your event, the one stop shop at Vanderbilt’s Office of Conferences has the resources to help you achieve your goals and host a successful event.

name: Vanderbilt University Public Safety
description:
About

The Vanderbilt University Police Department comes under the charge of the Office of the Vice Chancellor for the Division of Administration. As one of Tennessee’s larger law enforcement agencies, the Police Department provides comprehensive law enforcement and security services to all components of Vanderbilt University including the main campus, Vanderbilt University Medical Center, Vanderbilt Health at One Hundred Oaks, and a variety of University-owned facilities throughout the Davidson County area.

The Police Department includes a staff of over 100 people, organized into four bureaus: Office of the Chief, Administrative Services, Support Services and Patrol (Main Campus and Medical Center). All of Vanderbilt’s commissioned officers have completed officer training at a state-certified police academy. Those officers hold Special Police commissions and are required to attend annual in-service, as well as on-the-job, training. The Department also employs non-academy trained officers for security-related functions. Employment opportunities are available.

Mission Statement

Vanderbilt University Police Department is a professional law enforcement agency dedicated to the protection and security of Vanderbilt University and its diverse community.

To fulfill this mission we will:

  • Deliver superior law enforcement services with integrity and pride.
  • Respect and safeguard the dignity and rights of all individuals.
  • Demonstrate respect toward the people we serve and one another to maintain an environment of trust.
  • Develop partnerships with all segments of our community through effective communication and collaboration.
  • Carefully select and maintain a well-trained, educated and professional staff.
  • Accept individual responsibility and accountability for our actions.

The Vanderbilt University Police Department is an institutional member of and accredited by the International Association of Campus Law Enforcement Administrators (IACLEA). The Department is also accredited through the Commission on Accreditation for Law Enforcement Agencies (CALEA) and the Tennessee Law Enforcement Accreditation Program (TLEA). Vanderbilt is the first university police department to achieve accreditation in the state-wide program.

Authority

Non-commissioned and commissioned officers staff the department. Commissioned officers are empowered to make arrests as “Special Police Officers,” through the authority of the Chief of Police of the Metropolitan Government of Nashville and Davidson County. Vanderbilt officers with Special Police Commissions have the same authority as that of a municipal law enforcement officer, while on property owned by Vanderbilt, on adjacent public streets and sidewalks, and nearby neighborhoods. When a Vanderbilt student is involved in an off-campus offense, police officers may assist with the investigation in cooperation with local, state, or federal law enforcement.

Metro Nashville police routinely work and communicate with campus officers on any serious incidents occurring on campus or in the immediate neighborhood and business areas surrounding campus. Vanderbilt University operates no off-campus housing or off-campus student organization facilities. However, many graduate students and some undergraduate students live in the neighborhoods surrounding Vanderbilt.

While Metro Nashville police have primary jurisdiction in all areas off campus, Vanderbilt officers can and do respond to student-related incidents that occur in close proximity to campus. Vanderbilt officers have direct radio communications with the city police, fire department, and ambulance services to facilitate rapid response in any emergency situation.

Strategic Plan

As the first Strategic Plan for Vanderbilt University Police Department, this three year plan is a work in progress and is designed to serve as an adaptable, flexible guide to focus the priorities of the organization. The plan is practical and realistic, providing a course for the future. As Vanderbilt University and Medical Center continue to grow and expand to meet the needs of our community, the Police Department must be prepared to meet the increasing service demands that result from that growth. This Strategic Plan will guide the department as we meet the challenges of new leadership within the University and the department, an increasingly conservative budget forecast, and the challenges of emerging technology. This Strategic Plan lays the groundwork for staffing plans, equipment prioritization and replacement schedules, identification and focus of agency training goals, and details departmental goals and objectives.

Vanderbilt University Police Department Strategic Plan
summary: The Vanderbilt University Police Department is a professional law enforcement agency dedicated to the protection and security of Vanderbilt University and its diverse community.

name: School of Nursing Graduate Student Council
description:
The purpose of the Graduate Nursing Council is to serve as a liaison between MN, MSN, Post Masters Certificate and DNP students, faculty and administration in the Vanderbilt University School of Nursing and the general University community.  The Grad Council achieves this purpose by:

 

  1. Providing a framework for vocalization of graduate and pre-specialty student’s needs
  2. Serving as representatives to School and University committees
  3. Promoting social interaction within and outside the VUSN

 

The Graduate Council solicits members through a self-nomination process.  Interested students are encouraged to complete the attached self-nomination form.  Any interested student is invited to become a member of the Graduate Council.   Members will serve from September, 203 through August, 2024.  Council members must be full time students in good academic standing.  Graduate Council membership generally consists of at least 10 members from the Pre-Specialty student group (including 1 from the RN Pre-Specialty Group) and at least 2 members from the Direct Entry student group.
summary: The Purpose of the VUSN Graduate Student Council is to serve as a liaison between nursing students and the faculty, staff and administration of the School of Nursing

name: Vanderbilt University Student Chapter of the Society For Biomaterials
summary: Our purpose is to encourage the development, dissemination, integration and utilization of knowledge in biomaterials primarily among students, the Vanderbilt University community, and other members of the Society For Biomaterials.

name: Alumni Relations
summary: Vanderbilt University Office of Alumni Relations

name: Black Student Nurses Affinity Group
description:
Welcome to the Black Student Nurses Affinity Group (BSNAG)! 
 
At BSNAG, you’ll find opportunities to connect with peers, faculty, staff, and the local community while exploring topics that matter. We host lectures, panel discussions, volunteer, and participate in scholarly projects that spotlight social and cultural experiences relevant to our community. We also explore the diverse roles of advanced practice nursing and promote cultural awareness in healthcare. 
 
Organizational activities include:
- Serve as a means of social support for students matriculating through VUSN
- Assist VUSN students with successful completion of generalist, advanced practice, and doctoral nursing programs
- Collaborate with community organizations to address the health needs of people in the Nashville-Metro area
- Provide social and networking opportunities for VUSN students
- Connect VUSN students with peer and faculty mentors
 
Membership is open to all nursing students who want to join the conversation and make a difference.
 
Join us to connect, collaborate, and thrive in your nursing journey! Let’s make an impact together!
summary: We aim to create a supportive space for self-identified nursing students. Our mission is to uplift and empower our members through mentorship, networking, and community-building activities.

name: VAW Global Health Alliances
description:
VAW Global Health Alliances (VAW Global) is a global health and development organization committed to cultivating sustainable and equitable partnerships with local communities and professionals through support and capacity-building for essential and accessible health services. Our focus is to work hand-in-hand with local initiatives and leaders to provide communities with improved access to medical, dental, and veterinary treatment, medication, nutritional security, clean water, and health education.

On international medical service trips, interns will shadow and assist the doctors, perform triage, measure vital statistics, manage the pharmacy, and teach public health classes, all under the direct supervision of the experienced and respected physicians.
summary: VAW Global Health Alliances provides international service opportunities for Vanderbilt students to help improve access to healthcare in developing communities around the world.

name: Civil & Environmental Engineering Council
description:
The purpose of the CEE Council is to promote social and professional interaction among the CEE graduate students and to facilitate communication between the department students and faculty members. Membership is open to all students at Vanderbilt University (including but not limited to faculty, staff, graduate students, and undergraduate students) with the interest to participate. 

The CEE Council is not affiliated with any local, state, regional, or national organizations.
summary: The purpose of the CEE Council is to promote social and professional interaction among the CEE graduate students and to facilitate communication between the department students and faculty members.

name: Harmonic Notion
description: Harmonic Notion is an ICCA-competing, co-ed a cappella group formed in January 2013. We have quickly risen to great heights, advancing to ICCA Quarterfinals in all 12 years of our existence. In 2018 we placed 3rd at the ICCA South Quarterfinal, and in 2012 we won the ICCA South Quarterfinal, advanced to Semifinals, and won best arrangement! More recently, in 2021 we placed 2nd in the ICCA South Quarterfinal, and won best choreography at Semifinals. We were also recognized on the 2020 and 2021 Best of College A Cappella Album. We also record music, with various EPs available on all streaming platforms (and another to come in the spring of 2025!). We showcase our music at concerts and campus events throughout each semester, striving to perform a cappella music at a high level for the Vanderbilt audience and throughout the greater musical community with a high-quality sound and stellar showmanship.
summary: Harmonic Notion is an ICCA competing, co-ed a cappella group. We strive to perform a cappella music at a high level for the Vanderbilt audience and throughout the greater musical community with a high-quality sound and stellar showmanship.

name: Peabody Office of Student Life
description:
Our Commitments

Our Mission: Supporting Peabody students by co-creating spaces and experiences in which to thrive.

Our Values in Action:

  • Advocating for positive change
  • Building communities of belonging
  • Co-creating meaningful co-curricular programming
  • Developing future global leaders and changemakers
  • Fostering student well-being
  • Supporting student-led initiatives

A Model for Holistic Engagement Compass

We partner with students, staff, faculty, and administrators to engage in programming that meets students’ diverse needs through a Holistic Engagement Compass. Areas of programming include: Cultural, Physical, Intellectual, Occupational, Community Building, Spiritual, and Emotional.
summary: Our office promotes engagement and holistic well-being for all Peabody students through community building and co-curricular programming. We partner with programs and offices across the university to ensure student awareness of and access to resources.

name: Center for Latin American, Caribbean, and Latinx Studies
description:
Why the Center for Latin American, Caribbean, and Latinx Studies? The Center for Latin American, Caribbean, and Latinx Studies fosters research and public engagement that center on populations that cross and re-cross borders constructed by geography, linguistics, class, race, and gender. As an interdisciplinary field of study, CLACX provides students the tools that allow them to succeed in a global marketplace: the ability to think comparatively and critically, in several languages, and to engage with the largest demographic populations in the Western Hemisphere.

name: Vanderbilt Satellite Club
summary: The primary objective of VUSat is to supplement and encourage student passion for space through a combination of technical projects, experiential learning, speaker events, and the discussion of space news, history, and various other relevant topics.

name: Vanderbilt Geology Club
summary: Our mission is to create a social, recreational, and educational space on campus for students to learn and express their passion for geology, and create opportunities for geology-interested students to explore new topics not covered in the classroom.

name: Next Steps at Vanderbilt Ambassadores
description:
The purpose of this organization is to empower the Vanderbilt students with intellectual disabilities enrolled in Next Steps through positive peer-to-peer relationships and support. Support areas include academics, organizational skills, exercise, inclusive campus life participation, and other program requirements.

Mission: To equip students in the Next Steps program with the skills and experiences that lead them to a future of flourishing.

Vision: To support students in the Next Steps program experience a sense of belonging within the larger Vanderbilt student body.

Current Ambassadores: Visit our Brightspace course for more specific information.
summary: Next Steps Ambassadores provide peer mentor support for students enrolled in the Next Steps at Vanderbilt certificate program in a variety of roles.

name: Medicine, Education, and Development for Low Income Families Everywhere
description: Vanderbilt MEDLIFE Chapter is an organization that offers students an opportunity to engage in hands-on work both locally and globally in order to advocate for underserved communities. Through our Mobile Clinics and community development projects, students are able to work alongside doctors and other professionals as we bring meaningful, sustainable change to areas of extreme poverty. Dedicated students also have the opportunity to help direct our domestic and international operations through various leadership positions and internships in Latin America. MEDLIFE has already served tens of thousands of individuals, and we will continue to grow with your help.
summary: MEDLIFE is an organization that partners with low-income communities across the globe, improving their access to medicine, education, & community development initiatives. We achieve our mission through Mobile Clinics, fundraising, & student involvement.

name: Global China Connection
description:
Objectives of GCC is to:

  1. Create business, networking or internship opportunities for students and their international counterparts to socialize, learn, and work together. 
  2. Bring together GCC members for learning, networking, and developing future professional opportunities through on-campus events, educational seminars, telecommunications, and multimedia tools.
  3. Advance international trust and understanding within GCC members and alums personal and professional relationships.
  4. Promote high ethical standards for GCC members’ academic, professional, and personal lives along with a commitment to make the world a better place through this lifelong fellowship.
  5. Have fun together.

We were founded in 2008 and had 58 chapters worldwide. For more information, please check http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Global_China_Connection
summary: Global China Connection is a global network for university students and young professionals of all nationalities looking to engage China’s emergence in the world.

name: Jewish Studies Program
description:
Vanderbilt's Department of Jewish Studies facilitates the critical study of Jewish history, religion, language, philosophy, politics, culture, society, music, art, and literature across continents and millennia.

Since Jewish Studies entertains the nature of the human condition, we provide a comprehensive background for individuals interested in diverse career paths.
summary: Vanderbilt's Program in Jewish Studies facilitates the critical study of Jewish history, religion, language, philosophy, politics, culture, society, music, art, and literature across continents and millennia.

name: Vanderbilt Innovation and Entrepreneurship Society
description:
The Vanderbilt Innovation and Entrepreneurship Society serves passionate innovators in the execution of ideas by providing resources, education, and an entrepreneurial community.

Through programs including Innovation Cohort, 1000 Pitches, Speaker/Workshop Series, and 48 Hour Launch, VINES provides wraparound support for students at all stages of their innovation journey.
summary: The Vanderbilt Innovation and Entrepreneurship Society (VINES) serves passionate innovators in the execution of ideas by providing resources, education, and an entrepreneurial community.

name: Commodore Orchestra
description:
Our Mission

The purpose of the VCO is to provide inclusive, musically enjoyable, educational, and culturally enriching performance opportunities for all members of the Vanderbilt University community while delivering high–quality music.


Our Beliefs and Commitments


We believe that music is essential to the well–being of both the individual and society at large. In addition, we believe that it is beneficial for everyone to participate in a musical experience at a meaningful level either as the performer or the listener.


We commit to encouraging a non-competitive atmosphere. We will welcome anyone from the Vanderbilt community to participate and will not exclude or discriminate against anyone for race, gender, sexual orientation, disability, or age.
summary: The purpose of the VCO is to provide inclusive, musically enjoyable, educational, and culturally enriching performance opportunities for all members of the Vanderbilt University community while delivering high–quality music.

name: Phi Sigma Rho
description:
Our goal is to foster sisterhood between women pursuing engineering and technical studies. We empower women who are heading into male-dominated STEM fields while forming friendships that will stand the test of time. 

Our sorority was founded in 1984 at Purdue University. Through Phi Sigma Rho, our sisters develop the highest standard of personal integrity, strive for academic excellence, and build friendships that will last a lifetime. To learn more about our national organization, please visit phisigmarho.org.
summary: Our goal is to foster sisterhood between women pursuing engineering and technical studies. We empower women who are heading into male-dominated STEM fields while forming friendships that will stand the test of time.

name: Department of Sociology
description:
Sociology is a vital and integral part of national and international profession. The thematic diversity of Vanderbilt sociology stems from the faculty's wide-ranging interests and methodological competencies and a strong commitment to academic freedom. As part of Vanderbilt's College of Arts & Science, the faculty represents a broad set of scholarly interests including:
  Arts, culture, religion
  Cities, states, and political economy
  Deviant behavior and social control
  Gender and sexuality
  Health and the life course
  Race, ethnicity, and immigration
  Social movements, politics, and power
  Work, labor, and occupations
summary: Sociology is a vital and integral part of national and international profession. The thematic diversity of Vanderbilt sociology stems from the faculty's wide-ranging interests and methodological competencies and a strong commitment to academic freedom.

A spacious fully equipped onsite fitness facility atop Kensington Garage is available for faculty and staff with regular full-time or regular part-time status where they can exercise on cardiovascular and strength equipment, engage in a variety of group fitness classes and obtain fitness consultation with experienced fitness professionals.

Services

Health Plus offers a variety of programs/services and resources to promote healthy lifestyles in the Vanderbilt community. Included are health screenings, classes, online education and self-help options.  Access is available to those located on or off campus and at a variety of times to meet the needs of Vanderbilt's diverse community.

name: EmbrACE
description:
The purpose of EmbrACE is to give female-identifying undergraduate students at Vanderbilt University the opportunity to regularly mentor elementary and middle school girls in the Nashville area on age- and gender-related issues. The topics for lesson plans relate to: maintaining a positive self-image, building healthy relationships, setting goals and working towards accomplishing them, and making a meaningful impact on one’s community. 

As an organization, our goals are to:

1) Provide girls in grades 3-8 with passionate, reliable, and knowledgeable college mentors

2) Advocate for the support and empowerment of young girls in disadvantaged communities

3) Challenge the unreasonable expectations for women and girls in contemporary society
summary: The purpose of EmbrACE (Aspire, Care, Empower) is to give female-identifying undergraduate students at Vanderbilt University the opportunity to regularly mentor elementary and middle school girls in the Nashville area on age- and gender-related issues.

name: Mail Services
description: Vanderbilt University Mail Services provides the Vanderbilt community with an efficient and economical mail system that ensures quick and accurate delivery for both incoming and outgoing mail and packages. We realize that we are a vital link between the campus and the rest of the world. Whether we are delivering a care package of cookies from parents to a new student away from home for the first time, or processing the mailing of brochures that will send a department's message across the country, we are committed to providing the highest level of care and service. Read more about us.
summary: Vanderbilt University Mail Services provides the Vanderbilt community with an efficient and economical mail system that ensures quick and accurate delivery for both incoming and outgoing mail and packages.

name: Department of Economics
description:
The Department of Economics is in Vanderbilt’s College of Arts and Science. It consists of more than 30 full-time faculty whose research covers most major areas of modern economics. The department has been consistently ranked among the top 40 economics departments in the United States.

The faculty are committed to delivering outstanding undergraduate and graduate programs and to producing high-quality research. Economics is the largest undergraduate major in the College of Arts and Science. The undergraduate program provides rigorous training in micro and macroeconomics and offers a variety of advanced elective courses. Students are prepared for future careers in business, law, and government, or for pursuing graduate study. The Ph.D. program in economics builds on a core of economic theory and econometric methods, and equips students to conduct frontier research in their dissertations. Students enter the Ph.D. program from around the country and abroad. Recent graduates have been hired in tenure-track positions at the London School of Economics, University of Michigan, the University of North Carolina, Emory University, and Universitat Pompeu Fabra, among others. The Graduate Program in Economic Development offers the MA degree in economic development, as well as a non-thesis certificate in economic development. Since its inception in 1956, about 1300 students from 120 countries have studied in the program and many of them have gone to distinguished careers in public service and international development organizations.
summary: The Department of Economics is in Vanderbilt’s College of Arts and Science. It consists of more than 30 full-time faculty whose research covers most major areas of modern economics.

name: Vanderbilt Student Academy of Audiology
description: The mission of the Vanderbilt University Chapter of SAA is to serve as a collective voice for students within the chapter area and to advance the rights, interests, and welfare of students pursuing careers in audiology. Chapters will engage students in lifelong professional activities that promote and advance the profession of audiology, and provide services, information, education, representation and advocacy for the profession and the public we serve.
summary: The Student Academy of Audiology is the national student organization of the American Academy of Audiology that serves as a collective voice for students and advances the rights, interests, and welfare of students pursuing careers in audiology.

name: Branscomb Quadrangle
description:
Branscomb Quadrangle

Located near central campus, Branscomb offers easy access to sporting events and the Recreation Center, as well as to dining and classroom facilities. Branscomb Quadrangle, built in 1962, is made up of four connecting residence halls: Lupton, Scales, Stapleton, and Vaughn.

Branscomb Quadrangle is named for Chancellor Harvie Branscomb and his wife, Margaret. The name of each residence hall in Branscomb memorializes one of the outstanding women in Vanderbilt history.

Lupton House

Lupton currently houses nearly 300 upperclass students. A resident adviser or head resident lives on each floor with up to 58 residents housed in traditional double rooms. All rooms and corriders are carpeted. The typical room measures 10’6″ x 15’6″.

Lupton House is named for Kate Lupton Wilkinson, the first woman to receive a degree from Vanderbilt University.

Scales House

Scales currently houses nearly 130 upperclass students. Each of its three floors houses up to 43 residents and one resident adviser in 26 traditional double rooms which are connected in pairs by half baths. All rooms and corridors are carpeted. The typical room measures 10’6″ x 15’6″.

Scales House is named for Anne Scales Benedict, an alumna and benefactress of the university who was a zealous advocate of improved educational opportunities for women at Vanderbilt.

Stapleton House

Stapleton currently houses 150 upperclass students. Each of its 3 floors houses up to 49 residents and one resident adviser in 26 traditional double rooms. All rooms and corridors are carpeted. A typical room measures 10’6″ x 15’6″.

Stapleton House is named for Ada Bell Stapleton, who was the first Dean of Women at Vanderbilt and also served on the University faculty as Professor of Literature.

Vaughn House

Vaughn currently houses nearly 100 upperclass students, along with the Office of Housing and Residential Education, which is located on the first floor. The second and third floors of Vaughn each house up to 49 residents and one resident adviser in 26 traditional double rooms which are connected in pairs by half baths. All residential rooms and corridors are carpeted. The typical room measures 10’6″ x 15’6″.

Vaughn House is named for Stella Scott Vaughn, who served as Vanderbilt’s first Director of Physical Education following her graduation from the University.
summary: Branscomb Quadrangle is an upperclass housing facility located near central campus.  It is made up of four connecting residence halls: Lupton, Scales, Stapleton, and Vaughn.

name: Alumni Lawn- Cole & Tolman
description:
Cole Hall

Located on spacious Alumni Lawn on the central campus, Cole offers easy access to dining and classroom facilities. It houses 116 upperclass women mostly in single rooms. Residents live on all four floors. One Resident Adviser/Head Resident lives on the second, third and fourth floors.

Constructed shortly after World War II in a modified collegiate gothic style, Cole has recently been completely renovated.

Cole Hall is named after Whiteford R. Cole, an early alumnus, who was president of the L & N Railroad and a longtime member of the Board of Trust.

Tolman Hall

Located on the central campus, Tolman offers easy access to dining and classroom facilities. It houses 102 upperclass men in single and double rooms. Residents live on the second, third and fourth floors. One Resident Adviser lives on each floor. A faculty member and his spouse currently live in an apartment on the second floor.

Constructed shortly after World War II and recently completely renovated, Tolman Hall is a modified collegiate gothic residence hall facing Alumni Lawn.

Tolman Hall is named after Dr. Herbert C. Tolman, Classics scholar and Dean of the College of Arts and Science. Dr. Tolman was also the founder of the Vanderbilt chapter of Phi Beta Kappa.
summary: Cole and Tolman Halls are both located on central campus, and offer easy access to dining and classroom facilities.  Cole houses 116 upperclass women mostly in single rooms, and Tolman houses 102 upperclass men in single and double rooms.

summary: The Political Science GSA works to promote excellence in scholarship and teaching amongst the graduate student body of the Department of Political Science. We create community within our department, and work to identify and solve common challenges.

name: Writing Studio
description:
At the Writing Studio, you will meet with writing consultants who are trained to discuss any writing project--whether for a class or otherwise--at any point in your writing process. Appointments begin on the hour and typically last for 50-minutes.

Our staff consists of Graduate and Undergraduate Writing Consultants from a variety of disciplines. In conversation with consultants, writers can clarify their ideas, strengthen their arguments, and work to improve their writing skills. Appointments are available both in-person at a variety of campus locations as well as online.

To schedule an appointment (any location or modality), visit the Writing Studio website: vanderbilt.edu/writing

Learn More:
Get to Know the Writing Studio: Undergraduate Edition
Get to Know the Writing Studio: Graduate and Professional Student Edition

More VU Know Video Introduction to the Writing Studio and Tutoring Services

 

APPOINTMENTS

To schedule an appointment, visit the Writing Studio's website.

What to expect

We believe that all writers benefit from conversation about their writing. Expect your one-to-one meeting with your writing consultant to be very interactive. Your consultant will engage you in conversation about your goals for the session, your purpose in writing, and your ideas.  Consultants are supportive readers who want to help you think about concerns like structure, argumentation, and clarity of expression. Keep in mind that the goal of an appointment is both to help you with a particular piece of writing and to contribute to the development of your writing skills overall. To reach that goal, consultants might ask you questions, suggest specific writing strategies, or initiate writing activities to help you generate or revise text. Since appointments are only 50-minutes long, you and your writing consultant will have to prioritize concerns you bring to the session.

Becoming a better writer happens over time and with continuous practice and conversation. Think of each session at the Writing Studio as an opportunity to work on at least one aspect of your writing.
summary: At the Writing Studio, you have the opportunity to meet individually with trained consultants to talk about any writing project or concern. Our services are free and confidential for any member of Vanderbilt’s scholarly community.

name: LEAPS- Liaisons Educating & Advocating  for Psychological Support
description:
MISSION STATEMENT

LEAPS is a group of undergraduate students who work with the Center for Student Wellbeing as peer educators to promote positive mental health and wellbeing on the Vanderbilt University campus through outreach activities.

GOALS:

  • To decrease the stigma associated with seeking help
  • To improve visibility and accessibility of the campus resources
  • To serve as student leaders and role-models
  • To increase knowledge and understanding of mental health and wellness topics of college students
  • To increase awareness and understanding on campus of mental health issues and emotional wellness
  • To develop programming and events for the VU Campus
  • Advocate for effective campus practices to promote positive coping behaviors

ACTIVITIES

  1. Host events that promote positive coping skills and healthy choices
  2. Collaborate with other campus organizations and departments on joint programming efforts
  3. Co-facilitate presentations to student groups about topics such as: Campus resources, Stress & Coping, Healthy Relationships, & Academic Success
  4. Host wellness information tables at various campus events
  5. Serve as positive role models for peers
summary: LEAPS is a group of undergraduate students who work with the Center for Student Wellbeing as peer educators to promote positive mental health and wellbeing on Vanderbilt campus through outreach activities.

name: Undergraduate Research
description:
Undergraduate Research at Vanderbilt

Undergraduate research takes many forms at Vanderbilt. The myriad research opportunities range across the many schools and colleges from the Blair School of Music to the School of Medicine.  With the emergence of Immersion Vanderbilt, undergraduates can pursue research within a supportive framework with access to an Opportunities Database which is generated by our world-class faculty.

Finding Research Opportunities

In addition to courses and course sequences in various departments and programs, students at Vanderbilt often participate in research informally or through independent study or summer work.  Department and laboratory websites,  discussions with academic advisers, with directors of Undergraduate Studies, and with other faculty, attendance at research colloquia and poster sessions, and consultation with returning students may give students insights into avenues they might pursue.

  • The School of Engineering provides guidelines for undergraduates seeking research opportunities in its programs.
  • Through the Blair School of Music, undergraduate students have annual opportunities to audition for the Blair School of Music Student Showcase and for the Vanderbilt University Concerto Competition; the final round of the Concerto Competition is open to the public.
  • At Peabody College, undergraduates frequently work on research projects with faculty through VUSRP projects, research classes, and on a voluntary basis.  The Peabody Library provides an overview of library services for students.
  • The College of Arts and Science describes its academic resources for research opportunities.

Summer Research Programs

Vanderbilt offers a rich array of summer research programs, including:

  • Vanderbilt Undergraduate Summer Research Programs (VUSRP)
  • Systems Biology and Bioengineering Undergraduate Research Experience (SyBBURE)
    • An article on SyBBURE appeared in Vanderbilt Magazine recently.
  • Vanderbilt School of Engineering Summer Research Program
  • Vanderbilt Summer Science Academy (VSSA), targeted at students from other colleges and universities; offers biomedical research opportunities
  • Data Science Institute Summer Research Program, geared toward Vanderbilt students with in interest in doing research in data science. 

Recognition for Undergraduate Research

  • The Vanderbilt Undergraduate Research Fair, which is hosted by the Office of Immersion Resources, welcomes all Vanderbilt undergraduate students to present research in all fields. 
  • The Vanderbilt Writing Studio sponsors an annual Undergraduate Writing Symposium.
  • An array of prizes for undergraduate research are offered each year by departments, programs, and the undergraduate colleges and schools.
summary: Undergraduate Research at Vanderbilt

name: Students with Children Initiative
description:
Students with Children Initiative

The Margaret Cuninggim Women’s Center supports students with children by connecting them  with relevant social, academic and childcare resources during their time at Vanderbilt.  In addition, the Women’s Center leads initiatives on behalf of students with children across the university and works to ensure the needs of each student-parent are met.

Through our office, we hope to help make Vanderbilt University a great place for you and your family. Please feel free to peruse this site but also stop by and visit us at the Women’s Center. We are happy to help you and your family acclimate to both the Vanderbilt and Nashville community.

Please visit our list of general resources for parents for information that may be helpful, and see the student-specific resources below.  To make an appointment contact Bridget Copes or call the Women’s Center at (615) 322-4843.

  • Healthcare Resources for Parenting Students
  • Vanderbilt parental leave policies for faculty, staff, and students
  • List of Breastfeeding Welcoming Spaces at Vanderbilt
  • Vanderbilt Child and Family Center, and their recommended list of alternate childcare facilities in the area
  • Pregnant and Parenting Student’s Rights : FAQ for College and Graduate Students
  • Vanderbilt Office of Student Financial Aid can help parenting students with financial concerns; for assistance, make an appointment with your Financial Aid Advisor or contact Sammie Huffmon.
  • Vanderbilt Office of Housing and Residential Education provides information about on- and off-campus living arrangements.  Visit the website or contact Jason Jakubowski for more information.
  • Vanderbilt Office of International Student and Scholar Services handles the unique needs of international families.
  • Frequently Asked Questions for Students with Children FAQ for SWC
summary: The Students with Children Initiative is a collaboration between the Margaret Cunningim Women's Center and International Student and Scholar Services with support from the Graduate Student Council.

name: Nichols Humanitarian Fund
description:
Nichols Humanitarian Fund

The Nichols Humanitarian Fund was established in 2006 by the E.C. and Lucile Hamby Nichols Trust. The purpose of the fund is to enable Vanderbilt students to volunteer for local, domestic, or international humanitarian service opportunities. Support is primarily focused on summer projects, or projects that take place during defined academic breaks. Funds are available for educational and other expenses.

All currently enrolled Vanderbilt students are eligible to receive assistance from the Fund, provided that they are citizens or permanent residents of the US. Preference will be given to undergraduate students who have not yet completed their baccalaureate degrees.

Students are encouraged to develop their own service opportunities, domestically or internationally. Funding can also be used to participate in established Vanderbilt offerings, including:

  • The Office of Active Citizenship and Service (OACS) summer programs
  • The Vanderbilt Initiative for Scholarship and Global Engagement (VISAGE)
  • Chinese Studies and Service Learning in Beijing
  • Service Learning at the University of Cape Town
  • Summer Public Health in the Dominican Republic

More information can be found here.
summary: The Nichols Humanitarian Fund was established in 2006 by the E.C. and Lucile Hamby Nichols Trust. The purpose of the fund is to enable Vanderbilt students to volunteer for local, domestic, or international humanitarian service opportunities.

name: Vanderbilt Gaming
description:
Welcome to VandyGaming! We are gaming enthusiasts at Vanderbilt University interested in everything to do with video games. Please feel free to join if you are a Vanderbilt student interested in developing your passion for any or all games or are simply interested in joining a community of like-minded gamers! The goals of this club are as follows:

- COMMUNITY

We want to create a tight-knit community of like-minded gamers. Through meeting each other via gaming, VandyGaming members will form valuable connections with others that will provide them with indispensable teammates within the game, and lasting friendships outside the game. We also want to create a space where everyone, regardless of background, can enjoy gaming! 

- COMPETITION

By fostering healthy competition among our members, we hope to enable members to put forth their full effort into playing video games, thereby improving their skill and creating a competitive environment through which they can develop exemplary teamwork and communicative skills.

To add on, VandyGaming maintains a roster of competitive players who play League of Legends, CS2, Overwatch 2, Marvel Rivals, Rocket League, and more, at an exemplary level. This club also exists to support them as they represent Vanderbilt University at the collegiate level.

All players interested in playing any game for our collegiate team, or future applicants hoping to have a spot, can reach out to luke.a.venable@vanderbilt.edu

Thanks for taking the time to visit our page! We hope to see you at some of our events!

Be sure to join our Discord: https://discord.gg/zJ5ZpkTGNw
summary: Vanderbilt Gaming is the official community of all Vanderbilt Gamers!  All undergraduates interested in learning to play, playing with friends, or looking for a competitive environment are welcome to join and attend our events!

name: Center for Medicine, Health & Society
description: The Department of Medicine, Health, and Society at Vanderbilt is an interdisciplinary program that investigates the political, cultural, economic, demographic, and biological factors that impact health. Drawing on a structural competency framework and courses in the medical sciences, humanities, and social sciences, this innovative interdisciplinary program transcends the traditional biomedical approach to understanding health and illness.
summary: Department of Medicine, Health, and Society

name: Robert Penn Warren Center for the Humanities
description:
Our center promotes interdisciplinary research and study in the humanities and social sciences. Because cooperative study in higher education is crucial to the modern university and the society it influences, the Center is designed to intensify and increase interdisciplinary discussion of academic, social, and cultural issues.

The Center began its programs in January 1988 under the sponsorship of the College of Arts and Science (Vanderbilt University). The Center quickly became an intellectual center of activity, providing opportunities for faculty members in different disciplines and with related research and teaching interests to learn and study with each other. Shortly after the Center's inception, the University chose to name the program in honor of one of its most prominent graduates in the arts and humanities, Robert Penn Warren.

In 1989, The Robert Penn Warren Center for the Humanities was awarded a $480,000 Challenge Grant from the National Endowment for the Humanities to assist in establishing a permanent endowment for the program. This award signifies the nationally recognized achievements of the Center and helps to ensure its long-term future.
summary: Our center promotes interdisciplinary research and study in the humanities and social sciences.

name: HEAL VU
description: The mission of HEAL VU is to address issues and current events in health law, health policy, public health, and global health by exploring the social and political aspects of healthcare and doing so in an interdisciplinary approach. This organization and its events are open to students of all ten Vanderbilt schools and colleges. HEAL VU's programming aims to create a community of students that will be prepared to address modern healthcare issues with new, informed, and often-niche perspectives.
summary: The goal of HEAL VU is to host programming regarding interdisciplinary issues and events in health law, health policy, public health, and global health. This organization and its events are open to students of all ten Vanderbilt schools and colleges.

name: Labor and Employment Law Society at Vanderbilt Law School
description: The Labor and Employment Law Society (LELS) seeks to educate and inform its members and the Vanderbilt Law community about L&E law issues and possible career paths at the local, state, federal and international levels.
summary: The Labor and Employment Law Society (LELS) seeks to educate and inform its members and the Vanderbilt Law community about L&E law issues and possible career paths at the local, state, federal and international levels.

name: Front Desk Assistants
summary: Organizational group for the OHARE FDAs

name: Kefi Art Collective
description:
WHAT IS KEFI? 

The term Kefi (or in Greek, κέφι) roughly translates to the spirit of joy, passion, enthusiasm and high spirits that stems from living. As a public art group, our aim is to showcase the joy and beauty of life often overlooked or hidden to most people, by giving them artistic form.

 

MISSION

Our goal is to increase the accessibility and presence of art within the Vanderbilt campus. We unite creative individuals within the Vanderbilt community to extend art’s reach into our everyday lives and provide opportunities for individuals of all artistic backgrounds to develop and explore the joy of artistic creation.

 

WHAT WE DO

Kefi hosts monthly art initiatives such as interactive art installations, DIY craft events, and art seminars. In addition, we host larger events such as student art exhibitions and art forums to showcase student artists on campus. Artists who decide to take part in our exhibitions have the benefit of having supplies and reimbursements covered by Kefi!

 

Follow us on instagram to stay updated!

Ig: @kefiartcollective

 

 

Interested? Become a member now!
summary: Dedicated to the creation of public art within the Vanderbilt campus, Kefi unites creative individuals within the Vanderbilt community to extend education beyond the classroom into the unexpected interactions of everyday life.

name: Women's Basketball
summary: Women's Basketball at Vanderbilt

name: Vanderbilt Club Golf
description:
The Vanderbilt Club Golf program is a coed institution that gives students the opportunity to casually play golf at local courses and competitively through collegiate competitions.  

We hold weekly practices at Nashville based golf courses and provide transportation for those students without access to transportation.  

The team competes through the National Collegial Club Golf Association (NCCGA - https://nextgengolf.org/college-golf/nccga/) in the South Region.
