summary: The Vanderbilt Green Fund is a fund set aside for sustainability projects proposed by students. This year, it will distribute $150,000. Members of SPEAR and VSG established the Green Fund in the spring of 2011.

name: Department of Teaching and Learning - Peabody College
summary: Peabody College's Department of Teaching and Learning offers degree programs at the graduate, professional, and undergraduate levels that will prepare you to make a positive difference in the lives of learners.

name: Partners in Health Engage
description: The mission of Partners in Health Engage is to build the right to health movement by growing and amplifying Partners in Health’s work to advance the right to health globally. We do so through advocacy, fundraising, and education. We meet with congress members to advocate for global health policy, lead major fundraising events for Partners in Health, and work to bring awareness of global health issues to the Vanderbilt and Nashville community.
summary: Partners in Health Engage is a grassroots, global health and social justice organization that focuses on building a social movement to demand that the right to health be protected for all people, everywhere.

name: Studio V
description: Studio V is a health and wellness organization celebrating women at Vandy! We are here to support you to your journey to happy, healthy, and strong. All fitness levels are encouraged to join! We partner with local Nashville Studios so that you can try out different types of workouts, for free! We also host on campus workouts, speakers, socials and more! Membership is open to all who share an interest in wellness and uplifting students who identify as female.
summary: Studio V is a health and wellness organization celebrating women at Vandy! Founded in 2020, this organization fosters a strong fitness community while exploring various workout styles at local studios in Nashville.

name: VUSN Asian Pacific Islander Desi American Student Nurses
summary: This is an affinity group for Asian Pacific Islander Desi American nursing students. Membership is open to all VUSN students!

name: Institute for Medicine and Public Health
description: The mission of the Institute for Medicine and Public Health is to improve personal and public health through discovery, training and service programs aimed to protect against threats to health, promote healthier living, improve the quality of health services, and prepare leaders to advance health and healthcare.
summary: The mission of the Institute for Medicine and Public Health is to improve personal and public health through discovery, training and service programs.

name: Students for Justice in Palestine at Vanderbilt
description:
Purpose

SJP is a secular, student-led human rights advocacy group comprised of students, faculty, and staff of various backgrounds. SJP stands firmly in the belief of a Free Palestine, from the river to the sea, which includes the end of the occupation and a one-state solution based on the self-determination of the Palestinian people. With this, SJP believes in the right of return of all Palestinians. SJP affirms the right of occupied peoples to resist occupation and understands that colonization is an inherently violent framework. It is SJP’s responsibility to stand against community normalization of Zionism and the ongoing occupation and genocide of the Palestinian people. SJP aims to embody these values by raising awareness and educating the Vanderbilt community about not only aggressions, war crimes, and genocide inflicted on the Palestinian people, but the long cultural history of Palestine and the resistance and resilience of the Palestinian people and diaspora. Additionally, these goals will be embodied through advocacy at the campus, city, state, national, and global levels as the organization sees fit. SJP recognizes that systems of oppression throughout the world are deeply intertwined, and that these systems can only be dismantled when the movements against them stand in solidarity with each other. Thus, SJP will strive to aid and stand with other groups on campus and in the community that are committed to raising awareness about systems of injustice that are present both at home and abroad. Moreover, SJP believes that the occupation of Palestine can only end when citizens of conscience from all backgrounds come together to call on those in power to be held accountable. We reject and will not tolerate any form of hatred or discrimination both in our organization and on campus as a whole.
summary: A chapter of Students for Justice in Palestine, SJP is a human rights advocacy group dedicated to fostering a space to spread awareness of the oppression facing the Palestinian people while also standing in solidarity with all marginalized groups.

name: Generation Action for Planned Parenthood
description: Generation Action for Planned Parenthood at Vanderbilt University exists to educate the university community about reproductive health and rights, to translate increased awareness into pro-reproductive freedom activism on campus, and to serve as a coalition partner to state, national, and international reproductive rights efforts. We believe in the fundamental right of every individual to manage their own fertility. We support full access to comprehensive reproductive and complementary health care services in settings that preserve and protect the essential privacy and rights of each individual; advocates public policies that guarantee these rights and ensure access to such services, and supports access to medically accurate educational programs that enhance understanding of human sexuality. As an organization, we are dedicated to amplifying BIPOC voices that have been historically silenced in the traditional reproductive rights movement.
summary: Gen Action strives to raise awareness about reproductive health and rights, educate about sexual health, and mobilize advocates for reproductive freedom in all communities.

name: Autism Tennessee Vanderbilt
description: Autism Tennessee Vanderbilt advocates for and celebrates autistic individuals while also educating students about autism spectrum disorder (ASD) in hopes of creating a more inclusive environment. We are partnered with Autism Tennessee, a local non-profit, and participate in their fundraisers, service events, and awareness efforts.
summary: Autism Tennessee Vanderbilt promotes an interdisciplinary platform to explore and take constructive steps towards supporting individuals on the autism spectrum through fundraising and awareness efforts.

name: American Association of Neurological Surgeons (AANS)
description:
AANS Medical Student Chapters provide early support and leadership training to medical students exploring a career in neurosurgery. The chapters provide a forum for students to organize professional, educational and research activities within their medical schools, promote mentorship and facilitate participation at a national level for the next generation of neurosurgical leaders.

*This organization is only open to current VUSM MD Students*
summary: Founded in 1931 as the Harvey Cushing Society, the American Association of Neurological Surgeons (AANS) is a scientific and educational association with more than 12,000 members worldwide.

name: Iceberg Theatre Company
description:
Throughout its operation, Iceberg will seek to:

-Produce high-quality, original theatrical productions

-Maintain an open culture of collaboration and creativity

-Encourage the personal and artistic growth of all members

-Establish relationships with diverse Vanderbilt student groups, faculty members, academic departments, and staff and to actively involve them in the creative process

-Serve as a bridge between Vanderbilt University and the Nashville community by building collaborative ties with local artists, performers, non-profits, and companies
summary: Our mission is to produce student-written theatre through an open, collaborative creative process.

name: Gold Humanism Honor Society
description: Since its inception in 1875, Vanderbilt University School of Medicine has pushed to create physicians who deliver compassionate and culturally competent care. With the advent of Curriculum 2.0, Vanderbilt has successfully embedded its students in clinical settings earlier than other conventional 4-year medical institutions. By creating this cutting-edge learning environment, Vanderbilt maintains its commitment to promoting medical careers centered upon lifelong learning and patient advocacy. Beyond working with patients at an earlier stage in clinical training, Curriculum 2.0 requires its students meet once weekly in their respective Learning Communities (LC). Learning communities, an emerging trend in medical education, create a sustainable medium for professional and academic development by establishing longitudinal relationships between students and faculty. The Vanderbilt Advisory Colleges Program (VACP) has structured LC to promote an appreciation for the humanities and other factors influencing medical care today, such as care for the uninsured and end-of-life care. The LC mentors lead sessions dedicated to professional development, research and service-based learning. With these sessions and strong mentorship in place, our students have excelled at improving the care delivered to those in Tennessee and beyond. Vanderbilt medical students currently serve over 300 patients in their student-run clinic, Shade Tree, and have transitioned countless others to a new primary care provider after helping them secure health insurance. Vanderbilt medical students also write and publish essays and anthologies, including Tabula Rasa, a journal devoted to medical humanities. Such endeavors have assisted many Vanderbilt students in distinguishing themselves at a national level clinically. For the above-mentioned reasons, it comes as no surprise that we wish to form our own chapter of the Gold Humanism Honor Society (GHHS). Through recognizing the achievements of our students who exemplify the most humanistic attributes, we hope to further a long tradition of leaders devoted to humanism in medicine.
summary: GHHS is focused on recognizing the achievements of our students who exemplify the most humanistic attributes. We hope to further a long tradition of leaders devoted to humanism in medicine.

name: Asian Studies Program
summary: This is the official page for the Asian Studies Program.

name: Synesis
description: Synesis is a journal of Christian thought and expression that strives to be a platform for thoughtful and creative articulation of a Christian worldview.
summary: Synesis is a journal of Christian thought and expression that strives to be a platform for thoughtful and creative articulation of a Christian worldview. We aim to promote dialogue around Christianity and its relevance to all of life and the university.

name: Engineering Ambassador Network
description:
Mission

Our mission is to inspire middle and high school students to pursue a STEM education. By having in-class interactive discussions and hands-on activities at schools around the Nashville area, we educate the students on the importance of STEM careers and the broad impact they have on society. Sharing ideas and inspiring young students to challenge those ideas facilitates both the intellectual development of younger students and the growth of communication and leadership skills in Vanderbilt's EAN members.

About

The Engineering Ambassadors Network is a service based organization, consisting of both graduate and undergraduate students. We primarily work with Nashville based middle and high schools to get students excited about STEM.
summary: Our mission is to inspire middle and high school students to pursue STEM education through interactive discussions and hands-on activities in Nashville-area classrooms. We aim to spark curiosity and confidence by showing how STEM shapes the world.

name: Facilities Department Employee Engagement
description:
Facilities Employee Engagement & Learning

At Facilities, Engagement is an essential part of how we create community! We know everything that happens at Vanderbilt is possible because of our awesome employees and their focused involvement in our purpose and mission.  We owe them our thanks daily!  And, we strive to make them feel engaged, recognized, and appreciated.

For additional department information, please check out our webpage:

https://www.vanderbilt.edu/facilities/

And be sure to check out our socials:

https://www.instagram.com/facilitiesvu?igsh=MWJmdm05Y2l1bno4Zw==

name: Vanderbilt Women in Business
description: Vanderbilt Women in Business (WiB) stands out as an affinity space rooted in the principles of equity, intersectional feminism, and opportunity for those traditionally underestimated in this industry. WiB is the one-stop shop for undergraduate women seeking non-judgemental professional development and leadership training.
summary: Women in Business empowers future female leaders by engaging a network of advocates for businesswomen's ambitions.

name: Vanderbilt Robotics
description:
Vanderbilt Robotics supports and facilitates endeavors in robotics at Vanderbilt University, including organizing participation in collegiate robotics competitions, facilitating an interest in the discipline across the campus, supporting students who pursue robotics as a career or hobby, and contributing to the community through outreach and service. Members of the organization gain experience in applied engineering, teamwork, and leadership extending beyond requisite coursework. 

Join Our Discord! https://discord.gg/5yepSq7QwP
summary: Vanderbilt Robotics supports and facilitates endeavors in robotics, including organizing participation in collegiate robotics competitions, facilitating an interest in the discipline, and supporting students who pursue robotics as a career or hobby.

name: Peabody Council
description:
The mission of the Peabody Council is to take an active role in student government issues for the betterment of the Peabody and the Vanderbilt community, enhance the lives of Peabody students, faculty, and staff alike, and to uphold Peabody's traditions. 

 

These are the different branches of Peabody Council:

- Events & Community Outreach: Engage the Peabody community and spearhead programming efforts

- Bridges: Support minority, first-generation, and low-income students in Peabody       

- Mentoradores: Works on the mentor relationships between first-years and upperclassmen

- Community Service: Support community service within the city of Nashville

 

 

2020-2021 Student Org. Presentation: https://tinyurl.com/y6mxnajo
summary: The mission of the Peabody Council is to take an active role in student government issues for the betterment of the Peabody and the Vanderbilt community, enhance the lives of Peabody students, faculty, and staff alike, and to uphold Peabody's traditions.

name: Vanderbilt Immigration Law Society
description: The Vanderbilt Immigration Law Society seeks to educate the Vanderbilt Law community about immigration issues, to connect students with attorneys working in the immigration field, and to aid the local community with time, knowledge, and compassion.
summary: The Vanderbilt Immigration Law Society seeks to educate the Vanderbilt Law community about immigration issues, to connect students with attorneys working in the immigration field, and to aid the local community with time, knowledge, and compassion.

name: Owen Outdoor Adventure Club
description:
The Owen Outdoor Adventure Club (OOAC) provides students, faculty, and family with a structured means for outdoor exploration, fitness, and fun. The OOAC aims not only to be an active outlet for outdoor activities and regional exploration, but also to serve as a means for leadership development, relationship building, and education for a student body that is truly global.

Members, regardless of prior outdoor experience, will discover the outdoor areas immediately surrounding Nashville and will get the opportunity to explore far beyond it as a part of larger “Anchor Adventures.”
summary: The Owen Outdoor Adventure Club (OOAC) provides Owen students, faculty, and family with a structured means for outdoor exploration, fitness, and fun.

name: Vanderbilt Biostatistics Graduate Student Association
summary: We seek to facilitate an environment for students to engage in discussions regarding biostatistics and data science concepts in order to further their knowledge and understanding of the current state of the field.

name: Vanderbilt QuestBridge Chapter
description: The Vanderbilt QuestBridge Chapter is part of the national QuestBridge organization. QuestBridge is a powerful platform bridging the nation's brightest, under-served youth and leading institutions of higher education and further opportunities. QuestBridge envisions a world where America's leadership encompasses thoughtful and broad-minded decision-makers drawn from every economic segment of society. As the Vanderbilt QuestBridge Chapter, we aim to create a community for QuestBridge Scholars, and other students interested in bridging the gap between socioeconomic class and education, and engage in our Vanderbilt community to talk about these issues.
summary: The Vanderbilt QuestBridge Chapter is part of the national QuestBridge organization. QuestBridge is a powerful platform bridging the nation's brightest, under-served youth and leading institutions of higher education and further opportunities.

name: Vanderbilt AMA-TMA - Medical School Chapter
description: The Vanderbilt University Chapter of the American Medical Association and Tennessee Medical Association (VU AMA-TMA) serves as the formal connection between Vanderbilt University and the American Medical Association, Tennessee Medical Association, and Nashville Academy of Medicine, with the goal of creating opportunities for VUSM students to become more engaged in political advocacy at the local, state, and national levels. We host events on campus that foster VUSM student involvement in politics (such as lunches with physician legislators and panels about hot topics in health policy), and we also organize VUSM student involvement in advocacy events off-campus (such as TMA Day on the Hill, Nashville Academy of Medicine legislative receptions, and MedTenn-TMA's annual conference).
summary: The medical school chapter of Vanderbilt AMA-TMA connects medical students to the AMA, TMA, and Nashville Academy of Medicine, creating opportunities for VUSM students to become more engaged in political advocacy at the local, state, and national levels.

name: ANCHOR LINK
description:
Anchor Link is Vanderbilt’s student involvement platform that helps students become connected on campus and manage their experiences outside the classroom. Anchor Link serves as the central calendar for student life and has groups for every registered student organization and residence hall on campus, as well as a variety of university departments, programs, and resources.

 

Anchor Link has robust functionality for managing student organizations including recruiting new members, publicizing events and news, tracking attendance, maintaining organization records, conducting elections or polls, and communicating with members.

 

The public Anchor Link site is accessible to anyone, including those outside of Vanderbilt. However, every student, faculty, and staff member may log in with their VUnet ID and password for a more robust campus user view as well as customized content for each individual user based on their involvement and interests.
summary: Anchor Link is Vanderbilt's online student involvement platform that helps students get involved on campus and manage their experiences outside the classroom.

organizationType.name: Student Affairs Department, Office, or Program

name: POCUS Student Interest Group
description: The POCUS student interest group exists to provide directed training, resources for independent study, and opportunities for leadership in interdisciplinary point-of-care ultrasound in a manner accessible to undergraduate medical trainees of all levels.
summary: Point of Care Ultrasound Group

name: Literature, Arts, and Medicine at VUSM
description:
LAM is a student-run organization at the Vanderbilt University School of Medicine dedicated to promoting and celebrating art in all forms, to foster community around creative expression at VUSM, and to provide a forum by which students can cultivate, practice, and showcase their artistic talents.

 

Arts Showcase

The Vanderbilt University School of Medicine’s Arts Showcase is a bimonthly symposium featuring the talents of medical students, house staff, faculty, and other members of the School of Medicine and Medical Center communities. The performances take place monthly from noon to 1:00 p.m. in the EBL boardroom. Past events have featured instrumental and vocal performances, poetry recitations, essay or short story readings, displays of visual arts, and much more. Food and beverages are provided for attendees.

 

Stay Informed

If you would like to receive email notifications of future Cultural Series performances and other Literature, Arts, and Medicine events, subscribe to our mailing list.

 

Perform!

Have a talent that you would like to share with our community? Sign up to perform at an upcoming Cultural Series.

 

Submit to us!

Have a creative work that you’re particularly proud of? We invite you to submit it for inclusion in Tabula Rasa, an anthology featuring contributions in writing and visual arts from members of the Vandy Med community.

To submit a piece to be considered for publication in the upcoming volume of Tabula Rasa and Post Call, email it as an attachment to postcallanthology@gmail.com. All submissions should meet the following guidelines:

•Written submissions should be typed and double-spaced.
•For fiction and non-fiction, please limit submissions to 7,500 words. Excerpts of longer works are welcome, if self-contained.
•For poetry, there is no line limit.
•For artwork and photography, we accept pieces in either color or black-and-white. We recommend submitting works in .tif format, but will accept submissions as .jpg, .gif, .png, or .pdf files as well.
•On a separate page from your submission, submit a one-paragraph biography (approx. 100-125 words will suffice) written in third person. Include your full name, current degree program and year (for students), and professional title and department at VU/VUMC (for faculty/staff).
summary: Medical School Organization

name: Vanderbilt Ballet Theatre
description: Vanderbilt Ballet Theatre is Vanderbilt's premier ballet company. We aim to diversify the Vanderbilt Performing Arts Community by providing access to high-level classical ballet training and performance in a fun, positive environment. Members have the opportunity to perfect ballet technique, choreograph, and dance with a variety of commitment levels to our organization. We strive to offer dancers quality performance opportunities and expose ballet's magical art form to all Vanderbilt community members.
summary: Vanderbilt Ballet Theatre is Vanderbilt's premier ballet-focused performing group.

name: International Student Association-Divinity and Graduate School
description: The main purpose of this organization will be to foster a community for International Students at Vanderbilt University Divinity School and The Graduate Department of Religion. This organization will represent visibly and be a voice for the International Students. The organization will also work closely with the admissions office at VDS and GDR every year, to be in touch with incoming International Students so that they may be helped to settle in Nashville. Other tasks will include but are not limited to helping International Students with questions on International Student and Scholar Office and services, how to get in touch with the ISSS, how to receive help in visa related issues. We will also create a platform for students to showcase their cultures, and learn from & with one another across cultures.
summary: The main purpose of this organization will be to foster a community, visibly represent, and be a voice for the International Students at Vanderbilt University Divinity School and The Graduate Department of Religion.

name: American Academy of Environmental Engineers and Scientists
description:
About

The American Academy of Environmental Engineers and Scientists® is a not-for-profit 501(c)(6) organization serving the Environmental Engineering and Environmental Science professions by providing Board Certification to those who qualify through experience and testing. The Academy also provides training through workshops and seminars, participates in accrediting universities, publishes a periodical and other reference material, interacts with students and young professionals, sponsors a university lecture series, and rewards outstanding achievements through its international awards program.

Vision

Leadership and excellence in environmental engineering and science

Mission

Protecting public health and the environment by:

  • Recognizing leadership and excellence through Board Certification of Environmental Engineers and Scientists.
  • Providing professional development opportunities for students, engineers, and scientists.
summary: This organization is in the process of registering.

name: OHARE Summer Housing - Highland Quad
description:
This page serves Summer Housing within OHARE. Summer housing residents are granted permission to remain on campus by OHARE. Summer is staffed by two Area Coordinators, a Graduate Area Coordinator, and a team of Summer Resident Advisers. Residents will have the opportunity to attend virtual summer programs and events, receive support resources, and connect with OHARE staff through one on one meetings. Residents will be assigned to an RA who is responsible for building community through Meaningful Conversations and community-wide programs.

 

Each summer, the location of Summer Housing will vary depending on demand and significant facilities projects. Summer Housing 2021 is located in HIGHLAND QUAD!!! All residents are assigned to spaces with private kitchens and bathrooms and have access to our staff through various means. Follow us online @VUOHARE on Twitter, Facebook, & Instagram!
summary: Summer Housing is offered each summer to residents enrolled in classes. Each summer OHARE provides programs, support, and referral resources to students who live in our residential communities over the summer.

name: Experience Vanderbilt
description:
Experience Vanderbilt’s goal is to break down monetary barriers between undergraduate students and extracurricular programs that define the Vanderbilt experience. These programs may include participation in student organizations, club sports, art and cultural experiences, service trips, outdoor recreation trips, Greek Life or other initiatives with financial considerations. We also award a ticket pass that students can use to purchase tickets to on campus events from the Sarratt box office. EV is funded by donations from alumni, faculty/staff members, and current students. 

The funding application for the 2025-26 academic year will be open for the month of April, and will open again on a rolling basis in the fall of 2025. The funding application for Freshmen and Transfer students will open in the fall of 2025.
summary: Experience Vanderbilt (EV) supports the University’s mission of creating a financially inclusive community by providing undergraduate students who qualify for need-based financial aid with resources to engage in extracurricular programs.

name: Vanderbilt Athletics
summary: Vanderbilt Athletics is committed to creating a fun and exciting atmosphere for students and fans. We encourage fans to be engaged and support our student-athletes, all while showing their Vanderbilt pride! Anchor Down!

name: iLEAD
description: International Orientation Leaders (IOLs) are current international and US students who are selected based on their experience and enthusiasm to build a welcoming environment to Vanderbilt for all incoming undergraduate international students.
summary: International Orientation Leaders (IOLs) are current international and US students who are selected based on their experience and enthusiasm to build a welcoming environment to Vanderbilt for all incoming undergraduate international students.

name: Social Venture Club
description:
The Social Venture Club is the leadership arm of Vanderbilt's Center for Social Ventures (CSV) an interdisciplinary, student-led organization committed to developing socially and environmentally conscious leaders and doers. We prepare our students to drive systemic, impactful change through market-driven forces and social entrepreneurship.

While any Vanderbilt student is welcome to engage with our programming, students have more opportunities to engage with social impact and social enterprise through Social Venture Club membership.

Signing up for Anchor Link membership ensures:

  • You receive Club event invitations to your email as soon as they are published on Anchor Link. This is particularly helpful for events with limited RSVP spots!
  • Your Anchor Link homepage is up-to-date with Club events. 

 

social entrepreneurship | social impact | design thinking | corporate social responsibility | impact investing | triple bottom line | social finance | microfinance | social innovation
summary: The Social Venture Club is an organization committed to developing socially and environmentally conscious leaders and doers at Vanderbilt University through student-led and interdisciplinary programming.

name: Vanderbilt Young Americans for Freedom
description:
Vanderbilt Young Americans for Freedom, like all other YAF chapters, holds to the principles of the Sharon Statement, adopted on September 11, 1960 in Sharon, Connecticut at the childhood home of conservative icon William F. Buckley, Jr.

We, as young conservatives, believe:

That foremost among the transcendent values is the individual's use of his God-given free will, whence derives his right to be free from the restrictions of arbitrary force;

That liberty is indivisible, and that political freedom cannot long exist without economic freedom;

That the purpose of government is to protect those freedoms through the preservation of internal order, the provision of national defense, and the administration of justice;

That when government ventures beyond these rightful functions, it accumulates power, which tends to diminish order and liberty;

That the Constitution of the United States is the best arrangement yet devised for empowering government to fulfill its proper role, while restraining it from the concentration and abuse of power;

That the genius of the Constitution- the division of powers- is summed up in the clause that reserves primacy to the several states, or to the people, in those spheres not specifically delegated to the Federal government;

That the market economy, allocating resources by the free play of supply and demand, is the single economic system compatible with the requirements of personal freedom and constitutional government, and that it is at the same time the most productive supplier of human needs;

That when government interferes with the work of the market economy, it tends to reduce the moral and physical strength of the nation; that when it takes from one man to bestow on another, it diminishes the incentive of the first, the integrity of the second, and the moral autonomy of both;

That we will be free only so long as the national sovereignty of the United States is secure; that history shows periods of freedom are rare, and can exist only when free citizens concertedly defend their rights against all enemies;

That the forces of international Communism are, at present, the greatest single threat to these liberties;

That the United States should stress victory over, rather than coexistence with, this menace; and

That American foreign policy must be judged by this criterion: does it serve the just interests of the United States?

To conduct educational activities and provide conservative leadership to bring about the objectives outlined in the Sharon Statement.

To assist students in their efforts to understand and promote conservatism on campus and in the surrounding community.
summary: Young Americans for Freedom, a project of Young America's Foundation, is an ideological organization purposed to promote individual freedom, free enterprise, a strong national defense, and traditional American values at campuses nationwide.

name: Office of Chancellor
summary: Events hosted by the Office of the Chancellor

name: The Innovation Center
summary: The Innovation Center is the epicenter for creativity, collaboration, invention, and entrepreneurship at Vanderbilt University.

name: HR Diversity and Inclusion
description: The HR Diversity and Inclusion Committee aspires to create an inclusive work environment within Vanderbilt Human Resources through information, education, and dialogue. We value, respect and support all ideas that will enable the HR staff to bring their best selves to work.  We will use these efforts to model inclusive excellence in our work interactions with the Vanderbilt community.
summary: The HR Diversity and Inclusion Committee

name: Vanderbilt Law Tennis Club
description:
This is a tennis club aimed at promoting the sport of tennis to current law students and law faculty, whom share the common interest in the sport of tennis. 

Mission:

1) To provide a group for law students and law faculty to learn, compete, and enjoy the game of tennis, and 2) to organize and encourage member participation in local racquet sport clubs and tournaments, including university wide racquet sport intramurals and club tournaments.
summary: This is a tennis club aimed at promoting the sport of tennis to current law students and law faculty, whom share the comment interest in the sport of tennis as well as affiliate racquet sports, such as table tennis, squash, and racquetball, etc.

name: Vanderbilt Hellenic Students' Association
description:
What's up Vandy! WELCOME TO GREEK CLUB!!!!! The Vanderbilt Hellenic* Students' Association (Greek Club), is back and here to stay. As part of our culture, we strive for Greek Club to be a space that not only connects us by our heritage but our love of strangers and friends. We strive for a space welcome to all those who enjoy Greek culture, the mediterranean lifestyle, food, festivals, dancing, and the occasional breaking of a plate, so come hang out with us! Show up to a GBM or hop on the list-serv to be kept in the loop about all things Greek club that you can show up to outside a GBM -  volunteering, Greek dancing, grabbing a gyro. 

The mission of the Vanderbilt Hellenic Students' Association (VHSA) is to foster community, thought, and connection amongst Commodores of Hellenic descent and all members of the Vanderbilt community interested in Hellenic culture, language, and history.

*Hellenic: the original word for a person of Greek origin is actually "Hellene," which means child of the sun.
summary: The mission of the Vanderbilt Hellenic Students' Association (VHSA) is to foster community, thought, and connection amongst Commodores of Hellenic descent and all members of the Vanderbilt community interested in Hellenic culture, language, and history.

name: Commodore Debating Society
description: The Commodore Debate Society (formerly Vanderbilt Parliamentary Debate Club) is an organization dedicated to political and philosophical discourse. Our meetings act as a forum to respectfully and tactfully debate relevant issues facing the United States. We are open to everyone; ideological diversity is encouraged. Students across the political spectrum are welcome to join and engage alternative perspectives.
summary: The Commodore Debate Society is a student-run political & philosophical debate organization at Vanderbilt University!

name: Neuroscience Student Organization (Grad Org)
description:
The purpose of the Neuroscience Student Organization is to improve the quality of life of the neuroscience graduate students and advocate on their behalf. The NSO is also designed to act as a liaison between faculty, administration, and students.

The Neuroscience Student Organization (NSO) has existed at Vanderbilt University for almost 15 years, and the role of this organization continues to evolve. The organization is comprised of a group of neuroscience graduate students in the Vanderbilt Brain Institute. The organization supports a wide array of aspects of neuroscience graduate student life, including supporting studying, providing social events, promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion, and advocating to faculty on behalf of the students.
summary: The purpose of the Neuroscience Student Organization is to improve the quality of life of the neuroscience graduate students and advocate on their behalf. The NSO is also designed to act as a liaison between faculty, administration, and students.

name: Graduate Diversity and Inclusion Committee
summary: The Graduate Diversity and Inclusion Committee, affiliated with the Graduate Student Council, represents and advocates for the interests of graduate students on issues related to diversity, inclusion, and justice.

name: Office for Equity, Diversity and Inclusion
description:
The Vice Chancellor for Equity, Diversity and Inclusion will serve as an advisor in matters of diversity and inclusion for the Chancellor. The Vice Chancellor for EDI will ensure that our seminal efforts are promulgated to thought leaders in our city, region, nation and the world.

In addition, the Vice Chancellor for EDI’s focus will be on activities that advance diversity and inclusion tools and best practices with all Vice Chancellors and Vanderbilt University staff and in addition, serve when requested, as a supportive counsel to the Vice Provost of Strategic Initiatives/Office of Inclusive Excellence.

In building and codifying these and other activities, the office of the Vice Chancellor for Equity, Diversity and Inclusion will assist the Chancellor and the Vice Chancellors in making Vanderbilt University a national leader in developing tools and practices that ensure a welcoming, nurturing and robustly inclusive community for all who study, learn, teach, research and serve here.
summary: The Office for Equity, Diversity and Inclusion fosters institutional equity, diversity and inclusion by creating educational opportunities, raising awareness and providing strategies & tools to bring to life the vision of inclusive excellence on campus.

name: VMS Knits for (K)Nashville
description: Our mission is to provide an artistic outlet for medical students through the creation of various yarnwork projects, with the ultimate goal of promoting wellness and collaboration. Secondarily, we aim to serve the community by donating these projects to different patient populations in Nashville.
summary: Our mission is to provide an artistic outlet for medical students through the creation of various yarnwork projects, with the ultimate goal of promoting wellness and collaboration...

name: International Student Council
description: Vanderbilt University International Student Council (ISC) is a non-political and non-profit student organization. The goal of ISC is to unite all the undergraduate and graduate international students on campus, and acts as a programming platform to promote cultural diversity to the Vanderbilt and Nashville communities. ISC dedicates to promote mutual cultural and intellectual sharing among international students and American students, to promote the welfare of international students, and to work as a liaison between ISSS and international students.
summary: The International Student Council (ISC)  is  an organization dedicated to uniting all the undergraduate and graduate international students on campus and promoting cultural diversity to the Vanderbilt and Nashville communities.

name: Vanderbilt Alzheimer's Buddies
description:
Alzheimer’s disease is a terminal neurodegenerative disease that impairs memory, motor function, and emotional response. An estimated 5.4 million Americans are currently afflicted with this disease, and their numbers are growing rapidly.

 

Alzheimer’s Buddies is a response to the profound isolation and social disengagement experienced by people in the intermediate-to-late-stages of Alzheimer's disease. Student volunteers are paired with nursing home residents with Alzheimer's (the volunteer’s “buddy”) in order to form meaningful relationships over the course of a semester or longer. Our initiative encourages students to take the mindset of a friend rather than of a care provider, to learn about their patients' past and passions, to establish real friendships. This interaction not only provides patients with a unique outlet, but also provides students with a formative clinical experience and formal training on this devastating disease.


When you volunteer with Alzheimer’s Buddies, you join an intergenerational response against the social isolation imposed by Alzheimer’s Disease. This Alzheimer’s Buddies chapter not only offers volunteers the opportunity to make a meaningful difference in the lives of Alzheimer’s residents and their families, but also to develop a stronger understanding of their personal identity, build new social skills, and makes new friends.
summary: To empower students and cognitively impaired individuals to break through the social and interpersonal boundaries imposed by Alzheimer's disease.

name: TAMID Group at Vanderbilt
description: The mission of TAMID Group at Vanderbilt is to expand the network and professional skills of all members through interactions with the Israeli economy. TAMID Group unites students of like interests and provides them with exceptional consulting and investing experience that will well prepare them for any future professional endeavors.  We aim to develop the next generation of entrepreneurs and professionals through a customized education program and to promote strong and effective leadership skills within each of our members.
summary: The mission of TAMID Group at Vanderbilt is to expand the network and professional skills of all members through interactions with the Israeli economy. We unite students of like interests and provide them with consulting and investing experiences.

description:

1) To provide survivors of brain injuries/illnesses/disorders with support, additional resources, and opportunities to connect with and learn from one another.

2) To provide students with an opportunity to serve as leaders within the local community and learn about the patient perspective of living with a brain injury.

3) To collaborate with the Nashville community in supporting survivors of brain injury and spread  awareness.

name: Vanderbilt Science Policy Group
description:
Vanderbilt Science Policy Group (VSPG) is a student organization that aims to educate its members on scientific policies and the process of policy-making at all levels of the federal and local government as well as to empower members to become advocates for their chosen scientific interests.

The mission of the VSPG will be to:
a. To create an environment for students interested in learning about scientific policies and the process of policy-making.
b. To enhance the ability of its members to advocate effectively for scientific issues. The group will work to promote written and oral communication between scientists and their elected officials as well as with the general public.
c. To support career development for members interested in pursuing careers in scientific policy
summary:
The mission of the VSPG will be to: 
a. To create an environment for students interested in learning about scientific policies and the process of policy making.

summary: IBA is an action-oriented group with a goal of raising awareness of inequities in the biological sciences and promoting inclusion of all traditionally underrepresented groups in the field.

The TOM: Vanderbilt mission is to help as many individuals in the Nashville area as possible by bringing together people with disabilities (“Need Knowers”) and innovative thinkers (“Makers”) to develop solutions on a personal and direct level.

name: Vandy Ballroom
description:
Vandy Ballroom aims to provide students with an opportunity to learn the joys and benefits of ballroom dancing in a safe, inclusive and affordable environment through the mentorship of professional coaches. Students will learn to dance figures and technique socially with the opportunity to venture into competitive level dancing with familiarity and improved proficiency in the various dance styles.
Vandy Ballroom incorporates a variety of dance styles including Standard/Smooth, Latin/Rhythm, and Social Dance. An exceptional strength of our organization is that we are partnered with community professionals who will mentor and train members in social dance and competitive dancesport categories. In addition to engaging in social dances, our members will have the opportunity to compete in collegiate dancesport competitions.

Join us on GroupMe! https://groupme.com/join_group/47019840/a0xyDDXD
summary: Vandy Ballroom is a student organization is dedicated to teaching students to ballroom dance, as well as providing a space for social dancing, competing, and performing.

name: Batson College
description:
Dean Batson was a decorated WWII veteran and nationally distinguished pediatrician who played a key role in the eradication of polio. During his tenure as Dean, he oversaw a tremendous period of growth for the school and medical center; notably, the creation of Light Hall, the ‘Round Wing’ and the Children’s Hospital.

---

Faculty Mentors: Dr. Maya Neeley and Dr. Calvin Gruss

Lead SAAs (2025-2026): Sarah Dulaney and Namrah Ajmal

name: Chapman College
description:
Chapman College - VUSM

Dr. John E. Chapman is known in Vanderbilt history as the “Dean of Deans,” conferring 3,317 graduate degrees, creating 32 acres of clinical research and education space, and establishing 789 new faculty positions during his extended tenure as the school’s commander-in-chief. He was adored by students and faculty alike, bringing satisfaction ratings to the top of the nation.

---

Faculty Mentors: Dr. Acosta and Dr. Ibrahim

Lead SAAs (2025-2026): Preethy Abraham and James Bathon

name: Gabbe College
description:
Dr. Steven Gabbe is remembered as the rousing force behind Vanderbilt’s emergence as an eminent academic medical center in patient care, education and research in the 21st century. Dean Gabbe led tremendous projects to upgrade and modernize Vanderbilt’s facilities which set the standard for other schools. Through his leadership, he assembled a student body and faculty full of heart, energy, and commitment to service.

---

Faculty Mentors: Dr. Plummer & Dr. Beaulieu

Lead SAAs (2025-2026): Tori Tong & Josh Betts

name: Robinson College
description:
Dr. George Canby Robinson was dean of the medical school from 1920-1928. He moved the school from South Nashville to the main university campus and is credited with transforming its traditional physician-apprentice educational program into that of a modern university medical school with a science-based curriculum and full-time faculty.

Robinson College is known for its excellence in all pursuits, from academics to athletics.

The raven at the center of the Robinson crest is a heraldic symbol of independence, ambition, and enduring constancy. The acorn and oak leaves are a symbol of Vanderbilt University, and the sword represents the just pursuit of honor in medicine and all things.

Robinson College is lucky to have two fantastic mentors, Dr. Shannon Lester and Dr. Ryan Fritz, leading the flock. We also have twelve Faculty Affiliate Advisors. More information about all of these faculty advisors can be found on the college website.
summary: Robinson College: The Ravens

We will also hold events to benefit and bring visibility to Veterans in the Nashville and Vanderbilt communities.

name: oVRcome
description:
oVRcome is an organization that seeks to provide hospital patients with the opportunity to experience virtual reality as a source of entertainment, distraction, and stress relief. 

Due to certain procedures, many patients must remain in hospitals for extended periods of time, often with reduced mobility. As a result, these patients frequently face boredom in addition to the typical anxiety that accompanies hospital visits.

 

To address these issues, oVRcome enlists the help of student volunteers, who visit hospitals and provide virtual reality experiences to patients. In addition to being an exciting source of entertainment, VR has been demonstrated to reduce stress, pain, and anxiety for patients.
summary: oVRcome is an organization that seeks to provide hospital patients with the opportunity to experience virtual reality as a source of entertainment, distraction, and stress relief.

description:
Scientific Immersion and Mentorship (SIM) provides students with the resources to prepare for and excel in careers in a variety of research disciplines. We expose students to the research opportunities available to them, both on campus and beyond, provide students with opportunities to explore career paths in various research disciplines to determine if it's the right fit for them, and equip students with the right experiences and mentors to make them competitive candidates for the next step in their path towards a career in research. 

Website: https://studentorg.vanderbilt.edu/sim/
summary: Scientific Immersion and Mentorship's (SIM) mission is to transform Vanderbilt's students into future leaders in a variety of research disciplines.

The Association’s goals are to encourage the participation of marginalized identities in the geosciences; to exchange educational, technical, and professional information; and to enhance the professional growth and advancement of diversity in the geosciences.
summary: The mission of the Association for Women Geoscientists is to attain equality of opportunity for women in the geosciences and train new leaders by funding high impact programs for women in the geosciences. The Association’s goals are ...

name: FirstVU
description:
FirstVU is a student-run organization dedicated to holistically supporting, celebrating, and empowering first-generation and/or low-income (FGLI) students at Vanderbilt University. Led by Peer Advocates, who are themselves first-generation students and serve as the organization’s executive board, FirstVU fosters a strong sense of belonging and advocates for equitable access to campus resources.

FirstVU aims to address the systemic challenges that impact FGLI students’ success through mentorship, academic and career guidance, and professional development. Our mission is to build a supportive and inclusive community where FGLI students are empowered to persist, thrive, and lead. FirstVU Peer Advocates connect students with resources in key areas, including:

  • Admissions
  • Student Life
  • Academics
  • Faculty and Instructors
  • Career Guidance

Stay connected with us:
Group Me
summary: A student-run organization to holistically support, celebrate, and empower first-generation students at Vanderbilt University. FirstVU is led by Peer Advocates who are also first-generation students and serve as the executive board.

name: Special Olympics Vanderbilt
description: Special Olympics Vanderbilt is a club on campus that brings together Vanderbilt students and individuals with intellectual disabilities through sport to build friendships and help lead the social justice movement of Special Olympics. During Fall 2025, this club will consist of weekly interactive in-person sessions (practices) between Vanderbilt student volunteers (Unified Partners) and individuals with intellectual disabilities (Athletes). During practice, Unified Partners and Athletes will engage in teamwork, camaraderie and friendship while improving their basketball abilities!
summary: Special Olympics Vanderbilt is a club on campus that brings together Vanderbilt students and individuals with intellectual disabilities through sport to build friendships and help lead the social justice movement of Special Olympics.

name: the Wond’ry
description: The Wond’ry is the epicenter for Innovation and Entrepreneurship at Vanderbilt University. Its three story, 13,000-square foot space, located adjacent to the new state-of-the-art Engineering and Science Building, facilitates transinstitutional collaboration between students and faculty from all schools, levels, and disciplines. From engineers to scientists to entrepreneurs to artists, the Wond’ry is the primary point of connection for students interested in innovation and entrepreneurship. The Wond’ry is the catalyst for building and strengthening creativity across campus, fostering innovation in all fields, and forging  a stronger “maker” culture at Vanderbilt University.
summary: The Wond’ry is the epicenter for Innovation and Entrepreneurship at Vanderbilt University.

name: First in Business
description: First in Business is a student-led organization dedicated to empowering first-generation and low-income (FGLI) students to explore, pursue, and thrive in business careers. Our mission is to demystify the business world, close access gaps, and build a supportive community where members can gain the confidence, skills, and networks they need to succeed. Through professional development workshops, speaker events, mentorship, and hands-on experiences, we aim to ensure that being “first” is not a limitation, but a powerful foundation for leadership and impact in business.
summary: First in Business is a student-led organization dedicated to empowering first-generation and low-income (FGLI) students to explore, pursue, and thrive in business careers.

summary: To prepare Vanderbilt Students to be successful in the business world, during and beyond college, as well as to provide a networking system within and outside of the Vanderbilt community.

name: Vanderbilt Blockchain
description:
Vanderbilt Blockchain aims to better inform the Vanderbilt population about blockchain technologies and cryptocurrencies while providing hands-on learning opportunities to get involved with live projects. Our monthly speaker series is a great way to get started and is open to everyone. We have a research committee where students can learn both fundamental and technical analysis skills and help create the Vanderbilt Blockchain Token Index. We also run a development committee where cohorts of students can learn blockchain-related programming languages, join hackathons, and find jobs. Finally, our new content committee is focused on building our online presence and sharing our research with the world.

 

We have a near-100% job placement rate, and we can help anyone serious about finding a job in the blockchain industry get connected to the right people. We've collaborated with the Ethereum Foundation, the Blockchain Acceleration Foundation, BTC Media, Ripple Labs, Aave Companies, and several other notable names to bring our members the best educational tools and resources to help them dive deep into Web3.

 

We also founded Anchor DAO, the first 501c3 nonprofit DAO created in the state of Tennessee, to expand our mission of blockchain adoption and awareness.
summary: Vanderbilt Blockchain is a multi-disciplinary organization bridging the gap between academia and the blockchain industry. We host an educational cohort and place individuals in internships and full-time roles throughout the industry.

name: Office of the Provost
summary: Office of the Provost

name: Vanderbilt University School of Medicine Office for Diversity Affairs
description: The Office for Diversity Affairs was founded in 2002 to increase the diversity of the medical school learning environment to ensure that Vanderbilt University School of Medicine became a center of excellence through the diversity of its student body, faculty, and leadership.  We believe the best environment for learning is a diverse one and that diversity is broad and far-reaching.  We aim to support students from varied backgrounds with regard to gender, race, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, socioeconomic background, and geographic origin.
summary: The Office for Diversity Affairs was founded to increase the diversity of the medical school learning environment to ensure that VUSM became a center of excellence through the diversity of its student body, faculty, & leadership.

name: Latino and Latina Studies Program
description:
https://as.vanderbilt.edu/latinolatinastudies/

Latino and Latina Studies focuses on cultural production and political and socioeconomic experiences of people inculcated with the U.S. experience, self-identifying as Latinos and Latinas and communicating primarily in English and sometimes in Spanish.  This program of study is designed to accommodate a range of voices and multiple manifestations of cultural identity and expression in historical and contemporary contexts to fill in this vital but often overlooked component of our national identity and discourse.
summary: Latino and Latina Studies focuses on cultural production and political and socioeconomic experiences of people inculcated with the US experience, self-identifying as Latinos and Latinas and communicating primarily in English and sometimes in Spanish.

name: Ingram Scholarship Program
description:
The Ingram Scholarship Program sponsors students who demonstrate a willingness and ability to combine a successful business or professional career with a lifelong commitment to finding solutions to critical societal problems. It is distinguished by the requirement that the recipient not only volunteer for community service but also design and implement projects that address significant community needs. The program encourages Ingram Scholars to work with established not-for-profit and business groups and to create projects that become self-sustaining.

The career goals of Ingram Scholars will represent the full spectrum of business and business-related possibilities, including the professional fields. Upon graduation, these students will become leaders in informed and creative ways. Mentoring undergraduate students, assisting them with summer internships or job placement, and involving others in vital community service are all ways that Ingram Scholars will continue the work they began at Vanderbilt University.

Ultimately, the program seeks to develop an active network of Ingram Scholar alumni who not only continue their commitment to positive social change, but also nurture the social sensibilities of others in their volunteer and professional lives.

The Ingram Scholarship Program is a unique and innovative example of how a university can prepare students for responsible careers as well as a lifetime of useful contributions to the well-being of others. If you are committed to positive social change, and if you possess the qualities of maturity, leadership, and initiative, you should consider this challenging and rewarding program.
summary: Scholars in Service to the Community

Dissertation Defenses

Please email bioseminar@vanderbilt.edu with event details. You may also include a flyer which can be uploaded as well.

name: Vanderbilt Climbing Club
description:
The mission of the Vanderbilt Climbing Club is to create an enjoyable, supportive, and ultimately a competitive collegiate climbing community. The club supports members who are first-time climbers all the way to national and international competition climbers. We welcome everyone on campus to join and participate. Coached team climbing sessions take place weekly on Tuesdays and Thursdays 7-9 pm at Climb Nashville West/Kraft with rides provided. Outdoor climbing trips are also organized throughout the season. Anyone interested in climbing is encouraged to join in whatever capacity they can! Team members enjoy membership to Climb Nashville, a group of dedicated teammates, access to club sports, and the ability to improve their climbing skills at any level by joining the Vanderbilt Climbing Club!

Please check out our Instagram page for news, events and send footage @vanderbiltclimbing

If you are interested in linking up with our climbing community, please join our GroupMe! https://groupme.com/join_group/69360972/m84oRm8r
summary: The mission of the Vanderbilt Climbing Club is to grow the Vanderbilt climbing community. The club supports members who are first-time climbers all the way to national and international competition climbers, with a focus on community bonding.

Community engagement, in the form of town halls, expos, pop-up space projects, etc., is integral to the implementation and success of FutureVU.
