summary: To promote animal welfare and conservation in the contexts of zoos, entertainment industries, research facilities, and the wild (e.g. anti-poaching, habitat preservation) via awareness campaigns, educational trips, and volunteering

name: Honor Council at Vanderbilt School of Medicine
summary: The Honor Council in the School of Medicine serves to investigate reported violations of the Honor Code as well as remind students and faculty of the importance of adhering to the code.

name: The Provost's Office for Inclusive Excellence
description: The Provost's Office for Inclusive Excellence supports all faculty and students, both undergraduate and graduate, to ensure that they excel. The office creates and supports workshops, programming, and events, as well as offering information, providing resources, and advising best practices for strengthening the University climate.
summary: The Provost's Office for Inclusive Excellence supports all faculty and students, both undergraduate and graduate, to ensure that they excel.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
description:
Hello! Welcome to VSA!

The Vietnamese Student Association at Vanderbilt University strives to spread the understanding and admiration of what constitutes a resilient Vietnamese identity — our culture, our history, and our peoples.

Vanderbilt VSA is one of many affiliated universities under the Union of Vietnamese Student Associations of the Southeast (UVSASE), a non-profit, non-partisan, community-based organization with a commitment to unify Vietnamese youth organizations in Alabama, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Tennessee. Under this, Vanderbilt VSA is also apart of the Union of North American Vietnamese Student Associations (UNAVSA), a partner of all VSA Regions in the U.S. and Canada.

VSA is home to everyone regardless of whether or not you're Vietnamese. We don't care! Join us to celebrate our rich heritage, build lasting friendships, and make endless memories.
summary: Seeking to promote the Vietnamese culture in the Vanderbilt community, VSA welcomes anyone interested in learning more about the unique history, tradition, cuisine, and culture of this Southeast Asian country.

name: VU ASM
description: The purpose of this organization shall be; (1) promote student interest in and further improve the quality of Microbiology research at Vanderbilt University; (2) engage in local STEM K-12 educational and other community or institutional outreach; (3) promote student participation in local, state and national ASM meetings; (4) establish trans-departmental and trans-institutional collaborations and networks, and (5) provide aid for students in Microbiology at Vanderbilt University with respect to research and employment.
summary: VU ASM aims to promote student interest in microbiology, engage in local STEM K-12 educational and other community or institutional outreach, promote student participation in ASM events, and provide career development for Vanderbilt students.

name: Vanderbilt Undergraduate Moot Court Board
description:
Undergraduate Moot Court promotes legal education and develops students' oral communication and writing skills through participation in appellate law competitions. Members travel to and participate in the AMCA (national) oral argument competition, the AMCA brief-writing competition, and/or the TISL AMC3 (Tennessee) oral argument and brief-writing competition.

 

Outside of competitions, students have further opportunities for pre-law and pre-professional development. These include chances to work with and speak to current law school students and to attend guest speaker events featuring Tennessee's most accomplished attorneys.
summary: Undergraduate Moot Court promotes holistic legal education through participation in oral argumentation and brief-writing competitions. Students have opportunities to learn about appellate litigation while developing oral communication and writing skills.

name: Change++
description:
Change++ was founded in 2019 as a project by a small group of students seeking to code for good. Since then, teams of talented and motivated students have developed over 25 large-scale software projects at an impressive pace for nonprofits worldwide. Our free service increases the efficiency of our partners while saving $10,000 – $100,000 in operational costs. We deliver professional-grade software for impactful charitable organizations while supporting aspiring software engineers, designers, and managers. 

Each year, we partner with 5-7 nonprofit organizations and create full-stack custom software to help them grow their impact. Our members join development groups of a product manager, an engineering manager, a UX designer, and 3-6 developers and build these projects from start to finish. Our teams mimic real-world companies and serve as an excellent way for students to learn in a collaborative and supportive environment.

Change++ directly supports the Chancellor's vision of immersion and the critical project skills that students need.

The initiative to create a tool or useful piece of technology will be spearheaded by Vanderbilt students with a passion for computer science, design, or community service.

Partners

Works alongside the College of Engineering, The Wondry, and the Office of Active Citizenship and Service with advisors from the respective groups

Sustainability

Focuses on maintaining the applications which are built through continued relationships with nonprofit partners.

Cross-Disciplinary

Promotes diversity in the technology sector, not only across gender and race but also across academic interests.

Creates teams composed of students from students across all disciplines at Vanderbilt.

Professional Development

Enhances students’ job application portfolios at Vanderbilt

Provides students with a mechanism to showcase their technical, team, and presentation skill
summary: Change++ is digitizing the world of nonprofits by providing free, custom software solutions to impactful nonprofit organizations.

name: Student Curriculum Committee
description:
The Vanderbilt University School of Medicine faculty values students' perspectives on their education, and its members invite students to take an active role in shaping the VUSM curriculum. The Student Curriculum Committee (SCC) is the official body through which medical students can provide feedback to course instructors, voice opinions about their medical education, and effect changes to the VUSM curriculum.

The Student Curriculum Committee reports to the Undergraduate Medical Education Executive Committee (UMEC), the VUSM committee that has primary responsibility for planning and maintaining the medical school curriculum. The Undergraduate Medical Education Executive Committee takes students' opinions very seriously and values their input. If you are interested in learning more about how students’ input is incorporated in the work of UMEC, speak with your classmates who serve on the Student Curriculum Committee.

Members

Class of 2022
Carsen Cash, Michael G. Clark, Alistair Hilton, Sharon Kam, Emily Thomas

Class of 2023
Shirley Chen, Rachel Goodman, Pulak Goswami, Rosie Mahoney, Bushra Rahman, Kelsey Richard, Ananya Sharma, Caroline Soane, Dennis Zhou

Class of 2024
Malini Anand, Gabriel Bendfeldt, Ratannya Bralley, Zoe Finer, Shravi Lam, Gloria Nashed, Georgina Sellyn

Class of 2025
Elections to be held in the Fall
summary: The Student Curriculum Committee (SCC) is the official body through which medical students can provide feedback to course instructors, voice opinions about their medical education, and effect changes to the VUSM curriculum.

name: Biomedical Research Education and Training
description:
The Office of Biomedical Research Education and Training (BRET) supports graduate education, postdoctoral training, minority affairs, career development, and educational technology initiatives for the Vanderbilt biomedical research community.  Included under the BRET auspices are the Office of Career Development, the Office of Outcomes Analysis, and Poster Printing Services. The BRET office has extensive responsibility for Training Grant applications, administration and coordination.

 

The BRET Office handles all aspects of the admissions processes for graduate programs in the biomedical sciences including the Interdisciplinary Graduate Program (IGP), the Quantitative & Chemical Biology Program (QCB), and the Master in Laboratory Investigation Program. Other training programs administered by the BRET office are the Vanderbilt Summer Science Academy for undergraduate students, the Vanderbilt Program in Molecular Medicine (VPMM), and the Initiatives for Maximizing Student Diversity (IMSD). The BRET Office organizes and hosts a Responsible Conduct of Research training day each spring for Vanderbilt researchers and trainees.
summary: The Office of Biomedical Research Education and Training (BRET) supports graduate education, postdoctoral training, minority affairs, career development, and educational technology initiatives for the Vanderbilt biomedical research community.

name: Kappa Delta Pi
description:
Kappa Delta Pi is an International Honor Society in Education that aims to support educators throughout their careers by providing resources, programs, and other services. The Vanderbilt Chapter hopes to provide members with opportunities to get involved in the local community and learn from one another through discussions on different education topics.

Our Vision: Quality Learning for All

Our Mission: Kappa Delta Pi advances quality education by inspiring teachers to prepare all learners for future challenges. We encourage quality learning by giving educators the means to implement research-based strategies, continue professional growth, assume leadership, and become master teachers. KDP achieves this goal of quality learning for all by:

  • Providing teacher education programs with professional resources and sustained support for retaining effective educators in all classrooms.
  • Developing teacher leaders to be change agents and empowering them to advocate for the best interest of students and the teaching profession.
  • Advancing instruction that ensures students are globally aware, socially responsible, resilient, and able to solve problems in a just and equitable way.
summary: Kappa Delta Pi (KDP), International Honor Society in Education, was founded in 1911 to foster excellence in education and promote fellowship among those dedicated to teaching.

name: Insights Lab at Vanderbilt
description: Vanderbilt students and staff as well as Nashville community members can participate in our ongoing research. Students may also inquire about becoming a research assistant in the lab and attending lab meetings, in which the latest insights coming out of the lab are discussed.
summary: The Insights Lab runs consumer behavior and other behavioral research studies with the Vanderbilt students and staff.

name: Immersion Vanderbilt
summary: Coordinating office for the Immersion Vanderbilt degree requirement. Responsible for creating and maintaining resources for the community about IV and for hosting related events and showcases.

name: Pharmacology Department
summary: Pharmacology Department

name: E. Bronson Ingram College
description:
Teresa Goddu, Faculty Head of E. Bronson Ingram College

Teresa A. Goddu (she, her, hers) is professor of English at Vanderbilt University. She received her B.A. from Yale University and her Ph.D. from the University of Pennsylvania. Professor Goddu specializes in nineteenth-century American literature and culture, slavery and antislavery, as well as print, material, and visual culture. Her current research focuses on the environmental humanities, specifically contemporary climate fiction. She is the recipient of the Jeffrey Nordhaus Award for Excellence in Undergraduate Teaching in the Humanities and the Chancellor’s Cup.

The Residential College

E. Bronson Ingram College, opened in fall 2018, is located on the former site of Vanderbilt and Barnard halls and provides housing for 340 sophomores, juniors, and seniors. E. Bronson Ingram College offers a variety of spaces in which its residents can live, learn, collaborate and socialize. The 205,000 sq. ft. facility houses students, faculty and graduate students. Residents have on-site access to a shared dining room, study lounges, social spaces and formal & informal outdoor green spaces.
summary: E. Bronson Ingram College

name: Moore College
description:
Jesús Ruiz, Faculty Head of Moore College

Jesús G. Ruiz (he, him, his) is an assistant professor of the practice in Caribbean Studies at the Center for Latin American, Caribbean, and Latinx Studies (CLACX). At CLACX, he also serves as both the director of the Caribbean Studies Minor and program coordinator of the Foreign Language and Area Studies (FLAS) Fellowships, as well as the Simon Collier Summer Research Awards.

Jesús's research explores slavery, freedom, and Black political thought in the Atlantic World. His first book, The Black Royalists: Haiti and A Politics of Freedom in the Atlantic World, which examines the politics of royalism in the Haitian Revolution, is currently under contract with Harvard University Press. 

Jesús is a first-generation citizen of the U.S., born in Los Angeles, CA, to immigrants from Sonora, Mexico. He was raised in Maricopa County, Arizona and became the first in his family to attend college. He has a B.A. in Spanish with honors from St. Lawrence University and an M.A. in Caribbean studies from The University at Buffalo (SUNY). He will lead Moore College, where he'll live with his wife, Elise, and his two boys, Marcos and Santos.

The Residential College

It is home to 341 upperclass students in four, five, and six-person suites along with more traditional double and single rooms. Students live on all floors of this six story building. Building residents are encouraged to take advantage of the lounges, study areas, music rooms, conference rooms, common kitchen and laundry facilities. The faculty head of college lives on the fifth floor in an apartment with his family.

Moore College is named in honor of Stanford Moore, a 1935 Vanderbilt graduate who received the Nobel Prize in chemistry in 1972. Moore Residential College represents a key pillar of the Academic Strategic Plan representing vibrant living-learning communities that create transformative educational experiences for Vanderbilt students.
summary: Moore College

name: Warren College
description:
Robin Jones, Faculty Head of College

Robin Jones (he, him, his) is an associate professor and vice chair for faculty development in the department of hearing and speech sciences, and the director of the developmental stuttering laboratory. His research is interdisciplinary and focuses on speech-language, cognitive, emotional, and genetic contributions to the pathogenesis of developmental stuttering as well as the translation of this work to diagnosis and treatment. To address these research questions, his lab uses a diverse set of approaches and methods, including caregiver report, behavioral, and neuro- and psycho-physiological measures. Jones joined the Vanderbilt faculty in 2013 and has been head of Warren College since 2024.

The Residential College

It is home to 319 upper-division students in four, five, and six-person suites along with more traditional double and single rooms. Students live on all floors of this five-story building. Building residents are encouraged to take advantage of the lounges, study areas, music rooms, conference rooms, common kitchen and laundry facilities. The faculty head of college lives on the fifth floor in an apartment.

Warren College is named in honor of 1925 Vanderbilt graduate Robert Penn Warren, member of the influential Fugitive poets and the first poet laureate of the United States.
summary: Warren College

name: Vanderbilt Creative Writing
summary: Creative Writing, Department of English

name: Undergraduate Seminar in Mathematics
description:
The Undergraduate Seminar in Mathematics is a weekly, hour long talk. There you can hear about some of the many deep and interesting areas of mathematics beyond what you would see in the classroom of most math classes. The talks are designed to be accessible to any college student; little to no math background is required. The seminar occurs most weeks on Tuesday from 6-7pm, in Stevenson 1206. Come by and enjoy some pizza and soda before the talk, and hear about something new. We hope to see you there!

Please visit our website to see information about upcoming talks!
summary: The Undergraduate Seminar in Mathematics is a weekly, hour long talk. There you can hear about some of the many deep and interesting areas of mathematics beyond what you would see in the classroom of most math classes.

name: Program in American Studies
description: The Program in American Studies is an interdisciplinary program fostering the in-depth study of American cultures from a variety of perspectives. Students choose their path through the major or minor based on their own intellectual commitments. Through course offerings, colloquia, and research opportunities, students and faculty examine questions of power and conflict, experience and identity, tradition and social change within and beyond the territorial borders of the United States and the Americas. Matters of class, race, region, gender, ethnicity, sexuality, religion, law, migration, environment, technology, politics, and the arts are central to the curriculum. The program allows study at the local, subnational, national, and global levels, from in-depth investigation of Nashville communities to research on transnational nongovernmental organizations.
summary: The Program in American Studies is an interdisciplinary program fostering the in-depth study of American cultures from a variety of perspectives.

name: The Center for Effective Lawmaking
description: The Center for Effective Lawmaking is a joint initiative between the University of Virginia’s Batten School of Leadership and Public Policy and Vanderbilt University that utilizes a data-driven approach to study the causes and consequences of each U.S. representative’s and senator’s ability to advance agenda items through the legislative process into law.  The co-directors are Craig Volden from the University of Virginia and Alan Wiseman from Vanderbilt University.
summary: The Center utilizes a data-driven approach to study the causes and consequences of each U.S. representative’s and senator’s ability to advance agenda items through the legislative process into law.

name: Design as an Immersive Vanderbilt Experience
description: DIVE is a program at Vanderbilt that will teach human-centered design thinking to students so that they may solve complex, real-world problems, develop critical thinking skills, and work in multidisciplinary teams during an immersive, mentored project.
summary: DIVE is a program at Vanderbilt that will teach human-centered design thinking to students so that they may solve complex, real-world problems, develop critical thinking skills, and work in multidisciplinary teams during an immersive, mentored project.

name: Iris Journal of Scholarship
description: The Iris Journal of Scholarship is a venue for Graduate and Professional students to publish accessible academic writing while engaging in the peer-review and publishing process. Led by Peabody College students, the journal aims to connect the education community in scholarship across disciplines, providing interdisciplinary, multifaceted perspectives on local, national, and global issues in education and human development. Through publication, graduate students are empowered to circulate their work among policymakers, academics, professionals, and the public.
summary: The Iris Journal of Scholarship is a venue for professional and master’s students to publish accessible academic writing while engaging in the peer-review and publishing process. Led by Peabody College students, the journal aims to connect the education

name: Law School Council for Culture & Community
description:
Council for Culture & Community (CCC) offers a forum for student leaders to discuss issues surrounding belonging, community engagement and enrichment, and to plan initiatives related to academic programming, career opportunities, recruiting, community building, and orientation.

Our ultimate goal is to provide outreach to prospective students, foster an academic and social environment where every student feels comfortable, safe and welcome, and to serve as a representative voice for student governance at VLS.
summary: The CCC was founded to address the growing concerns of Vanderbilt law students who belong to underrepresented communities.

name: Volunteers for Health
description:
Volunteers for Health (V4H) is a service organization dedicated to bringing an interactive health curriculum and education to elementary school students. In the Metro Nashville Public School District, health class is not required to be taught, thus paving a road for us to provide that education. About four times a semester, groups of 3-4 volunteers visit elementary school classes to teach health topics including sickness prevention, healthy eating, and mental health. Check out our website for more information:https://sites.google.com/view/volunteersforhealth/home

A member of V4H has the opportunity to be a general member within a group of 3-4 other volunteers or a group leader. 

V4H provides Vanderbilt students the opportunity to give back to Nashville and lessen health disparities.

If you would like to join, please email Gina Yu (gina.l.yu@vanderbilt.edu) with your updated Protection of Minors training.
summary: Volunteers for Health empowers children to make healthy decisions by developing and teaching health lessons to Metro Nashville elementary schoolers.

name: Save the Children Action Network
description: Save the Children's mission is to give every child a strong start at life. Since 1932, Save the Children has been on the ground providing support to the most isolated and underserved children in rural America as well as ending preventable childhood deaths internationally. As the political advocacy arm of Save the Children, we’re building bipartisan will and voter support to make sure every child in the U.S. has access to high-quality early learning and that no mother or child around the globe dies from a preventable disease or illness. By investing in kids and holding leaders accountable, we are helping kids from birth to age five survive and thrive.
summary: Save the Children's mission is to give every child a strong start at life. Since 1932, Save the Children has been on the ground providing support to the most isolated and underserved children in rural America as well as ending preventable childhood death

name: Student Care Network
description:
What is the Student Care Network?

The Student Care Network is a holistic network of services and resources pertaining to health and wellness available to all Vanderbilt University students. Primary offices include the Office of Student Care Coordination, the University Counseling Center, the Student Health Center, and the Center for Student Wellbeing. Students also have access to a wide range of additional on-campus and community resources through the Student Care Network.

 

How do I access my Student Care Network resources?

  • Explore your Resources online. They are organized within nine wellness categories.
  • Complete the Student Care Coordination Intake Form with the Office of Student Care Coordination, or follow-up with your Student Care Coordinator directly if you have already completed the intake form. Once you complete the intake form, you will be directed to a link to schedule a 30-minute face-to-face, Zoom, or telephone appointment with a Student Care Coordinator.
  • You may also call the Office of Student Care Coordination at 615-343-WELL (9355) if you have any questions.
  • Our Offices is open Monday-Friday 8 a.m. to 5 p.m., and we are located in Rand, Suite 305.
  • The Student Care Coordinator will refer you to the best resources to meet your needs. These may include on-campus resources, off-campus community providers, or telemedicine services. (Telemedicine is remote treatment by a community provider through telecommuting, including video and/or phone consultations.)
  • Take advantage of a FREE Headspace membership and other self-directed tools that the Student Care Network offers to help with reducing stress, improving focus and increasing resiliency.
summary: The Student Care Network is a holistic network of services and resources pertaining to health and wellness available to all Vanderbilt University students.

organizationType.name: Student Affairs Department, Office, or Program

name: Vanderbilt University Sports and Society Initiative
description: The Sports and Society Initiative, established in January 2018, intends to elevate awareness and understanding of the ways in which sports and society impact each other across a variety of disciplines. The Initiative host a yearly series exploring issues at the intersection of sports and society. The 2018-19 series will explore the various ways the Olympics has influenced our society.
summary: The Sports and Society Initiative, established in January 2018, intends to elevate awareness and understanding of the ways in which sports and society impact each other across a variety of disciplines. The Initiative host a yearly series exploring issues

name: Vanderbilt Piano Society
description: Our mission is to bring together pianists and piano enthusiasts all over the Vanderbilt campus in celebration of our passion for piano. We seek to learn from one another through collaborative approaches and share our artistry with the Vanderbilt and Nashville community. We promote collaborative musical discovery, appreciation, and teaching.
summary: Our mission is to bring together pianists and piano enthusiasts all over the Vanderbilt campus in celebration of our passion for piano. We seek to learn from one another through collaborative approaches and share our artistry with the Vanderbilt and Na

name: Vanderbilt Association of Bangladeshi Students
description: To inform the Vanderbilt community about the rich Bangladeshi culture - a culture with which over 250 million people all over the world identify.
summary: To inform the Vanderbilt community about the rich Bangladeshi culture - a culture with which over 250 million people all over the world identify.

name: The Alexander Hamilton Society
description:
The Alexander Hamilton Society is Vanderbilt's only nonpartisan organization focusing on American foreign policy, national security, and economic statecraft. We bring in distinguished speakers from the public sector, think tanks, and academia who speak on foreign policy and international affairs.

Vanderbilt AHS also offers fully-funded book clubs, crisis simulations, and community conversations about international relations. Vanderbilt AHS, is part of the national Alexander Hamilton Society network, which connects students to jobs, scholarships, and other professional development opportunities in the foreign policy world. Our chapter boasts a commitment to connecting its members to the very best opportunities in Washington, DC and beyond.

 

Ideologically, AHS believes "the world is a better, safer, and more prosperous place when the United States is willing and able to lead; and a commitment to maintaining the moral authority and material strength on which that leadership rests."
summary: The Alexander Hamilton Society is Vanderbilt's only nonpartisan foreign policy and national security organization. We host acclaimed speakers, book clubs, crisis simulations, and connect students to a robust network of foreign policy practitioners.

name: Vanderbilt Club Basketball
description: The Vanderbilt Club Basketball team promotes the student athlete without compromising academic performance. We aim to foster a competitive, prestigious, but also constructive environment to capable athletes who wish to primarily focus on academics while also engaging in athletics.
summary: The Vanderbilt Club Basketball team promotes the student athlete without compromising academic performance. We aim to foster a competitive, prestigious, but also constructive environment to capable athletes.

name: VUSN Pride
description:
VUSNPride is dedicated to fostering social engagement, networking and sense of belonging for LGBTQ+ students and allies. It provides support and mentorship for VUSN students and applicants, while striving to create a culture of safety and community for all LGBTQ+ people at the school.

VUSNPride is a vibrant and influential community that fosters social engagement, networking and a sense of belonging. We provide support and mentorship for VUSN applicants and students across all programs, and strive to create a culture of safety for all LGBTQ+ students. We are an avenue for addressing any issues that may arise for the community and we are a place to call home.
summary: VUSNPride is dedicated to fostering social engagement, networking and sense of belonging for LGBTQ+ students and allies. It provides support and mentorship for VUSN students and applicants, while striving to create a culture of safety and community...

name: VUSM Social Mission Committee
description:
VUSM Social Mission Task Committee is a medical student-run organization dedicated to advancing the VUSM Social Mission Statement:

VUSM Social Mission Statement

Vanderbilt University School of Medicine empowers students, faculty, and staff to actively partner with individuals and communities to achieve health equity.

Philosophy

Our philosophy is to educate diverse, socially and culturally accountable future physician leaders who understand systems of care and social determinants of health. By advocating to meet the needs of all persons within the context of their care and working for larger systemic changes, our physicians will promote equity and justice within their local and global communities.
summary: VUSM Social Mission Task Committee works to advance social mission at Vanderbilt University School of Medicine with the aim of empowering students, faculty, and staff to actively partner with individuals and communities to achieve health equity

name: Vanderbilt Asian Pre-professional Society
description: Our mission is to grow a global network of highly motivated student leaders who seek to impact Vanderbilt and the greater business community. We want to foster a tight-knit community focused on developing professional and leadership skills for success. We welcome students of all majors and interests to join our club in order to develop their professional skills.
summary: Our mission is to grow a global network of highly motivated student leaders who seek to impact Vanderbilt and the greater business community. We want to foster a tight-knit community focused on developing professional and leadership skills for success.

name: ALPFA Vanderbilt
description: ALPFA Vanderbilt (Association of Latino Professionals For America) aims to empower the Latino community at Vanderbilt by providing networking opportunities across various career fields and equipping students with the tools to prepare for their future. Through career development workshops, professional speakers, and the extensive connections offered by the national ALPFA organization, we strive to support Latino students—and anyone who supports the Latino community—on their journey through college and beyond.
summary: The mission of ALPFA Vanderbilt is to empower and provide networking opportunities for Latino students—and those who support them—at Vanderbilt. Across a variety of fields, we strive to help our members achieve success in their careers.

name: Biochemistry & Chemical Biology Student Association
description: The mission of the Biochemistry and Chemical Biology Student Association is to represent, unite, and empower the students pursuing this intense, inter-disciplinary science major. The organization aims to provide unique opportunities for BCB students to pursue research under Vanderbilt faculty, participate in summer research internships, and connect with some of the most distinguished faculty at Vanderbilt. Furthermore, the organization establishes a peer mentoring system and provides student perspective on the major's curriculum.
summary: The mission of the Biochemistry and Chemical Biology Student Association is to represent, unite, and provide resources for the undergraduate students pursuing this intense, inter-disciplinary science major.

name: Owen Honor Council
description: The OGSM Honor Council is an organization of students that seeks to preserve the integrity of the Honor Code at Vanderbilt University. It aims to secure justice for any student under suspicion of dishonesty, to vindicate those innocent, and, if guilty, to protect the honor and standing of the remaining students by punishing the guilty as set forth in the Bylaws. It proposes to do this in accordance with the procedures, rules, and organization hereinafter set forth.
summary: Owen Honor Council

name: Office of Commencement & Special Events
description: The Office of Commencement and Special Events is a production and logistics team that manages 3 days of Commencement ceremonies annually for the university. By blending tradition with technology, we seek to foster innovation as a leading event organization on campus. Our team supports the Office of the Provost by hosting large and small events year-round and provides event consultation to the university at large.
summary: The Office of Commencement and Special Events manages the 3 days of Commencement each year in May. Our team also supports the Office of the Provost by hosting large and small events year-round and provides event consultation to the university at large.

name: National Organization for the Professional Advancement of Black Chemists and Chemical Engineers
description: The purpose of the NOBCChE chapter is to increase the participation of people of color in chemistry, chemical engineering, biomedical sciences, and related disciplines through diverse programming and other opportunities designed to foster scholarship, encourage research innovation, facilitate collaboration, and provide resources for career and professional development. The Chapter is committed to the professional and educational growth of underrepresented minorities in the chemical sciences and related fields. This chapter also intends to impact the national view of scientists by working within the local Nashville community.
summary: The purpose of the NOBCChE chapter is to increase the participation of people of color in chemistry, chemical engineering, biomedical sciences, and related disciplines through diverse programming and other opportunities designed to foster scholarship...

name: English Major Association
description: Our mission is to unite lovers of literature across Vanderbilt's campus to create a more inclusive community of English majors. We seek to be of service to each English major by providing networking opportunities, job information, as well as a personal resource for those pursing a path in literature. Finally, our goal is to encourage others to join the English major community and promote pride in our chosen area of study.
summary: Our mission is to unite lovers of literature across Vanderbilt's campus to create a more inclusive community of English majors. We seek to be of service to each English major by providing networking opportunities, job information

name: VUSM Student-Led Initiative for Disability Education
description:
To educate medical students on the approach to disability, including topics such as physical exam, medical knowledge, referral practices, and social issues. Furthermore, we will serve as an advocacy group for patients and providers living with a disability, and we will provide opportunities for members to develop educational and lecturing skills.

This organization is only open to current MD students.
summary: To educate medical students on the approach to disability, including topics such as physical exam, medical knowledge, referral practices, and social issues.

name: DORE Initiative—Drug Overdose and Reduction Education Initiative
description:
DORE Initiative is a student-led organization with overarching goals of reducing drug abuse and eliminating drug overdose at Vanderbilt University and in the broader Nashville community by eliminating leftover prescription drugs and changing perceptions of social norms regarding drug use. We host programming events that raise awareness, volunteer at addiction recovery centers, and conduct addiction-related research at the medical center. We are working to advocate for VUMC and surrounding pharmacies to implement a program that distributes drug disposal kits for free, which has been proven to effectively decrease prescription drug diversion. 

Join our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/103169228/67aguIRQ

Exec Board Application 24-25: https://forms.gle/YVhSbLLjHqCmcF9X6
summary: DORE Initiative is a student-led organization dedicated to addressing the opioid epidemic and prescription drug abuse by hosting programming events that raise awareness and by advocating for actionable change.

name: Office of Academic and Student Affairs at Owen
description: The Office of Academic and Student Affairs provides support and services for all Owen students from orientation through commencement. Additionally, the department offers curricular support to teaching faculty through the production of course and exam schedules. The office promotes student development and academic achievement and fosters student health, wellness, diversity and inclusion. The team of six staff members offer academic advising, personal counseling, financial support for academic endeavors, oversight and administrative support of club activities and student organizations, accommodations for disabilities, and general guidance for integration and success at Owen. The office has an established support structure to ensure that each student has a fulfilling, safe, and successful experience.
summary: The Office of Academic and Student Affairs provides support and services for all Owen students from orientation through commencement.

name: Tutoring Services
description:
At Tutoring Services, we help students enhance their academic performance, take control of their own learning, and reach their potential in chemistry (general and organic), biological sciences, mathematics, physics, and economics.

 

1-ON-1 TUTORING SESSIONS

Meet with a peer tutor for a (free!) 50-minute session to talk through what you know and what you need to know. We work collaboratively with you to address your questions and concerns.

 

GROUP STUDY OPPORTUNITIES

Join us to study alongside your peers as your exams approach. Tutors are on hand during group study to assist students working to answer questions, clarify course content, and learn together.
summary: At Tutoring Services, we help students enhance their academic performance, take control of their own learning, and reach their potential in chemistry (general and organic), biological sciences, mathematics, physics, and economics.

name: Vanderbilt School of Medicine Veterans Association
description:
The mission of VUSM VA is to create an organization where veterans and service members may:

a. gather and recreate the sense of military family and community

b. provide mentorship for junior members as they begin their military careers

c. engage in ongoing opportunities to serve our community

 

This organization is only open to current VUSM MD Students.
summary:
The mission of VSOMVA is to create an organization where veterans and service members may: 
a. gather and recreate the sense of military family and community 
b. provide mentorship for younger members as they begin their military careers

name: Graduate & Postdoc Academic Success
description: The Graduate & Postdoc Academic Success (GPAS) (formerly known as Academic Life Coach) program provides the critical infrastructure and support for College/School leadership, departments/programs, and faculty to assist their students and postdocs in reaching their academic goals. We do this through direct outreach to departments and faculty, responsive programming and workshops, and direct support of students and postdocs in alignment with their development as the next generation of scholars and leaders. In addition to tailored and responsive programming to support departments, graduate student, and postdoc needs, we offer individual success planning sessions which include conversations around progress, productivity and navigating academic relationships.
summary:
Graduate & Postdoc Academic Success (GPAS)
The Graduate School
Vanderbilt University

name: English Language Center (ELC)
description: The Vanderbilt University English Language Center (ELC) provides English-language support for individuals who use English as an additional language and who are enrolled or working at Vanderbilt University. Our role is to help you achieve the most from your Vanderbilt experience by providing quality, individualized English-language instruction.
summary: The Vanderbilt University English Language Center (ELC) provides English-language support for individuals who use English as an additional language and who are enrolled or working at Vanderbilt University.

name: Anthropology Department
description: Vanderbilt's Department of Anthropology emphasizes these subdisciplines to achieve a better understanding of the world, past and present. The members of the faculty are internationally prominent for their research and publications on the New World, especially Mesoamerica, the Andes, and Amazonia. The department maintains research interests and active field programs in Mexico, Guatemala, El Salvador, Brazil, Bolivia, Columbia, Peru, Argentina, and Chile.
summary: Vanderbilt's Department of Anthropology emphasizes these subdisciplines to achieve a better understanding of the world, past and present.

name: Vanderbilt English Department
description: The page is for events hosted by the Vanderbilt English Department, including readings, social gatherings, and alumni talks.
summary: The page is for events hosted by the Vanderbilt English Department, including readings, social gatherings, and alumni talks.

name: Vanderbilt Institute for Digital Learning
summary: VIDL supports faculty, staff, and students in online learning design, digital media production, and research.

name: Society for the History of Medicine and Surgery
description:
The mission is to foster an appreciation for the rich and complex history of Medicine/Surgery and for its relevance to the training of future physicians. We aim to share the lessons in humanism that History of Medicine/Surgery can teach, and to incorporate these lessons into empathetic patient care. We hope to encourage critical analysis of the past to gain an understanding of Medicine’s future, as well as to inspire solutions to current challenges faced by our profession. Additionally, we hope to challenge students to understand the social and political impact of Medicine through the ages. We also will foster a better understanding of the historical roots and traditions in medicine that continue to this day. Finally, we aim to build relationships between students, residents, attendings, and historians who share a passion for History of Medicine/Surgery.

 

Note: This organization is only open to current VUSM MD Students.
summary: Our mission is to foster an appreciation for the rich and complex history of medicine and surgery and for its relevance to the training of future physicians.

name: Vanderbilt Pre-Medical Book Club
description: We expose pre-medical students at Vanderbilt University to the social, ethical, personal, and humanistic aspects of the practice of medicine through the collective reading and discussion of novels and books about healthcare. Furthermore, we provide effective mentorship from physician faculty members who serve as role-models, generous teachers, and regular members of the book club. Finally, we create an encouraging, collegial, thought-provoking space for pre-medical students to connect with one-another and have earnest, fruitful, guided discussions with peers and mentors in their chosen career path. Fill out our application to join us in the Fall of 2024!
summary: We expose pre-medical students at Vanderbilt University to the social, ethical, personal, and humanistic aspects of the practice of medicine through the collective reading and discussion of medical humanities literature.

name: Vanderbilt University Student Chapter of the Association for Women in Mathematics
description: AWM aims to foster a sense of community among students who love math and are passionate about diversifying the field. We provide a space where people of all gender identities, backgrounds, and majors can talk about their experiences in mathematics, make connections with other people who love math, and work to inspire positive change on campus! We host biweekly meetings that focus on doing math together, building community, and discussing social issues in the math community (centering these around the experiences of women and other mathematicians in historically excluded groups, such as people of color and the LGBT community). All are welcome, and you do not have to be a math major or identify as a woman to be a part of our community.
summary: AWM is a community centered around supporting women in math and increasing representation. We aim to provides a space where people of all gender identities, backgrounds, and majors can talk about their experiences in mathematics and do math together.

name: Women in Government
description: The purpose of Women in Government is to break down barriers to women’s leadership in the public and private sectors. Women in Government seeks to create a community on campus for women interested in law, policy, public service, and other leadership roles. This organization’s primary goal is to provide opportunities for women to develop their communication, leadership, and professional skills.
summary: Women in Government is a nonpartisan organization that aims to provide women with the tools and confidence to become leaders in their community through a welcoming campus community for women interested in the law, public policy, public service, and more.

name: VUSM Methuselah Society
description: To improve non-traditional student enrollment and build a welcoming community for non-traditional medical students at VUSM. We accomplish this through mentorship opportunities and faculty discussions for current VUSM students and through recruitment initiatives for prospective VUSM students. Please email august.kim@vanderbilt.edu with any questions.
summary: To improve non-traditional student enrollment and build a welcoming community for non-traditional medical students at VUSM.

name: Global Surgery Student Alliance
description: To educate and inspire students through engagement and mentorship in global surgery and to facilitate collaboration between students working towards the common goal of increasing access to safe and affordable surgical care for all people. This organization is only open to current VUSM MD Students.
summary: To educate and inspire students through engagement and mentorship in global surgery and to facilitate collaboration between students working towards the common goal of increasing access to safe and affordable surgical care for all people.

name: MoveMENTAL
description: MoveMENTAL is an organization dedicated to examining the intersection of mental health awareness and cultural identity. It is a dynamic, fluid organization that strives to cultivate new initiatives to demystify mental health across generational and cultural gaps. MoveMENTAL is not a therapy group; rather, it strives to engage individuals in expressing their perspectives on mental health, encouraging them to share their story to bring awareness to the greater community - and, as a result, bring healing into their own lives. In this way, MoveMENTAL hopes to extend its vision of multicultural mental health not just into every corner of Vanderbilt's campus, but into the ethnically diverse neighborhoods of Nashville, addressing the mental health concerns surrounding socioeconomic status, sexuality, immigration, and race and ethnicity.
summary: MoveMENTAL is an organization dedicated to examining the intersection of mental health awareness and cultural/personal identity.

name: Vanderbilt Programs for Talented Youth
summary: Vanderbilt Programs for Talented Youth is part of Vanderbilt University's Peabody College of education and human development committed to providing quality talent development opportunities for advanced learner.

summary: The fund was endowed by Wayne S. Hyatt '68 (BA'65) and his late wife, Amanda M. Hyatt (BA'67, MA'74), to support student-initiated programs that enrich the intellectual content of student activities at Vanderbilt University Law School.

name: Space Law Society
summary: The Space Law Society's mission is to educate the Vanderbilt Law School community about the issues and advancements of Space Law and to connect the Vanderbilt Law School community with practitioners and scholars in the field of Space Law.

name: Out In Engineering
description:
Out in Engineering is an organization dedicated to the visibility and advancement of LGBTQI+ people in the engineering field. We make it our mission to attract speakers from the community to come and inspire members, share opportunities specifically for LGBTQI+ engineering professionals (such as the Out for Undergrad conference series), and provide a safe space for those struggling with the balancing act of the stress of engineering classes and finding their true self in college.

We strive to provide a mix of social and professional programming. A big issue we hope to tackle is that there are members of the LGBTQI+ community in engineering but very few know each other. By providing safe social events, we can connect this lesser-known network of the school. We will be proactive in partnering with other student organizations and companies to encourage our members to develop both socially and professionally.
summary: Out in Engineering is a professional organization dedicated to the visibility and advancement of LGBTQ+ people in the engineering field.

name: Vanderbilt Vanguard
description:
The Vanderbilt Vanguard is an online, student-run STEM publication. We aim to educate and energize readers about scientific & technological advancements occurring on-campus and in the Nashville community.

 

Interdisciplinary in nature, the Vanguard includes students from all backgrounds. We not only publish in Life Sciences & Engineering, but also in English, Public Policy, Medicine, and Psychology -- any and all disciplines taught at Vanderbilt are welcome here.

                                              

Interested in joining the organization as a Staff Writer? Contact zoe.a.breininger@vanderbilt.edu, fill out this form, and join our AnchorLink! Visit us today: vanderbiltvanguard.com.
summary: The Vanderbilt Vanguard is an online, student-run STEM publication. We aim to educate and energize readers about scientific & technological advancements occurring on campus and in the Nashville community.

name: Vanderbilt Community Circles
description: Vanderbilt Community Circles is an interprofessional initiative developed to provide the Vanderbilt medical community a dedicated venue to discuss current events and societal issues. Vanderbilt Community Circles strives to promote civil discourse between students, faculty, and staff, broach difficult topics important to contemporary medicine in an unbiased, open, and respectful environment, and host neutral and safe events where every single person feels confidence to share personal experiences and hear stories from others with whom they may disagree.
summary: The Vanderbilt Community Circle is an interprofessional initiative developed to provide faculty, staff, and students within the entire medical center a dedicated venue to discuss current events and societal issues in an open and inclusive environment.

name: Peabody Chinese Student Organization
description: (Formally Known as MAZE) Studying abroad means stepping into a new life and also gaining a variety of opportunities. To taste every bit of life aspects and to grow from our holding environment, we aim to help all Chinese international students customize individual successful experiences with Peabody values and partnership.
summary: Studying abroad means stepping into a new life and a variety of opportunities. To taste every bit of life aspects and to grow from our holding environment, we aim to help all Chinese international students customize the individual successful experiences.

name: Vanderbilt SAFE
description:
Vanderbilt SAFE (Scrubs Addressing the Firearm Epidemic) is an organization at Vanderbilt University School of Medicine committed to addressing the firearm epidemic in the United States. Vanderbilt SAFE aims to encourage medical students, physicians, nurses, and other healthcare professionals to treat gun violence as a public health crisis. This organization will focus on a variety of gun-violence related issues in an effort to explore the complex dynamics of gun violence in modern society.

The mission of this organization is:

  • To support gun violence research by partnering with AFFIRM (American Foundation for Firearm Injury Reduction in Medicine)
  • To educate healthcare professionals on the multi-systemic effects of gun violence
  • To support evidence-based policy on a local and national level
  • To partner with local grassroots organizations to address gun-related injuries and gun violence in Tennessee
  • To promote proper gun-ownership while supporting the second amendment

Membership is open to all School of Medicine students.
summary: Vanderbilt SAFE (Scrubs Addressing the Firearm Epidemic) is an organization that recognizes gun violence as a public health crisis. Through research, education, and policy, Vandy SAFE hopes to address the firearm epidemic in both local & national spheres

name: Ethiopian-Eritrean Student Association
description: The Ethiopian-Eritrean Student Association’s (EESA) mission is to provide a safe, inclusive space for Ethiopian and Eritrean Vanderbilt students to build community, celebrate their shared heritage, and connect through culture. In addition to supporting our members, we are committed to increasing awareness of Ethiopian and Eritrean cultures across campus. Through events, workshops, and cultural showcases, we aim to educate, engage, and invite the broader Vanderbilt community to learn about and experience the rich traditions, history, and contributions of Ethiopia and Eritrea. Our organization fosters an environment where all students—regardless of background—can feel welcome to participate, learn, and grow alongside us.
summary: The Ethiopian-Eritrean Student Association strives to promote Ethiopian and Eritrean culture within the Vanderbilt student body and encourage service and awareness of pressing issues regarding Ethiopian and Eritrean communities, both locally and globally

name: Confidance
description:
Confidance was founded in 2020 by a group of friends who loved to dance. Today, Confidance hosts community dance workshops and our performance team is comprised of a tightknit group of undergraduate and graduate students from various dance backgrounds.

The name Confidance (Confidence + Dance) represents our goal to inspire dancers of all levels to experience the joy of dance, artistry, and community. Come dance with us at one of our workshops or audition for our performance team!
summary: Confidance aims to train, uplift, and advance dancers of all levels and styles, valuing excellence, student leadership, artistry, and community.

name: Double Major A Cappella
description: Double Major is Vanderbilt’s ~zaniest~ all-gender a cappella group! We perform a cappella arrangements from a variety of genres and foster a fun environment for creating music together by forming a close-knit community of members.
summary: Double Major is Vanderbilt’s ~zaniest~ all-gender a cappella group! We perform a cappella arrangements from a variety of genres and foster a fun environment for creating music together by forming a close-knit community of members.

description:
At Anchor Marketing, our mission is to create real-world marketing experiences for all Vanderbilt students. We introduce students to real-life, team-based projects where they serve as consultants while also learning about the principles of marketing.

Join our groupme: https://groupme.com/join_group/95604204/F6904BBU
summary: At Anchor Marketing, our mission is to create real-world marketing experiences for all Vanderbilt students. We introduce students to real-life, team-based projects where they serve as consultants while also learning about the principles of marketing.

name: Google Developer Group
description:
The Google Developer Group chapter at Vanderbilt is dedicated to provide opportunities to students interested in learning about Google technologies and solving problems in the community through tech-based solutions. Through workshops, panel discussions, and other events, our club hopes to foster an environment of technical and community growth. Throughout the year, members will work on a team project to submit to the annual Solution Challenge hosted by Google.

Visit the Google Developer Student Clubs site to learn more:

https://developers.google.com/community/dsc


https://groupme.com/join_group/84685501/1PPaffzO
summary: To provide an opportunity to learn about Google technologies, to build solutions for problems in the community, to enhance programming and teamwork-based skills though practical application, and to develop connections in the tech industry.

name: StoryOrg
description: Our monthly storytelling events have a wide spectrum of themes and methods. Our mission is to explore story/narrative to foster connectedness and understanding between diverse groups of people. Undergrad students, grad students, faculty, and anyone else interested in participating are welcome at our events. Further details on each month's event (including that month's storytelling theme + involved activities) will be sent out via email to everyone signed up on Anchorlink.
summary: Story Org hosts monthly storytelling events to foster connectedness and understanding among diverse groups of people.

name: Design Studio
description: The Design Studio is a student-run workshop located in Featheringill Hall room 131A. Volunteer student mentors monitor the space and assist users with their creations. Come say hello or apply to be a mentor – we’d be happy to show you around!
summary: The Design Studio is a student-run workshop located in Featheringill Hall room 131A. Volunteer student mentors monitor the space and assist users with their creations. Come say hello or apply to be a mentor – we’d be happy to show you around!

name: Data Science Institute
description: Vanderbilt established the Data Science Institute to accelerate data-driven research, promote collaboration, and train future leaders. The institute brings together experts in data science methods with leaders in all academic fields to spark new discoveries in research as well as to study the impact of big data on society. The institute educates students in computational and statistical data science techniques to become future leaders in industry, government, academia and the non-profit sector.

description: The Hispanic/Latinx Student Affinity Group is committed to representing students of all nursing levels who identify as Hispanic/Latinx at VUSN. We are a passionate group of nursing students that focus on exploring the health-related aspects of the Hispanic/Latinx patient population to ultimately raise awareness on how we, and our nursing peers/colleagues, can provide culturally-appropriate care for this community. Additionally, our organization also functions as a safe resource of support and mentorship to its members as they embark on and/or explore their journey as an APRN who desires to best serve the Hispanic/Latinx population.
summary: VUSN student affinity group committed to representing, supporting, and building community for Hispanic/Latinx students

name: 1GMD
description:
1GMD (First-Generation in Medicine at Vanderbilt University School of Medicine) is dedicated to fostering a supportive environment for medical students who identify as first-generation college graduates, first-generation graduate students, and/or come from challenging financial backgrounds. We strive to build meaningful resources, cultivate a strong sense of community, and raise awareness of the unique strengths and challenges faced by first-generation college graduates, first-generation graduate students, and/or medical students coming from challenging financial backgrounds as they navigate medical education.

This organization is only open to current VUSM MD Students.
summary: 1GMD is a medical school organization committed to enhancing the unique strengths of and alleviating the distinct challenges faced by first-generation college graduates, first-generation graduate students, and students facing financial challenges.

summary: Our mission is to not only educate Vanderbilt students in MSF’s medical outreach and the world’s ongoing public health issues but also to empower them to take action through fundraising and participation in group activities.

name: Pi Mu Epsilon
description:
Pi Mu Epsilon is a national math honor society founded at Syracuse University in 1914. PME hosts an annual national conference as well as various regional conferences where members can present and discuss interesting math topics and original research. PME also publishes student-authored papers in a semi-annual journal. The Tennessee Zeta Chapter of Pi Mu Epsilon was founded in 2013. Our chapter also assists students with funding for math-related contests and activities. In order to join PME, a Vanderbilt student must:

- declare a math major or minor

- complete

  1) MATH 1200-1201-1301-2300 and one course above calculus difficulty, or

  2) MATH 1300-1301-2300 and one course above calculus difficulty, or

  3) MATH 2500 and 2501

- maintain a 3.3 or higher GPA in math courses

- maintain a 3.0 or higher GPA overall.

Students who satisfy these requirements may apply to join PME at no cost.

Applications in October of each calendar year.
summary: Pi Mu Epsilon is dedicated to the promotion of mathematics and recognition of students who successfully pursue mathematical understanding. The Tennessee Zeta Chapter will specifically organize mathematically engaging events and induct members.

name: Arabic Language Club
summary: The mission of the Arabic Language Club is to foster an inclusive community for students interested in learning, practicing, and engaging with the Arabic language and culture.

name: Transfer Connect
description: Connect. Lead. Grow.
summary: Transfer Connect strives to provide a robust Transfer Student Orientation and help transfer students transition into the Vanderbilt community through our motto: Connect. Lead. Grow.

name: InclusAbility
description:
Led by the Office for Inclusive Excellence and in collaboration with several campus and community partners, InclusAbility provides interactive and educational programs that aim to highlight specific strengths and challenges for individuals with disabilities. We are creating a forum for education, addressing the marginalization of individuals with disabilities, and recognizing the disability community as an important facet of diversity.

Please join this page if you are interested in helping with InclusAbility events!
summary: InclusAbility provides interactive and educational programs that aim to highlight specific strengths and challenges for individuals with disabilities.

name: Vanderbilt Business and Medicine Club
description: Our organization’s mission and purpose is to educate the students of Vanderbilt on the myriad of business opportunities in the medical industry. At the time of writing, Vanderbilt has a low amount of official resources dedicated to helping students explore the intersection of business and medicine. Part of this reason is due to this vocational realm to be both niche and extremely diverse. VBAM will provide mentorship, professional development, and networking opportunities to students interested in any area of the healthcare and business field. The organization will host speakers and events that inform students on topics such as creating their own private practice, working in a hospital, consulting, investing, engaging in medical research, and the many other opportunities that present themselves. VBAM will strive to inform the members on the business information that the core science curriculum often omits in order to give members a more complete understanding of the field and to find their place in the future of business and medicine.
summary: We aim to provide Vanderbilt’s pre-med students with experiential learning opportunities to explore diverse applications of medicine outside of a traditional healthcare setting.

summary: Student Access aims to provide students with a welcoming, accessible campus experience.

summary: The mission of the Peabody Office of Student Development and Support Services (OSDSS) is to promote student development and success during your Peabody experience through a continuum of care, individual support and campus partnerships.

name: Investment and Securities Law Club
description: The Investment and Securities Club is a student organization for law students at Vanderbilt University Law School. As students with a common interest in the legal side of the investment world, the organization’s goals are to (1) provide a forum for students to discuss investment and securities law issues, (2) coordinate guest speakers to lecture on unique topics of interest to the club’s members, (3) promote and encourage students to develop expertise in federal and other securities regulation; the legal side of various forms of investment, such as, but not limited to, securities and debt investments, and (4) discuss the many forms of investment institutions, ranging from mutual funds and hedge funds, to private equity and venture capital, and more.
summary: The Investment and Securities Club is a student organization for law students at Vanderbilt University Law School where students may discuss and learn about investment and securities law issues through guest speakers and other club events.

name: Vandy Votes
description: Created in 2018, Vandy Votes is a student-led organization dedicated to increasing voter registration, voter turnout, and overall civic engagement in the Vanderbilt community through collaboration with students, faculty, and staff. To date, Vandy Votes has been involved in hosting voter registration drives, civic engagement speaker panels, absentee ballot parties, presidential and vice-presidential candidate debate watch parties, meaningful interactions with your candidate workshops, and constitution week activities on campus. In July 2020, we officially became a student organization while still working under OACS (Office of Active Citizenship and Service). Currently, Vandy Votes is creating a campus plan and working with many student organizations and communities to spread awareness about the upcoming elections and how to address civic engagement.
summary: Vandy Votes is a non-partisan student organization committed to increasing civic engagement through registration resources, education, strategic planning, and campus-wide voting coalitions.
