name: Voting Rights & Advocacy Society at Vanderbilt University Law School
description: The Voting Rights & Advocacy Society is a non-partisan group of Vanderbilt Law students whose mission is to support and defend free and fair elections in Tennessee and the United States writ large. We seek to engage the Vanderbilt student body in voting-advocacy efforts, to educate and assist Nashvillians who desire to vote, to end gerrymandering and voter suppression, to challenge efforts that disenfranchise Tennesseans, and to reform the electoral process to more accurately represent the people.
summary: The Voting Rights & Advocacy Society is a non-partisan group of Vanderbilt Law students whose mission is to support and defend free and fair elections in Tennessee and the United States writ large.

name: Student Advocates for Public Health
description:
Student Advocates for Public Health

 

Objective

The Student Advocates for Public Health (SAPH) is a university initiative established in partnership with the School of Nursing, the Health Professions Advisory Office, The Office of the Dean of Students, and the Office of the Vice Provost for Academic Affairs in an effort to engage students to promote the safest possible return to campus for every individual including faculty, staff member, and student.  The Advisory Council will work in collaboration with the SAPH Coordinators to establish a curriculum and facilitate discussions with university leadership to provide an opportunity for open dialogue throughout the semester.  Design of the curriculum will be focused on preparing a diverse group of student leaders who will propagate a “culture of caring” and open communication about safe public health behaviors as they specifically relate to transmission of COVID-19 on the Vanderbilt campus and in the Nashville community.

 

Structure

The Student Advocates for Public Health Advisory Council will serve as an advisory team of students to lead and fulfill this mission. The Advisory Council will report to the SAPH Coordinators and is comprised of 5-6 undergraduate students whose objective is to guide Student Advocates for Public Health (SAPH), a group of 40-50 student leaders integrated with in the Vanderbilt community in a diversity of settings who are trained to engage in meaningful dialogue with peers about safety behaviors that will protect the community at large. The SAPH Advisory Council will also serve as a student liaison to the Vanderbilt University Public Safety’s Public Health AmbassaDores program.

 

SAPH Advisory Council Coordinators:

Leah Branam, MSSW, LMSW, Assistant in Clinical Nursing

Michelle Grundy, Ph.D., Director, Health Professions Advisory Office, Assistant Professor, School of Medicine

Natasha McClure, DNP, PNP-BC, Associate Professor, School of Nursing

 

Activities

 

Training will equip the SAPH Advisory Council and Student Advocates for Public Health to engage in meaningful dialogue with their peers that mimic the conversations they are currently having with their family members and friends about behaviors that support safe, healthy living according to public health and safety guidelines and campus policies.

 

Positions on the SAPH Advisory Council are open to rising sophomores, juniors, and seniors.  SAPH positions are open to all undergraduate students currently enrolled who are living on campus or in off campus housing.  Both roles are open to all undergraduate students, although it may be an especially attractive and transformative experience for future healthcare providers, to prepare them to open, navigate, lead, and support challenging and sensitive conversations with their future patients.

 

Student Advocates for Public Health will operate as formalized role models, and will hold regular one-on-one or group meetings with their respective peer groups to discuss current challenges and successes in engaging in positive public health practices on campus.

 

The SAPH Advisory Council will hold weekly meetings with the Student Advocates for Public Health to reflect on these discussions and provide support for future peer engagement.

 

The SAPH Advisory Council will also serve as a support to address questions about behavior, expectations, and safety procedures on- and off-campus from undergraduate students through peer education conversations and/or advising for ways to alter or modify individual student or student organizations’ behaviors.  The SAPH Advisory Council will also serve as a liaison to the VUPS Public Heath AmbassaDORE program to provide an avenue for student feedback as the institution moves forward in these unprecedented times. 

 

The SAPH Advisory Council may have subcommittees that focus on:

 

  1. Recruitment of Student Advocates for Public Health
  2. Development of Education Program for Student Advocates for Public Health
  3. Student feedback on experiences and challenges in implementing safety measures

 

Student Advocates for Public Health and SAPH Advisory Council members will be selected through a competitive application process.

 

Timeline

  • Application posted (August 14-although applications will continue to be reviewed on a rolling basis)
  • Application review (will begin the week of August 24)
  • SAPH Advisory Council Interviews (week of August 31)
  • SAPH Advisory Council selections announced (Sept 7th)
  • SAPH selections announced (beginning Sept 7th, but selection will be rolling)
  • SAPH Advisory Council and SAPH training begins (week of September 7th)
  • Ongoing SAPH meetings with Advisory Council and SAPH
  • Ongoing SAPH engagement meetings with students

The SAPH Coordinators will provide support to the SAPH in their peer leadership role. The coordinators will create a space for student feedback to be heard, and for an open dialogue about student concerns. They will also cultivate leadership skills in the SAPH in understanding the context of the critical role that dialogue between SAPH and VU administrators can play in establishing a sense of shared responsibility for a positive public health culture on our campus.
summary: Student leaders who will propagate a “culture of caring” and open communication about safe public health behaviors as they specifically relate to transmission of COVID-19 on the Vanderbilt campus and in the Nashville community.

name: Nicholas S. Zeppos Residential College
description:
Jeong-Oh Kim, Faculty Head of College

Professor Kim teaches as a lecturer at Vanderbilt University, where he studied the histories and theories of geography and their application to nineteenth-century British literature. He completed his doctoral dissertation entitled A Sense of Place and the Uncertainty of the Self and has been working on a book project tentatively entitled Romantic Thresholds. This work is part of a larger exploration of geography and cartography that increasingly concerns scholars of 18th and 19th century across a range of fields. He has published on space in poetry and the novel and has presented at a wide range of international conferences. He was the recipient of a Mellon Foundation fellowship and provided a professional service to the Modern Language Association as a Delegate Assembly. As a future humanist interested in contributing to the English-speaking world, he intelligently absorbs Anglo-American literary culture and re-interprets it for scholars and students of diverse nationalities and disciplines.

The Residential College 

Opening in August 2020, Nicholas S. Zeppos College is located on the corner of West End Avenue and 25th Avenue South. Nicholas S. Zeppos College provides state-of-the-art accommodations inside gothic-style architecture. Nicholas S. Zeppos College will can house 335 upper-division students in four-person suites, traditional double rooms, and traditional single rooms.
summary: Nicholas S. Zeppos College

name: Vanderbilt Wrestling
description: The Vanderbilt Wrestling Club offers a great workout while helping members learn and sharpen wrestling skills. Open to all skill levels, the student-led club emphasizes physical, mental, and moral development, along with building a supportive community. Whether experienced or new to the sport, members will improve their technique, build strength, and connect with others who share a passion for wrestling. Practices are held twice a week and are designed to challenge and inspire growth. No experience is required—just bring a willingness to learn and a positive attitude.
summary: Join the Vanderbilt Wrestling Club! Whether you’re experienced or a beginner, our team is open to everyone. No experience? No problem! Learn, train, and grow while improving your skills, staying fit, and connecting with others. See you on the mat!

name: Vanderbilt Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu
description:
The mission of our organization is to bring the art of Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu to our Vanderbilt community.

 

 

What is Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu? A sport? A martial art? A fun pastime for friends? For enemies? For lovers?

Come find out and decide what it means to you.

 

NO PREVIOUS EXPERIENCE NEEDED: Just come with a learner's mindset, normal workout attire (rashguards if you wanna look flashy), and remove any dangling jewelry before stepping on the mat.

There are no mandatory dues and no demands for commitment. Come by for a day and decide if you like it, no hard feelings if you don't.

Have a suggestion for how we can improve our club activities? Talk to a member of the executive council. Inclusivity is crucial to our mission and it is often the outsider's voice who gives the most clarity towards this.

 

Practices are 7:30-9:00pm Tuesday and 2:00-4:00 Saturday in the SEC lobby of the Rec Center.
summary: The mission of the organization is to bring the art of Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu to the Vanderbilt community.

name: The Community Research and Action (CRA) Student Association
description: The Community Research and Action Student Association’s mission is to serve the needs of CRA graduate students and facilitate connection between CRA students and the wider university community. We serve as a forum for the discussion of matters pertaining to the well-being of graduate students, provide leadership opportunities for students, represent student opinions and concerns, foster unity and belonging through meetings and events (for CRA students as well as other graduate students, independently or through co-sponsorship), and aid in the recruitment of graduate students to our program and department.
summary: The Community Research and Action Student Association’s mission is to serve the needs of CRA graduate students and facilitate connection between CRA students and the wider university community.

name: Black Student Athlete Group
description: The Vanderbilt Black Student Athlete Group works to build up the communities of the underprivileged, stimulate the hearts and minds of the unlimited, amplify the voices of the unheard back to and affirm the culture of the unbreakable.
summary: The Vanderbilt Black Student Athlete Group works to build up the communities of the underprivileged, stimulate the hearts and minds of the unlimited, amplify the voices of the unheard back to and affirm the culture of the unbreakable.

name: Scholars of Finance
description:
Scholars of Finance is a rapidly growing organization on a mission to inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow.

We seek to solve the world’s largest problems by investing in undergraduate students through leadership development, mentorship, and the active support of hundreds of purpose-driven finance executives and investors.

Our members then join top firms, funds, and a life-long support community that empowers them to spend their careers serving the greater good using the biggest lever in the world: finance.

Application: https://app.scholarsoffinance.org

Instagram: @sof.vanderbiltu
summary: Scholars of Finance is a rapidly growing organization on a mission to inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow. We seek to solve the world’s largest problems by investing in undergraduate students.

name: Vanderbilt Minorities and Philosophy (MAP) Chapter
summary: MAP’s mission is to address structural injustices in academic philosophy and to remove barriers that impede participation in academic philosophy for members of marginalized groups.

name: Vanderbilt Students for Underserved Pre-Health Scholars
description: Vanderbilt Students for Underserved Pre-Health Scholars (VSUPS) is an undergraduate organization that seeks to inspire Nashville-area high school students of underserved communities to pursue pre-health careers. We hope to create change in the healthcare landscape in underrepresented communities, which is rife with disparities. We use a series of resources to achieve this goal, including: in-person lecture series with relevant speakers, interactive demonstrations, and one-on-one mentoring with a current Vanderbilt student of a relevant pre-health focus. The path towards a pre-health career can be difficult and stressful; with these tools we hope to guide our scholars towards success in their pre-health journey.
summary: Vanderbilt Students for Underserved Pre-Health Scholars (VSUPS) is an undergraduate organization that seeks to inspire Nashville-area high school students of underserved communities to pursue pre-health careers.

name: MW STEM (Minority Women in STEM)
description: Minority Women in STEM is a community of minority women dedicated to bridging the gap for the most underrepresented demographic throughout STEM fields: Minority Women. We strive to empower the next generation of female science professionals by connecting these students with women in STEM-related careers who represent them through speaker events, mentorship programs, and outreach opportunities. We not only aim to augment the future success of these students, but to also support communities that suffer from treatment disparities within science and public health, through community service projects.
summary: MW STEM is a community providing career development and educational resources for minority female students seeking STEM-related careers. Our definition of a minority woman is holistic in respect to ethnic, religious, sexual, and socioeconomic identity.

name: Gift of Life Vanderbilt
description:
Gift of Life is an international stem cell and marrow registry that finds life saving matches for blood cancer, inherited immune disorder, and sickle-cell patients. Our goal is to help you SWAB TO SAVE! We use noninvasive cheek swabs to add you to the registry. 18-25 year olds are 90% more requested by donor centers, so we have a real opportunity to save lives and make a difference as college students. Our volunteers run swab drives and information sessions around campus and Nashville. Last year, we ran over 20 drives, including Light the Night with the Leukemia and Lymphoma Society, William Morris Endeavor Talent Agency, and three nights at Nashville Predators games. We have been incredibly successful so far, having swabbed 5665 Vanderbilt students and Nashville residents, found 209 matches, and facilitated 18 transplants. Join our Anchorlink group to receive updates on events to volunteer at!

 

You can swab at www.giftoflife.org/swab
summary: Gift of Life is an international stem cell and marrow registry that finds life saving matches for blood cancer, inherited immune disorder, and sickle-cell patients.

name: Justice Through Tech
description:
These weekly sessions are the kind of experience that feels good in the moment and is meaningful for your future too!

 

What our sessions are like
Each week at Dismas House, we run relaxed, hands-on sessions on email, resumes, coding, and whatever else residents are curious about.

​

Learning by doing
Everyone has a computer, and we move through tasks together instead of just talking at people. There is a lot of troubleshooting, but also celebrations for the small wins when something finally “clicks” on the screen.

 

Why it matters
For residents, stronger tech skills make job searches, training programs, and everyday online life feel more possible. For students, it turns an interest in technology into real relationships and impact in Nashville, while giving you concrete stories and skills to highlight on your job applications.
summary: We partner with the Dismas House to address Nashville’s tech access gap and employment barriers facing people returning from incarceration. We provide free technology training that helps residents build essential skills for work and daily life.

name: SKY Campus Happiness Program
description:
Join us for our weekly free meditations, yoga sessions, and meetings to learn powerful breathing techniques, and other activities focused on increasing our general wellbeing.
We are also going to have the SKY Happiness Retreat a couple of times this semester, which is our main program. These are going to be done with other universities as well, so it's a great way to connect with students from other places. This Retreat (for now is an online program only) is a 3-day powerful program that is being conducted in several universities already.
Check this great video about the program:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Vlu3arLc0WE&feature=youtu.be
summary: SKY Campus is an organization that already operates in several campuses and colleges across the country. - As a new student organization in Vanderbilt we will be conducting regular free on-line activities such as meditations, yoga sessions, and others.

name: The Women's Network
description: The Women’s Network (TWN) was created for ambitious women looking to grow professionally while being lifted by their community. TWN offers opportunities to gain exposure to the professional world, network with high-profile speakers, and meet peers around their campus and the country. TWN meets biweekly (twice a month) and holds speaker meetings, various networking events, resume, internship and LinkedIn workshops, alumni networking receptions, and interactive activity sessions to develop relationships with fellow members. Our workshops and guest speakers help prepare you for your next steps and may open your eyes to paths you haven't even considered.
summary: The Women’s Network is a networking organization that celebrates ambition and connects women to fellow high-achieving, noncompetitive peers on their respective college campuses and to seasoned female professionals in various fields.

name: American Association for Men in Nursing (Vanderbilt Chapter) Affinity Group
description:
Mission

To shape the practice, education, research, and leadership for men in nursing and advance men’s health.

Vision

To be the association of choice representing men in nursing.

The purpose of AAMN is to provide a framework for nurses, as a group, to meet, to discuss and influence factors, which affect men as nurses.

Organizational Structure

AAMN is a national organization with local chapters recognized and sanctioned under the Bylaws of AAMN. However Chapters may have independent bylaws and a separate dues structure. Membership in the national organization does not require membership in a local chapter.

AAMN is governed by the board of directors who, under the organizational bylaws have the right and responsibility to administer the business activities of the organization to include decisions related to the expenditure of organizational funds.

AAMN's Objectives Include...

Encourage men of all ages to become nurses and join together with all nurses in strengthening and humanizing health care.

Support men who are nurses to grow professionally and demonstrate to each other and to society the increasing contributions being made by men within the nursing profession.

Advocate for continued research, education and dissemination of information about men's health issues, men in nursing, and nursing knowledge at the local and national levels.

Support members' full participation in the nursing profession and its organizations and use this Association for the limited objectives stated above.
summary:
Mission
To shape the practice, education, research, and leadership for men in nursing and advance men’s health.
 
Vision
To be the association of choice representing men in nursing.

name: DNP Cultural Connections Network
description:
The Vanderbilt University School of Nursing (VUSN) Doctor of Nursing Practice (DNP) Cultural Connections Network aims to bring together DNP students of all backgrounds to celebrate and advocate for diversity in the nursing profession. This organization welcomes DNP students who support diversity, inclusion, and anti-racism. We also serve as a platform for DNP students who identify with an underrepresented group in nursing. We embrace and celebrate all ethnicities, races, nationalities, gender identities, cultures, sexual orientations, and abilities that create a diverse community of nurse leaders and scholars within the VUSN DNP program. 

We are intentional in our vision to optimize the visibility, recruitment, and support of underrepresented and diverse nurse leaders by promoting their unique attributes and contributions to the nursing profession. Our organization provides a safe and supportive space for gaining insight and understanding of intersectionality in healthcare through social engagement, networking, and professional learning opportunities to improve the quality of healthcare delivered to multiple and diverse patient populations. The VUSN DNP Cultural Connections Network aims to help nurse leaders broaden their perspectives on emancipatory knowing to foster strategies that increase inclusion, equity, and embrace diversity in all of their professional domains.
summary: The Vanderbilt University School of Nursing (VUSN) Doctor of Nursing Practice (DNP) Student Diversity Affinity Group aims to bring together DNP nurse leaders of all backgrounds to celebrate and advocate for diversity support in the nursing profession.

name: Nurses for Global Health (NGH)
description: Nurses for Global Health at Vanderbilt University School of Nursing strives to educate and advocate for equitable and sustainable change through the lens of healthcare.
summary: Nurses for Global Health at Vanderbilt University School of Nursing strives to educate and advocate for equitable and sustainable change through the lens of healthcare.

name: Katie's Art Project
description: The mission of KAP is to connect children facing life-threatening illnesses with emerging and established artists through collaborative programs to create a lasting legacy through art.

name: Vanderbilt Sports Business Club
description: Formerly the Vanderbilt Sports Marketing Club, the purpose of the organization is to educate Vanderbilt students on the many opportunities in the sports business industry. The VSBC will provide mentorship, professional development, and networking opportunities to students interested in any area of the sports business field. Areas will include marketing, finance, management among others. VSBC will consult Vanderbilt athletics to identify problems and provide solutions aimed to improve the overall department. The club’s goal is to build the Vanderbilt athletics fan base, using marketing and business strategies to attract a larger demographic to games and increase fan engagement. The club will host speakers and events that inform students on topics such as how professional organizations build their brands, how to create a franchise culture, and how to develop business strategies.
summary: Vanderbilt Sports Business Club aims to create a community of students who are passionate about sports and pursuing careers in the industry. VSBC provides mentorship, professional development, and networking opportunities to its members.

name: Vandy Graduate Tennis Club
summary: We provide a social network for all graduate and professional students, faculty, staff and their dependents through recreational and competitive tennis events.

name: Association of Vanderbilt Black Faculty and Staff
description: The Association of Vanderbilt Black Faculty and Staff (AVBFS) has a three-fold purpose: 1) to support and uplift Black Faculty and Staff, 2) to build and maintain a sense of community among Black faculty and staff, and 3) to advocate for the recruitment, retention, and advancement of Black faculty and staff at Vanderbilt. The AVBFS is currently housed under the umbrella of the Bishop Joseph Johnson Black Cultural Center. Membership is open to all levels of faculty and staff at Vanderbilt who are interested in and supportive of the mission and objectives of the AVBFS.
summary: The Association of Vanderbilt Black Faculty and Staff (AVBFS) has a three-fold purpose: 1) to support and uplift Black Faculty and Staff, 2) to build and maintain a sense of community among Black faculty and staff, and 3) to advocate for the recruitment,

description: Men as Allies is a professional organization at the Owen Graduate School of Management meant to provide graduate business students a forum to discuss issues in the workplace and the opportunity, resources, and support to initiate and implement change. This organization will partner closely with the Women’s Business Association (WBA) at the Owen Graduate School of Management and other student organizations (e.g., Out and Allied) for co-sponsorship of various events and will also sponsor independent events for the benefit of its members and the broader Owen community.
summary: Men as Allies is a professional organization at the Owen Graduate School of Management meant to provide students of graduate business students a forum to discuss gender issues and implement change.

summary: The Office of Faculty Affairs oversees and promotes data-driven initiatives to improve faculty recruitment, development, promotion and retention. The Office administers many faculty funding initiatives derived from the Academic Strategic Plan, including

name: Mexican American Student Association
description:
¡Hola!

We are MASA, the Mexican American Student Association at Vanderbilt University. MASA is a lively and inviting community for all Vanderbilt students, regardless of whether you identify as Mexican, Mexican American, or simply wish to learn more about and celebrate Mexican culture.

Our mission is to elevate and mirror the richness and diversity of the Mexican experience. Through cultural events, guest speakers, professional development, and community outreach, we foster an environment for empowerment, connection, and enjoyment.

We are here to foster community and enhance the visibility of the Mexican experience throughout the campus. All Vanderbilt students are invited to join us on Anchor Link to remain connected and be part of our familia!
summary: Mexican American Students Association (MASA)

name: Hearts for the Homeless Nashville
description: Hearts for the Homeless International contributes to the health and well-being of one of the most vulnerable populations in our nation with free heart health screenings, educational events, and by providing outreach opportunities for students who wish to contribute to our effort. By partnering with food banks and neighborhood clinics in Nashville, we hope to raise awareness about important health issues within the homeless population, connect with our community, and drive demonstrable impact.
summary: Hearts for the Homeless International contributes to the health and well-being of one of the most vulnerable populations in our nation with free heart health screenings, educational events, and by providing outreach opportunities for interested students.

name: Vanderbilt Pre-Law Society
description: The Vanderbilt University Pre-Law Society aims to educate undergraduate students about career opportunities in law and provide them with the resources to be successful in the law school application process and professional careers beyond. By facilitating educational opportunities and providing greater access to resources, VPLS hopes to create a lasting support infrastructure and community for pre-law students at Vanderbilt.
summary: The Vanderbilt University Pre-Law Society aims to be an accessible and inclusive organization that provides students with opportunities and resources to enhance their prospects for law school admission.

name: The Vanderbilt Project on Unity and American Democracy
description: The Vanderbilt Project on Unity and American Democracy is a nonpartisan initiative that aims to elevate research and evidence-based reasoning in the national discourse. The core contributions of the project are the dissemination of research and evidence-based papers from renowned thought leaders and timely and crucial conversations with influencers of all political persuasion. The timely endeavor aims to give policy makers and the public the tools needed to combat conspiracy and unfounded ideology with evidence, data and respectful discourse.
summary: The Vanderbilt Project on Unity and American Democracy is a nonpartisan initiative that aims to elevate research and evidence-based reasoning in the national discourse.

name: Department of Biomedical Engineering: Brunger Lab at Vanderbilt
summary: VU BME research lab run by Dr. Brunger

name: Opening Statement: Vanderbilt First Generation Lawyers
description: The purpose of Opening Statement is to celebrate the accomplishments of first-generation law students and honor the challenges they face. It seeks to empower first-generation law students to thrive not just at Vanderbilt Law School, but in the legal profession overall. A first-generation law student is broadly defined as the first person in his/her/their family to attend law school. Opening Statement strongly encourages membership from students who identify as the first in their families to immigrate to the United States, the first in their families to have graduated college, or the first in their families to have graduated high school. However, Opening Statement is open to all law students. It welcomes membership from any students who feel they may benefit from joining a community of first-generation students, including those hailing from lower-income or working-class backgrounds. Opening Statement’s mission is to maximize its members’ success and happiness in the profession by fostering friendship, candid dialogues, and mentorship among its members and the greater community.
summary: Opening Statement is an organization for first-generation law students at Vanderbilt.

name: Peabody APIDA (Asian Pacific Islander Desi American)
description:
APIDA is a passionate group of Asian Pacific Islander Desi American graduate students at Peabody College that:

  • Provides support for and empowers Asian identifying students at Peabody College.
  • Hosts a space in which discussions of Asian experiences and mental health in the context of American culture and more specifically, Peabody College, are held.
  • Promotes awareness about significant issues and advocates for the benefit of the larger Asian community at Vanderbilt.
  • Serves as representation for Peabody College’s Asian student body.
summary: Peabody APIDA is professional and graduate student organization whose mission is to support and empower APIDA identifying students through hosting inclusive spaces to discuss Asian American experiences and promote cultural awareness.

name: Frist Center for Autism and Innovation
summary: From a strengths-based understanding of autism and neurodiversity, the Center sees opportunities for innovation in technology and in workplace practices.

name: American Muslim Medical Student Association
description:
This organization is only open to VUSM MD students.

 

AMMSA is open to medical students of all backgrounds and is dedicated to advocating for Muslim patient populations through addressing pertinent religious and cultural issues. AMMSA also serves to bring together Muslim medical students through volunteerism, social activities, and academic/personal support. AMMSA seeks to: 

  • Enhance the understanding and appreciation of Islamic culture, especially with regards to medical care.
  • Encourage professional interaction between Muslim physicians and other health care providers and physicians.
  • Facilitate the learning and practice of Islam among students.
  • Participate in medical community service and other charitable activities.
  • Provide mentorship to Muslim undergraduate students who are interested in pursuing medicine.
summary: American Muslim Medical Student Association is dedicated to raising awareness of the Muslim patient populations through addressing pertinent religious and cultural issues. They also serves to bring together Muslim medical students.

name: Bass Military Scholars Program
description: Bass Military Scholars participate in campus activities designed to foster the military-civilian relationships and engage students by sharing their wide range of military experiences, values, character, and mission focus. The program values academic excellence, distinguished military service, and positively impacting society.
summary: Bass Military Scholars participate in campus activities designed to foster the military-civilian relationships and engage students by sharing their wide range of military experiences, values, character, and mission focus.

description: Our mission is to build and grow a productive entrepreneurial ecosystem conducive to creating new and innovative businesses. The center accomplishes the mission through structured programs, financial support through grants and scholarships, and network connections through alumni in Nashville, throughout the US, and across the world.
summary: Our mission is to build and grow a productive entrepreneurial ecosystem conducive to creating new and innovative businesses.

name: Peabody Latinx Alumni and Student Association
description: PLASA - Peabody Latinx Alumni and Student Association aims to connect alumni and students of Latinx backgrounds at Peabody College to foster a supportive community rich with culture. PLASA serves as an organization dedicated to establishing meaningful relationships and mentorships with fellow Latinx alumni and students in educational spaces. PLASA’s goals include organizing to ensure our academic spaces are inclusive. We want to address how Latinx people are represented and treated in all academic institutions and what we can do to enact change.
summary: PLASA - Peabody Latinx Alumni and Student Association aims to connect alumni and students of Latinx backgrounds at Peabody College to foster a supportive community rich with culture and enacting change in educational spaces.

name: V:Pog
description:
V:Pog's goal is to make the complex aspects of video game development more accessible and understandable to anyone with an interest. We work specifically on personal and group projects giving members a way to display their work and skills in a portfolio to show potential employers. Welcoming a variety of majors (including outside of STEM), backgrounds, and experience levels, V:Pog provides a community and space for those with a shared love for games and game development.

Join our discord in order to receive updates and communicate with other V:Pog Members!

Discord Link: https://discord.gg/sWJFdhaJW9
summary: V:Pog hosts a variety of events for students interested in video game development and provides members with support in bettering their skillset and working on personal or group projects.

name: VUSN Military and Veteran Student Affinity Group
description: The VUSN Military and Veteran Student Nurses Affinity Group is an organization formed to support those students at VUSN that are active duty military, veterans, and military spouses.  We provide an inclusive environment discuss any issues that may arise while in attendance at VUSN, and be a point of contact and problem solving for those students.  We support several initiatives throughout the school year to support various student lead initiatives.
summary: The VUSN Military and Veteran Student Affinity Group is an organization formed to support those students at VUSN that are active duty military, veterans, and military spouses.

name: Vanderbilt Coptic Medical Student Association
description: The Vanderbilt Coptic Medical Student Association is dedicated to serving the Nashville community while weaving the rich values of Coptic Orthodoxy into every facet of life, especially medicine. We are driven by a passion for fellowship, a commitment to education, and a deep-rooted desire to engage with our community. If you’re inspired by service, uplifted by spirituality, energized by meaningful connections, and eager to make a difference locally, this is the perfect organization for you!
summary: Our mission is to serve the Nashville community while naturally bringing the values of Coptic Orthodoxy into everything we do, especially in medicine. We’re rooted in fellowship, education, and a genuine connection with the community.

name: Ceramics Club
summary: A club for anyone who loves creating with clay! We provide an environment to create and to make friends!

name: Neurodiversity Student Nurse Advocacy Group
description: VUSN’s Neurodiversity Student Nurses Advocacy Group welcomes all students interested in promoting mental health awareness at VUSN and the Greater Nashville Community through education, advocacy, and outreach initiatives. We offer an inclusive space for all VUSN students. Whether personally impacted by mental health concerns or an ally—we invite your participation!  The purpose of VUSN’s Neurodiversity Student Nurses Advocacy Group is to deepen our understanding of the diverse lived experiences of folx experiencing mental health concerns and take actionable steps to break down stigmas associated with mental health differences at VUSN, in the Greater Nashville Community, and in our future careers as nurse practitioners.
summary: The Neurodiversity Student Nurses Advocacy Group is a VUSN Affinity Group for Vanderbilt nursing students interested in education, advocacy, and outreach initiatives within our school and the Nashville community.

name: Product Space
description:
About Us

Product Space @ Vanderbilt was founded in Spring 2021 to cultivate tomorrow's product leaders by providing Vanderbilt with a product community dedicated to the intersection of business and technology. New products are the lifeblood of any company and the cornerstone of any economy. We teach students from all backgrounds how to create and analyze Products and offer a Space for students to apply their skills in product management.

 

Our Mission

Our mission is to cultivate tomorrow's product leaders. We take a three-step approach to accomplishing this:

  • Educate

    Product Management remains a lesser known career path at Vandy but is in fact a good fit for many students' skillsets and interests. Through campus-wide events (such as our PM 101 Panel and Insight Series), we hope to raise awareness of this career path.

  • Grow

    If you are interested in product, we hope to grow with you and help you break into PM through our Product Fellowship program. No matter your skill level or background, you will walk away from the program as a top-tier PM.

  • Support

    Recruitment is hard. We all have moments of self-doubt and frustration. That's why we want to build an open, supportive community where we can share our successes as well as failures, so you don't have to go through this journey alone.

 

We're excited to meet you - come learn with us.

 

With 💜  -

Product Space @ Vanderbilt
summary: Product Space aims to cultivate tomorrow’s product leaders by providing Vanderbilt with a product community dedicated to the intersection of business and technology. Come learn with us - vandy.link/product

name: Vanderbilt Student Athlete Advisory Committee
description: Vanderbilt Student-Athlete Advisory Committee is a group of student-athletes meeting to provide opportunities to the athletic committee and enhance student-athlete experience.
summary: (insert summary here)Vanderbilt Student-Athlete Advisory Committee is a group of student-athletes meeting to provide opportunities to the athletic committee and enhance student-athlete experience.

name: Alpha Eta Mu Beta - Biomedical Engineering Honor Society
description: We are the Vanderbilt chapter of a national honor society that is open to junior and senior biomedical engineering students who are within the top 20% of their class. AEMB is the only BME honor society on campus, and we hope to leverage this organization to facilitate mentorship, research experiences, and community outreach for Vanderbilt students interested in biomedical engineering.
summary: We are the Vanderbilt chapter of a national honor society that is open to junior and senior biomedical engineering students who are within the top 20% of their class

name: Vanderbilt International Researchers Alliance
description:
NEW MEMBERS: Please take 5 minutes to fill out the member intake form (linked below) so we can hear from you what kinds of work you'd like to see from VIRAL!

Welcome to the Vanderbilt International Researchers Alliance!

We are a group of researchers (so, technicians, graduate students, post-docs, faculty, staff and everyone in between) dedicated to supporting, celebrating and expanding the community of international scientists at Vanderbilt University! 

The overarching goal of the Vanderbilt International Researchers Alliance (VIRAL) is to support and grow the community of international researchers at Vanderbilt and promote a space where to come together to celebrate and promote the diversity of our scientific community. Our organization provides four main "pillars" of support to the international community:

 

·       Lobby for the interests of international researchers to university administration in a unified fashion across Vanderbilt.
·       Foster a community which supports international researchers across labs, departments and programs.
·       Provide transition support for incoming international researchers.
·       Create a more tangible environment to encourage domestic researchers to engage with the international community and understand our unique circumstances as researchers in the US.

 

Who's welcome to join? Anyone! We are an inclusive community whose one requirement is a passion or interest in seeing the multicultural community of Vanderbilt grow beyond all borders.
summary: We aim to foster a community to support international trainees throughout their academic and professional development and promote an open dialogue between domestic and international researchers at Vanderbilt.

name: Vanderbilt Nepali Students Association
description:
The purpose of the Vanderbilt Nepali Students Association (VNSA) is to educate the Vanderbilt community about Nepal and Nepali culture and to help international students from Nepal in cultural, academic, and social adjustment at Vanderbilt University. VNSA plans to do so by organizing various cultural events, seminars, and social events throughout the year. 

The events organized by VNSA will provide a culturally immersive experience to Vanderbilt students and will allow them to learn about Nepali heritage. VNSA will also provide a platform for Vanderbilt students to discuss social, cultural, and political issues regarding Nepal.
summary: The purpose of the Vanderbilt Nepali Students Association (VNSA) is to educate the Vanderbilt community about Nepal and Nepali culture and to help international students from Nepal in cultural, academic, and social adjustment at Vanderbilt University.

name: Dores for Hearts
description: Our organization intends to promote healthy lifestyles among college students and community members of the Greater Nashville area with an emphasis on heart health. We aim to serve and further the mission and goals of the American Heart Association within Vanderbilt and the surrounding community.
summary: Dores for Hearts is partnered with the American Heart Association. Our organization promotes heart healthy lifestyles among Vanderbilt students and the greater Nashville community.

name: United Against Inequities in Disease
description:
United Against Inequities in Disease is a national student-led organization focused on ameliorating health inequities in the metro-Nashville area. The Vanderbilt UAID chapter leverages a public-health lens to couple community-focused research and local partnerships to identify community health needs and fight for a healthier Tennessee. 

We have 2 subteams: a research and a community projects. We have multiple projects available for involvement; past examples of our projects include: interviewing community stakeholders on the barriers to diabetes care in Middle TN and distributing STI test kits at local community events. Our research and community work culminates into one or more manuscript(s) every year, submitted to UAID's Journal of Health Equity and presented at UAID's annual Health Equity Leadership Confeence. You can read about our past work here: https://www.uaid.org/journal. 

No past experience is required for membership; we will introduce key research concepts and skills needed to perform our work. Meetings are biweekly. If you are interested, follow our Instagram (@vandyuaid), join our Anchorlink (United Against Inequities in Disease), or find us at the annual club fair!
summary: United Against Inequities in Disease is a student-led organization focused on alleviating health inequities in the metro-Nashville area.
