summary: Landing page for GRC

name: College of Arts & Science Dean's Office
summary: Ongoing list of events hosted for faculty, students and staff in the College of Arts & Science

name: James Lawson Institute for the Research and Study of Nonviolent Movements
description:
Vision: 

The James Lawson Institute will advance the power of nonviolence to address systemic injustice for sustained social change around the world.

Mission: 

The James Lawson Institute honors the legacy of Rev. James M. Lawson and non-violent movements. We inspire people to engage in preparation, dialogue, and research for social justice.

Aims: 

Towards these ends, we offer an array of programming including symposia, community forums, training in the methods of non-violence, research colloquy, as well as credit and non-credit bearing education.
summary: The James Lawson Institute fosters research, dialogue on the pressing social issues of the day and education rooted in nonviolent strategies and teachings of Rev. James Lawson.

name: Camp Kesem at Vanderbilt University
description: Camp Kesem is a national non-profit across college campuses dedicated to supporting kids through and beyond their parents' cancer. Throughout the year, we fundraise & host volunteer events to prepare for our week-long, sleep-away camp during the summer.
summary: Camp Kesem is a national non-profit across college campuses dedicated to supporting kids through and beyond their parents' cancer. Throughout the year, we fundraise & host volunteer events to prepare for our week-long, sleep-away camp during the summer.

name: Student Advocates for Women in Medicine
description: Student Advocates for Women in Medicine is an organization that seeks to spark discussions about current issues that women face in healthcare today—both as providers and patients. We aspire to foster a strong, supportive community of undergraduate students and to use our individual abilities and passions to collectively support women's healthcare needs in the broader Nashville community.
summary: Student Advocates for Women in Medicine is an organization that seeks to spark discussions about current issues that womxn face in healthcare today—both as providers and patients.

name: Undergraduate Data Science
description: A unique undergraduate minor in data science in the College of Connected Computing.
summary: A unique undergraduate minor in data science in the College of Connected Computing.

name: Vandy EDA (Eating Disorder Awareness)
description: Eating Disorder Awareness Club at Vanderbilt works to challenge harmful norms and stereotypes, promote healing and empathy, and spread awareness while building community.
summary: EDA (Eating Disorders Awareness) is a strong voice on campus that spreads awareness about eating disorders. We aim to challenge harmful norms surrounding students' relationships with food, exercise and their bodies, and foster a supportive community.

name: Vanderbilt Culinary Club
description:
Vanderbilt Culinary Club is a vibrant undergraduate community where food becomes a bridge between cultures, memories, and friendships. Through shared cooking experiences, members develop their culinary skills while exploring how food creates belonging and resonates across diverse backgrounds. Our organization celebrates cooking as an art, science, and cultural expression that brings people together.

We foster connection through hands-on cooking lessons, visits from professional chefs, curated dining experiences, and community service initiatives. Each gathering becomes an opportunity to share family recipes, cultural traditions, and personal stories—creating a space where every student can find their place at the table. Whether you're an experienced cook or just beginning your culinary journey, VCC offers a welcoming environment to explore how food shapes identity, builds community, and creates lasting bonds among Vanderbilt students.
summary: Vanderbilt Culinary Club is the organization for undergraduates to express and develop their love for cooking and appreciation of food as an art science, and cultural phenomenon. Experiences include lessons, chef visits, dining experiences, and charity.

name: Rem and Company
description: Rem and Company is a student-led, nonprofit consulting organization dedicated to supporting small businesses in Nashville’s underserved communities. We offer students valuable, hands-on business experience while providing transformative support to local entrepreneurs.
summary: Rem and Company is a student-led, nonprofit consulting group that works with small businesses in Nashville's underserved communities. We provide hands-on business experience to students and life-changing support for small businesses in our community.

name: Vanderbilt Data Science Institute Student Council
description: The Data Science Student Council (DSISC) exists to enhance the overall educational experience of the Master of Science in Data Science program at Vanderbilt University by promoting the general welfare and concerns of the DSI student body in the following areas: academic, professional, social, community. This is achieved through the creation of new programs and initiatives to provide opportunities for growth and interaction, as well as through communication with the Vanderbilt faculty and administration on behalf of DSI students. The DSISC consists of annually elected student representatives. All Vanderbilt DSI Master's students are welcome and encouraged to attend DSISC’s bi-weekly meetings and to get involved.
summary: The DSISC enhances the overall educational experience of the DSI in the interest of its Master's students through providing opportunities for student growth and interaction as well as communication with the Vanderbilt faculty and administration.

name: Vanderbilt Architecture Society
description:
The Vanderbilt Architecture Society or VArch is a student-led organization at Vanderbilt University, committed to deepening the understanding and appreciation of architecture, space, and design among its members and the broader Vanderbilt and Nashville community. This society serves as a hub for students interested in the diverse facets of architectural studies, from historical perspectives to contemporary design and urban planning.

 

The members of the Vanderbilt Architecture Society benefit from a variety of enriching activities including guest speaker lectures from distinguished architects and scholars, hands-on workshops that delve into practical design techniques, and insightful tours of significant architectural sites/firms in Nashville. The society also facilitates discussions and panels that explore the intersection of architecture with other disciplines, such as environmental science, engineering, and the arts.

 

Bringing creative souls together, VArch's overarching goals and purposes are outlined below:

 

I. DIALOGUE

To foster dialogue and a community driven by the exploration of space and design

 

II. GUIDANCE

To provide guidance to those seeking a career in architecture and design

 

III. COMMUNITY

To make the exploration of space and design accessible, engaging and fun for all members of the Vanderbilt undergraduate student body

 

IV. NETWORKING

To grow a network of individuals and organizations passionate about the field in both Vanderbilt and Nashville
summary: The Vanderbilt Architecture Society, VArch in short, is a driven dynamic community that supports the exploration of space and design at Vanderbilt and within greater Nashville.

name: The Vanderbilt Fusion Project
description: The Vanderbilt Fusion Project is a transformative, cross-disciplinary engineering project team. By building a functional nuclear fusion reactor, we hope to spark innovation and inspire others - both within our campus community and nationally. The primary goal of our project isn’t just to design, fabricate, and develop a fusion reactor; it’s to create a project team that will give our team members the background and skills to become top-notch engineers, innovators, and problem-solvers.
summary: The Vanderbilt Fusion Project is a cross-disciplinary engineering project team designing and constructing a miniature nuclear fusion reactor. More than just building a reactor, we’re developing top-notch engineers, innovators, and problem-solvers.

name: K-Singers Club
description: K-Singers Club (KSC) is an up and coming student organization dedicated to bringing together enthusiasts of Korean music—K-pop, K-indie, K R&B, K-drama original soundtracks (OSTs), and more! Open to individuals of all singing backgrounds, we aim to promote an inclusive and welcoming environment where individuals can bond over a shared passion for Korean music. We hope that this organization will inspire you to connect with fellow K-music enthusiasts!
summary: KSC aims to bring together enthusiasts of Korean music—K-pop, K-indie, K R&B, K-drama original soundtracks (OSTs), and more! We aim to share our passion for Korean music & singing through an uplifting community, such as through covers, karaoke, and etc.

name: Vandy Sports Business and Analytics Club
description: At the Sports Business and Analytics Club, we focus on the analytical, statistical end of sports. We aim to create an environment for discussion about statistics as they relate to sports and how statistics can be used to make educated predictions about the outcomes of sporting events. The analytics side of sports has become more and more relevant in the sports industry. All broadcasts provide you with a variety of statistics during games, some of which are important in determining the favorite team to win. Our organization's mission is to introduce this field to students and help them understand and gain experience if they are interested in pursuing a career in the highly in-demand sports analytics field.
summary: Our organization will combine students' interests in athletics and data statistics. It will help students enrich their knowledge of sports by applying skills from the classroom. We aim to help students learn more about sports business.

name: Alpha Psi Lambda
description:
The mission statement of Alpha Psi Lambda emphasizes the importance we place on academics, diversity, and community involvement.

Eight Purposes

The Eight Purposes are the foundation of Alpha Psi Lambda. These are the basis for the achievements that each member strives to fulfill.

  1. Serve the university campus and community.
  2. Bring forth on a scholastic level, Hispanics/Latinos and others at the university on the basis of friendship.
  3. Provide a supplementary educational, social, and cultural experience for the mutual cultivation of its members.
  4. Advocate for the needs and concerns of the Hispanic/Latino students.
  5. Promote a basis for the diversity of the Hispanic/Latino cultures.
  6. Enhance the educational experience through social and cultural activities, and assist in the development of members' leadership abilities.
  7. Promote inter-fraternity relations.
  8. Foster positive relations between the Greek system, University/College Administration, and the outside community.

COED LATINX FRATERNITY

Gender Inclusive, Not Latinx Exclusive
summary: The mission statement of Alpha Psi Lambda emphasizes the importance we place on academics, diversity, and community involvement. To promote continued personal and collective growth of our membership, success and unity through education, leadership.

name: VUSM Cutting Edge
description: Vanderbilt University School of Medicine Cutting Edge is a medical student-run organization with a goal to expose Vanderbilt's medical community to emerging areas of biomedical research through events with renowned experts. This may include speaking engagements, formal research events, and social mixers. Founded by first-year Vanderbilt medical students Austin Hilvert and Elizabeth Simonds in 2022.
summary: The goal of VUSM Cutting Edge is to expose Vanderbilt's medical community to emerging areas of biomedical research through events with renowned experts.

name: Commodore Capital Group
summary: Commodore Capital Group is Vanderbilt's leading undergraduate private equity and venture capital organization. CCG spearheads immersion, education, and networking programs that allow students to further their understanding of private financial markets.

name: Center for the Study of Democratic institutions
summary: CSDI was established in 2009 to support and disseminate systematic theoretical and empirical research on questions central to the health and survival of democratic institutions in the United States and abroad.

name: Project Outdoors
description: Vanderbilt undergraduate students work together to bring outdoor education lessons to students in Nashville. Lessons at Eakin Elementary and Warner Arts Magnet Elementary are bi-weekly. Both programs include topics such as Leave No Trace, setting up tents, wilderness first aid, survival techniques, edible plants, planting plants, nature walks, bugs, climate change, and more. As a volunteer, you do not need any prior knowledge of these subjects!
summary: Project Outdoors is committed to providing outdoor and environmental education to elementary school students in Nashville. We currently work with Warner Elementary and Eakin Elementary teaching creative and hands-on lessons!

name: VUSM Drug Policy Group
description: The Vanderbilt Drug Policy Group focuses on increasing education and awareness of the social cost of the war on drugs, advocating for policy reform, and organizing students from diverse backgrounds in the legislative process to benefit residents of Middle Tennessee. We join thousands of other students around the globe in the promotion of compassionate, evidence-based drug laws over counterproductive policies at the institutional and local levels. We serve to protect the health and dignity of people who use drugs rather than stigmatize and punish them.
summary: We represent students from diverse backgrounds advocating for compassionate and evidence-based approaches to treating and preventing substance use disorders.

name: Department of Human and Organizational Development
summary: The Department of Human and Organizational Development (HOD) is a group of interdisciplinary programs that share a focus on organizations and the people within them.

name: VandyLifts
description:
Visit our website vandylifts.com for extra information about the club! If you want to learn how to lift, teach others how to life, or are looking for a workout partner, fill out the corresponding Mentee, Mentor, or Buddy Application!

If you want to try out being a mentor or mentee (trial-run, zero commitment afterwards), fill out the Day in the Life of a Lifter Application!

**Mentor/Mentee and Buddy pairings come out on a rolling basis as people fill out the form. Day in the Life of a Lifter Pairings come out by Sunday night for the upcoming week. 

If you're not paired right away, don't worry! On our instagram @vandylifts_, we post exercise and machine tutorials, as well as workout splits to get you started:) Feel free to reach out to our Exec Team for extra information on body neutrality (speed sessions hosted throughout the school year) and nutrition/supplementation.

We also have fun social sports tournaments, and social excursions to expand our fitness experiences! All events are posted in the GroupMe.
summary: VandyLifts puts students who want to learn how to lift, want to help others learn to lift, or just want a workout partner in contact with one another to make going to the Rec less intimidating and more. Plus, you'll learn how to best fuel your body!

name: Golden Dores (Employee Affinity Group for those 50+)
description: Golden Dores welcomes employees who are age 50 years or older, as well as allies. The group hosts meetings covering topics such as Medicare/Social Security workshops, investing, eldercare, retirement and the like. They also host fun social and service-oriented events. For more information or to join, contact Valerie Lorusso or Karen Swoopes.
summary: Golden Dores welcomes employees who are age 50 years or older, as well as allies. The group hosts meetings covering topics such as Medicare/Social Security workshops, investing, eldercare, retirement and the like.

name: Vanderbilt Taekwondo
description: Vanderbilt Taekwondo offers specialized training in each division in Taekwondo, including Sports Poomsae, Sparring, Tricking, and Self-defense. In these classes, students participate in intense training drills and team-bonding activities with the objective to become an all-rounded martial artist. Members will also be given the opportunity to use the skills gained from class in renowned Taekwondo competitions both at the local and national level. To further reinforce the idea of building a close-knit community at Vanderbilt University, the organization directs yearly community service projects that allow members to share the “art” in martial arts as well as the corresponding virtues of Taekwondo to their peers and neighboring municipalities.
summary: Vanderbilt Taekwondo is a student-led Taekwondo organization that aims to promote student well-being through the art of Taekwondo.

name: Office of Student Transitions and Community Engagement
summary: Staff within Residential Colleges works to enhance the experience of transfer students and second-year students while supporting their transitions during their time at Vanderbilt.

name: Rothschild College
description:
Ravindra Duddu, Faculty Head of Rothschild College

Ravindra Duddu (he, him, his) is an associate professor of civil and environmental engineering. He received his B. Tech. from Indian Institute of Technology Madras and Ph.D. from Northwestern University and has been a part of the Vanderbilt faculty since 2012. Professor Duddu specializes in scientific computing focusing on the applications of finite element analysis and machine learning in earth, climate, and material sciences. He is recipient of the National Science Foundation CAREER award and Fulbright Kalam-Climate Fellowship. In his leisure time,  he likes to play board games with his kids, play ping pong and volleyball, go swimming, and listen to podcasts on health, climate, and economy.

The Residential College

Rothschild College, opened in fall 2022, is home to sophomores, juniors, and seniors. The facility features a dining hall, great room, study lounges and a reservable Black Box Theater. The faculty head of college also resides in the college alongside his family.
summary: Rothschild College

name: Lights, Camera, Objection (formerly Film Club at Vanderbilt Law School)
description:
LCO is an opportunity for students with a common interest in film to come together to watch films and discuss how the medium interacts with important issues and understandings of law.

LCO’s mission is to hold screenings of films that are related to important topics in the legal industry and current events so that members can engage with them in an entertaining and meaningful way. LCO seeks to invite Vanderbilt faculty withexpertise on the subject at issue to speak on these topics, so that students may have the opportunity to think critically about the films screened and have takeaways for their profession and interest in movies generally.
summary: Lights, Camera, Objection (LCO) is an opportunity for students with a common interest in film to come together to watch films and discuss how the medium interacts with important issues and understandings of law.

name: Sophomore Year Experience
description:
The Sophomore Year Experience (SYE) invites all undergraduates in their second year to explore campus resources and the greater Nashville community. SYE includes the CHARGED UP series that invites second-year students to engage with various offices and attend fun events for prizes! 

SYE is coordinated by Residential Colleges.
summary: The Sophomore Year Experience (SYE) invites all undergraduates in their second year to explore campus resources and the greater Nashville community.

name: Vanderbilt Title IX Office
description:
Vanderbilt University is committed to equal opportunity and to maintaining a safe and healthy environment for all members of the University community (including students, faculty, staff, postdocs, and trainees), guests, and visitors. The University’s policies, programs, and activities are designed to foster courtesy and respect. The University prohibits and seeks to eliminate all forms of sexual misconduct, including sexual harassment and sexual assault. Under federal law, Vanderbilt has a duty to take steps to prevent and redress sexual misconduct. Such conduct is contrary to Vanderbilt’s values and is not tolerated.

This Sexual Misconduct Policy (Policy) outlines the procedures that apply to allegations of sexual misconduct, including sexual harassment and sexual assault, involving members of the Vanderbilt community. It is applicable to all members of the Vanderbilt community, with respect to conduct that occurs on campus and conduct that occurs off-campus that has an on-campus effect, including conduct in connection with University programs or activities or that otherwise interferes with or limits the ability of a member of the community to participate in or to receive benefits, services, or opportunities from the University’s programs or activities.
summary: Homepage for Title IX training and events

name: Vanderbilt Law and Economics
summary: The digital hub for the Ph.D. Program in Law and Economics

name: IgniteVU
summary: IgniteVU is the comprehensive diversity, equity, and inclusion curriculum for all fraternity and sorority members. This program is a partnership between the Student Center for Social Justice and Identity and the Office of Greek Life.

name: Vanderbilt Center for Languages
description: Welcome to the Vanderbilt Center for Languages. We are located at the crossroads of language and culture, a dynamic point of departure to explore communities on campus and around the globe through language study, research, and cross-cultural exchange.
summary: Welcome to the Vanderbilt Center for Languages. We are located at the crossroads of language and culture, a dynamic point of departure to explore communities on campus and around the globe through language study, research, and cross-cultural exchange.

name: 180 Degrees Consulting at Vanderbilt
description:
Who are we?

180 Degrees Consulting (180DC) is the world’s largest university-based consultancy for non-profits and socially-conscious organizations. We operate in over 35 countries, currently have over 180 active branches, successfully created over 7,800 projects, and trained over 60,000 social impact leaders to date. We've been around for over a decade and we help socially-focused organizations and early-stage startups scale their impact, providing high-quality and extremely affordable consulting services. We do so while we build the next generation of leaders committed to making a difference.

 

What do we do?

180DC creates a ripple effect of positive change by leveraging the skills of passionate young professionals to help non-profits and social enterprises increase their impact. By offering affordable high-quality consulting services, we are democratizing access to expertise and helping organizations achieve greater sustainability and growth. Through this process, 180DC is cultivating a new generation of leaders who are dedicated to creating positive social and environmental change around the world.
summary: 180 Degrees Consulting strives to ensure that non-profits and social enterprises committed to education, health, and poverty alleviation can reach their full potential by developing innovative solutions using the expertise of its student members.

name: Ballet Folklórico Mexicano de Vanderbilt
description: We are a Mexican-inspired dance group whose mission is to promote and embrace the beauty and diversity of Mexican culture through the art of Ballet Folklorico dance with traditional folklore attire. Our dance team will perform dances from various regions of Mexico to celebrate the rich history and heritage of the country.
summary: Mexican-inspired dance group that embraces and showcases Mexican culture through various styles of Folklore dance performances.

name: Brain Exercise Initiative
summary: We use simple math, writing and reading aloud exercises as an intervention to improve cognitive function and overall wellbeing in those with Alzheimer's Disease, by partnering with local retirement homes in the metro Nashville Area.

name: Financial Modeling Club
description: The Financial Modeling Club (FMC) is a club whose primary activity is an 8-week course that teaches Excel skills to Vanderbilt students regardless of prior experience. The class will start with an excel crash course and then continue to cover models such as a three-statement model, DCF, and public comps model, among others. The club has weekly hour-long meetings. The goal of the organization is to help students understand how Microsoft Excel is used in the business world and prepare students with the Excel skills necessary to be successful in a diverse set of careers.
summary: The Financial Modeling Club (formerly known as Excel Modeling Club) is a club whose primary activity is an 8-week course that teaches Microsoft Excel skills to Vanderbilt students regardless of prior experience. The club has weekly hour-long meetings.

name: Future Educators of Color
description: Future Educators of Color seeks to promote diversity in the American education system by providing support for people of color who dream of participating in educating the youth of our nation. Currently, elementary and secondary public school teachers in the U.S. are remarkably less diverse in race and ethnicity in comparison to their students. Though racial and ethnic diversity of the students are rapidly growing, that of the teachers is not kept in pace. In addition, teachers of color tend to work in different school environments than their White counterparts, meaning that they are more likely to be found to be teaching in districts of lower income and less academic achievement. This is an issue that needs to be addressed not only in the current public school system, but also in educational institutions that raises future educators. Especially at a university that boasts the best education college (Peabody) in the nation, this is an important matter to be discussed. For that reason, Future Educators of Color aims to start this change at a college level for students of color who wish to partake in education. It will raise awareness of the contemporary diversity issues in school systems, search for possible solutions that address these concerns, and take action to make a meaningful change for the future.
summary: FEC promotes diversity in the American education system by supporting people of color who dream of being future educators. It raises awareness of the current diversity issues in school and creates solutions that will make a meaningful change.

name: Heart Strings
description: Heart Strings is dedicated to joining service and creativity through various forms of fiber arts (crochet, knitting, embroidery, sewing, Rainbow Loom, etc.). We aim to both allow Vanderbilt students to explore new avenues of creativity and de-stress from academic responsibilities, while also donating these items to local children’s hospitals and hospice care services. Overall, we strive to foster creativity by providing both the Vanderbilt community and children at local hospitals with an activity that they can pursue long-term.
summary: Heart Strings strives to help children navigate the stresses of hospitalization by donating fiber arts created by Vanderbilt students and facilitating workshops for hospitalized children. We seek to promote creativity in fiber arts in our community.

name: Hip Hop Public Health
description:
At Hip Hop Public Health, we harness the transformative power of music, art, and culture to inspire positive health choices for young people and their families. We partner with legendary hip hop artists to create culturally-relevant, science-based, multimedia educational resources that get students excited to learn about healthy literacy knowledge and skills. The organization's Volunteer Program connects college students with elementary-aged students in their local communities. Students volunteer in local schools to provide interactive health education lessons using Hip Hop Public Health materials that inspire positive health choices among young people.

 

Hip Hop Public Health volunteers will be partnered with a Nashville public elementary school and visit throughout the school year to facilitate fun, hip-hop-based lessons about important health topics for students in grades 3-5. 

 

Volunteers will be part of a dynamic community dedicated to advancing health equity, with opportunities for learning first-hand about health education, social determinants of health, and approaches for mitigating health disparities.

https://linktr.ee/hhphvandy
summary: Hip Hop Public Health harnesses the transformative power of music and art to inspire positive health choices for children and their families. Join to be part of a dynamic community that advances health equity and mitigates health disparities in Nashville

name: Kern National Network - Vanderbilt Chapter
description:
Our organization promotes the values and mission of the Kern National Network through involvement and activities at Vanderbilt. The KNN statement of purpose is below:

 

"The Kern National Network for Caring and Character in Medicine (KNN) is a national movement whose work is focused on the integration of four foundational elements within the profession of medicine: caring, character, practical wisdom and human flourishing. We advance this work in the following ways: connecting and convening stakeholders across the healthcare ecosystem, catalyzing transformative initiatives, and influencing policy and systems change."
summary: Vanderbilt is one of the seven founding medical schools of the Kern National Network, a collaboration of medical schools working to promote caring, character, practical wisdom, and human flourishing in the clinical environment.

name: Nurses for Sexual and Reproductive Health
summary: Helping nursing students at Vanderbilt to become skilled care providers and social change agents in sexual and reproductive health and justice.

name: Organ Donation Awareness Club
description:
Organ Donation Awareness Club is a place for all students to engage with service, advocacy, and outreach regarding the apolitical topic of organ donation. The organization will have three major divisions: 
  1. Community Advocacy: consisting of youth advocacy targeting Vanderbilt undergraduate students and others of a similar age group and general advocacy targeting the overall Nashville community. The overall goal is to directly increase organ donor registration by going to the people and providing education. Members of the club will organize events such as tabling, service, and outreach events that will be open to all VUSM students to engage with to be a part of organ donation advocacy.
  2. Transplant Relations: working with the larger transplant community, including other transplant organizations, OPOs, VUMC transplant staff, organ donor families, and of course, transplant patients. This division will highlight the real stories of those impacted by organ donation and invite speakers to educate VUSM about the ethical and social components of the transplant process.
  3. Social Awareness: Use social media as a tool to collaborate with other advocacy groups to highlight the intertwined nature of organ donation with numerous other social causes and to increase awareness of the need for more organ donors in the US. This will help engage with the broader community beyond our geographic limitations. Additionally, there will be a historian role to document, record, and photograph events and use social media posts to highlight the great work being done by the organization.

This organization is only open to current VUSM MD Students.
summary: A VUSM club that educates about and advocates for organ donation, emphasizing untangling the complicated history and social elements of the transplant process.

name: oSTEM at Vanderbilt
description: oSTEM is a global organization that has the general mission, "oSTEM empowers LGBTQ+ students in STEM to succeed personally, academically, and professionally by cultivating environments and communities that nurture innovation, leadership, and advocacy." At Vanderbilt's chapter, this mission is furthered on campus with students' involvement to create a more comfortable space for their identities and how they intersect with their future career goals through networking, career fairs, research events, and outings.
summary: The oSTEM at Vanderbilt chapter empowers LGBTQ+ undergraduate students in STEM to succeed personally, academically, and professionally by cultivating environments and communities that nurture innovation, leadership, and advocacy.

name: Taiwanese American Student Association
description: The Vanderbilt University Taiwanese American Student Association is organized for the purpose of promoting and providing awareness of Taiwanese and Taiwanese American issues and cultures within the University, the local community, and beyond. This Association also acts as a support group for those of Taiwanese descent as well as those interested in participating in this Association’s activities, helping to achieve its principles and objectives. These objectives include encouraging public awareness and fostering conversation around Taiwanese and Taiwanese American culture and identity, providing a space for Taiwanese and Taiwanese American students to connect on Vanderbilt campus, and serving as a platform to connect with the intercollegiate and Nashville's Taiwanese American and Taiwanese organizations.
summary: TASA aims to provide a space for Taiwanese and Taiwanese American students on Vanderbilt's campus and raise awareness of our culture within our community.

name: Clinical Problem Solvers
description: The Vanderbilt Clinical Problem Solvers is a group dedicated to building a community of medical students, house staff, and faculty that are passionate about case-based discussions and the practice of clinical reasoning.
summary: The Vanderbilt Clinical Problem Solvers is a group dedicated to building a community of medical students, house staff, and faculty that are passionate about case-based discussions and the practice of clinical reasoning.

name: Vanderbilt Athlete Ally
description: VAA's mission is to end homophobia and transphobia in sports and to uplift LGBTQIA+ athletes.
summary: VAA's mission is to end homophobia and transphobia in sports and to uplift LGBTQIA+ athletes.

name: Vanderbilt Bird Club
description: The Vanderbilt Bird Club is for everyone who likes birds! The club aims to provide a social space for students to learn about and appreciate Nashville's local birds. Activities include birdwatching hikes and more.
summary: The Vanderbilt Bird Club provides a community for students to learn about and appreciate Nashville's local birds.

name: Vanderbilt CTF Club
description: Vanderbilt CTF Club is dedicated to fostering a competitive team and learning community to participate in Capture the Flag (CTF) competitions and hosting workshops and other events about cybersecurity and all computer science topics.
summary: Vanderbilt CTF Club is dedicated to fostering a competitive team to participate in Capture the Flag (CTF) competitions and hosting workshops and other events about cybersecurity and computer science topics.

name: Vanderbilt Data Science
description: VDS is a project-based organization that encourages students to explore data science, artificial intelligence (AI), and machine learning (ML) through hands-on experience with real-world data in local community partnerships.
summary: VDS is a project-based organization that encourages students to explore data science, artificial intelligence (AI), and machine learning (ML) through hands-on experience with real-world data in local community partnerships.

name: Vanderbilt Fly Fishing Club
description: The purpose of the Vanderbilt Fly Fishing Club is to promote fly fishing as a form of outdoor recreation by bringing together students and faculty, and providing an environment conducive to learning about the art of fly fishing and develop a conservation ethic. The club is part of Trout Unlimited’s 5 Rivers College outreach program, and the club will be affiliated with the Music City Chapter of Trout Unlimited so that the two organizations may jointly participate in conservation projects and fishing excursions. Trout Unlimited’s mission is to protect, reconnect, and restore North America’s coldwater fisheries and their watersheds. As a member of the club, you have the opportunity to participate in events such as fly fishing trips, fly tying classes, social and educational events, and conservation excursions.
summary: Vanderbilt Fly Fishing Club promotes fly fishing as a form of outdoor recreation by bringing together students, while providing an environment conducive to learning about the arts of fly fishing to developing a conservation ethic.

name: Vanderbilt Letter Project
description: Vanderbilt Letter Project is an organization with a goal for teaching medical students to learn how to have conversations around advance care planning. We use a user-friendly letter that is based on the Stanford Letter Project to have patients explore what is most important – their personal values, future milestones, and what type of medical care they do and do not want to receive at the end of their lives. Faculty from the section of Palliative Care and School of Nursing train and supervise students along with trained student leaders at weekly Thursday afternoon sessions. This organization is only open to current VUSM MD Students.
summary: The goal of the project is to train students to help patients plan for their care in advance so that their wishes for medical care can be followed even if they are unable to make decisions for themselves.

name: Vanderbilt University Thomistic Institute
summary: The Vanderbilt University Thomistic Institute (VUTI) is a philosophy club for the entire campus. The VUTI hosts lectures, seminars, and reading groups to explore contemporary issues through the lens of the rich Thomistic intellectual tradition

name: Wond'ry Innovation Consulting Program
description:
The Wond’ry Innovation Consulting Program (WICP) aims to introduce the
graudate student body to the field of consulting through multidisciplinary cross-program
collaboration on voluntary projects across a wide array of specialties and
institutions. Projects shall grow students’ abilities to define problems and create
evidence-based solutions for clients ranging from business startups to charitable
organizations.
summary: A nonprofit, volunteer, multidisciplinary graduate student consulting organization working with nonprofit, startup, and industry clients to solve significant problems in healthcare outcomes, management, operations, business strategy, and much more.

name: ECE Student Association
description:
The Electrical and Computer Engineering Student Association (ECESA) is a vibrant and dynamic organization dedicated to serving the academic, professional, and social needs of students within the Electrical and Computer Engineering department. Our mission is to enhance the educational experience of ECE students by fostering a supportive community, promoting professional development, and facilitating engagement with industry and academia.

Mission and Vision: Our mission is to empower ECE students by providing resources, opportunities, and a supportive network that promotes academic excellence, professional growth, and personal development. We envision a cohesive community where students are well-prepared to excel in their careers and make meaningful contributions to the field of electrical and computer engineering.

Core Values:

  1. Community: We strive to build a strong sense of community among ECE students, faculty, and alumni through various events, activities, and networking opportunities.
  2. Excellence: We are committed to promoting academic excellence and supporting students in their pursuit of knowledge and innovation.
  3. Professionalism: We encourage professional development by offering seminars, and mentoring programs that prepare students for successful careers.
  4. Innovation: We foster a culture of innovation and creativity, encouraging students to explore new ideas and technologies.
  5. Inclusivity: We are dedicated to creating an inclusive environment where all students feel valued and supported.

Activities and Programs:

  • Seminars: We organize seminars on the latest trends and technologies in the field.
  • Academic Support: We provide study groups, and resources to help students succeed academically.
  • Competitions and Projects: We encourage participation in competitions and collaborative projects that challenge students to apply their skills in real-world scenarios.
  • Social Events: From welcome parties to farewell events, we host a variety of social activities to strengthen bonds among students and create lasting memories.

Membership: Membership is open to all students within the Electrical and Computer Engineering department. We also welcome collaboration with students from other disciplines who share an interest in ECE. Membership provides access to exclusive events, resources, and a supportive network of peers and professionals.

Leadership: The ECESA is led by a dedicated team of student volunteers who are passionate about making a positive impact on the ECE community. Our leadership team works collaboratively to plan and execute events, manage resources, and ensure the smooth functioning of the association.

Contact Us: For more information about the ECE Student Association, upcoming events, or how to get involved, please contact us at sajal.islam@vanderbilt.edu

Join us at the ECE Student Association and be a part of a community that is committed to your success!
summary: ECE Student Association (ECESA) intends of fostering scientific interactions and strengthen the career development of ECE students. It serves as a network for undergrad, grad students and post-docs within the ECE Department.

We serve as a forum for the discussion of matters pertaining to the well-being of graduate students, provide leadership opportunities for students, represent and advocate for student opinions and concerns, foster unity and belonging through meetings and events (for CDA students as well as other graduate students, independently or through co-sponsorship), and aid in the connection of incoming, current, and former students.
summary: The Community Development and Action Student Association’s mission is to serve the needs of CDA graduate students and facilitate connection between CDA students, faculty, and the wider university community and promote the mission of the CDA Program.

The Vanderbilt Gymnastics Club encourages students to learn and participate in the art and sport of gymnastics. description: As Vanderbilt's premier South Asian classical dance collective, Agni aims to promote cultural diversity through the performance, education, and appreciation of South Asian classical dance forms including, but not limited to, Bharata Natyam, Kathak, Kuchipudi, and Odissi. Agni allows students at Vanderbilt to engage, learn, and appreciate these multifaceted art forms, truly embracing the various backgrounds of students at the university. Agni hosts informal workshops for each dance form, along with annual showcases highlighting these classical dance forms. Agni also partakes in various charity and cultural showcases to garner enthusiasm for the classical South Asian arts on campus.

name: iCare Health Guide
description:
iCare aims to improve medical literacy and ameliorate health disparities for international students, immigrants, and refugees in the local Nashville community through various programs both on-campus and outside of campus.

Our current initiatives include,

Author and post weekly articles on the official Wechat subscription account with more than 400 subscribers to provide detailed explanations of the student medical insurance plan for international students at Vanderbilt University in addition to offering a step-by-step guide regarding how to choose the optimal insurance plan and access healthcare services for Chinese immigrants;

Partner with Tennessee Justice for Our Neighbors to translate medical insurance documents and design video tutorials for refugees to access healthcare services in Nashville;

Partner with the Greater Nashville Chinese Association to organize in-person forums that raise awareness of healthy life practices and further explain different medical insurance policies for Chinese immigrants;

Design and update the multilingual website repository for other immigrants to facilitate their understanding of health insurance and medical terminology.
summary: iCare aims to improve medical literacy and ameliorate health disparities for international students, immigrants, and refugees in the local Nashville community through various programs both on-campus and outside of campus.

name: National Patient Advocate Foundation @ VU
description:
NPAF

📢 Join the Nashville Chapter of the National Patient Advocate Foundation (NPAF)! 

We are a student-led chapter of the National Patient Advocate Foundation, dedicated to connecting underserved Nashville patients with free, patient-centered healthcare resources. Our mission is to meet people where they are in community centers, food pantries, and classrooms and ensure everyone has access to health education and support. 

✨ What We Do: 

  • Partner with local organizations serving minorities, refugees, immigrants, and underserved populations 
  • Lead health education workshops on topics like diabetes, cancer, and dementia 
  • Provide hands-on support in community spaces (from food distribution to ESL classes) 
  • Advocate for health equity across Nashville 

🤝 Current Community Partnerships: 

  • NICE (Nashville International Center for Empowerment) 
    • Serving 40,000+ refugees & immigrants 
    • We lead weekly health lectures + work with ESL students after class 
  • The Store (Choice-Based Food Pantry) 
    • Preserves dignity through food choice & education 
    • We table, share healthcare resources, and volunteer with food distribution 
  • Park Center 
    • 40+ years supporting mental health, substance use recovery, and homelessness 
    • We host monthly health talks and distribute hundreds of first-aid kits  

💡 Why Join? 
Gain real-world exposure, learn about public health advocacy, and make a tangible impact on patient lives all while connecting with a national healthcare network. 

👉 Interested in volunteering, leading health talks, or expanding Nashville’s access to care? Feel free to contact us or join us at our next GBM. We’d love to have you!
summary: National Patient Advocate Foundation at Vanderbilt (NPAF@VU)'s mission is to connect Nashville’s underserved communities with accessible resources made by the National Patient Advocate Foundation to help them navigate their healthcare needs.

name: Older Wiser Law Students of Vanderbilt Law School
description: Older Wiser Law Students (OWLS) hopes to create a community for law students that have not come directly to law school after obtaining their undergraduate degree. Older law students often have families, careers, and advanced degrees, which gives them a different perspective and set of priorities while attending law school and when entering the legal job market. OWLS intends to serve as a resource for these students as they navigate their academic careers and their transition into the legal profession. It welcomes membership from any students who feel they may benefit from joining a community of older law students.
summary: We are a community for older law students, particularly those that had a career after obtaining their undergraduate degree and before entering law school. We hope to provide a community and guidance that respects our unique priorities as older students.

name: Public Health Student Association
description: The Public Health Student Association (PHSA) is a student-led organization for all students with a passion for public health and service. PHSA welcomes students (undergraduate, graduate, and professional from any major or field of study) from all backgrounds who are interested in becoming more involved in public health and the health field!
summary: PHSA is a student-led organization for all students with a passion for public health and service.

name: Tutor Nashville
description:
Tutor Nashville partners with the Metro Nashville Public Schools Accelerating Scholars program to provide math and literacy tutoring to elementary, middle, and high school students across Nashville. Tutor Nashville recruits volunteer tutors from Vanderbilt to be matched with individual tutees for the semester. Once tutors and tutees are matched, tutors and tutees work together for 90 minutes per week -- either three times a week for 30 minutes per session, or two times a week for 45 minutes per session. 

The Accelerating Scholars Program provides tutors with initial training and lesson plans. Tutor Nashville offers volunteer tutors additional support and a sense of community -- seeking to inspire active learning as well as student connection and engagement.
summary: Tutor Nashville partners with the Accelerated Scholars program to deliver math and literacy tutoring services to elementary, middle, and high school students across Metro Nashville Public Schools.

name: Vanderbilt Advancement of Blood Clotting Disorder Education
description: Every year, tens of thousands of Americans are born with a blood clotting disorder. Hemophilia is one of the more well-known of these disorders, with 1 in 5000 males being affected this condition. It is a chronic condition that currently has no cure. In addition, it is an invisible condition, not made apparent by simple observation. Most unfortunately, it is a classified as a rare condition, providing little incentive for pharmaceutical companies to direct research towards it. People living with this condition require access to special medical attention, specific and expensive medication, routine checkups, and depending on the severity of the condition, around the clock supervision. Because of all of these factors, hemophiliac individuals and their families face physical, emotional, and financial obstacles that are unrecognized by the larger society. There is a need for an advancement in our society's approach and understanding of blood clotting disorders through cultural understanding, programmed education, and funding to help families and for research related to blood clotting disorders. This organization, started by the 7 roommates of an individual with hemophilia, aims to bridging the gap between what is unknown into what is uncovered.
summary: Vanderbilt ABCDE aims to improve the visibility and awareness of hemophilia and other blood clotting disorders. We will host general fundraisers and public service actions. Our culminating project will be a public walk on April 17th, World Hemophilia Day

The goal of this organization is to streamline opportunities to students who are passionate about improving the lives of refugee and immigrant populations in Nashville. Volunteer opportunities include teaching English second language courses, running after school programs, daycare opportunities, youth summer camps, digital literacy tutoring, art therapy, and much more. Join us in helping integrate these people into the broader Nashville community. 

Get started with your volunteer journey here

name: Vanderbilt Association for South Asian Cuisine
description: Our mission is to celebrate cuisine from all regions of South Asia. South Asian cuisine is very diverse with each region hosting its own unique dishes. We hope to bring this diversity to Vanderbilt students by hosting dinners serving cuisine from different regions every month. We plan to host a dinner event every month. In these events, we will host a short presentation led by the board to give background about the region of South Asia whose food we are celebrating. Following this presentation, we will serve our members dinner. Dinner will consist of 5-6 dishes from the region of South Asia we are celebrating at the event. During dinner, we will play a short kahoot with questions regarding the region and its cuisine and socialize. Our organization will be open to all Vanderbilt students, including South Asian students and other students who are interested in celebrating South Asian cuisine.
summary: Our mission is to celebrate cuisine from all regions of South Asia. We hope to bring this diversity to Vanderbilt students by hosting dinners serving cuisine from different regions every month.

name: Law Students for Life at Vanderbilt
description:
Law Students for Life at Vanderbilt Law School (LSL) is a non-partisan, non-religious student organization that seeks to foster open and informed discussion regarding legal, scientific, and philosophical approaches to the critical issue of when life and personhood begin.

            

Treating all individuals with equal dignity and inherent value regardless of their stage in life, LSL shall promote a culture that respects the life of each member of the human race, born and unborn.

 

Asserting that an essential end of government is the protection of human life, LSL shall advocate for policies that uphold, affirm, and safeguard this fundamental freedom. 

 

Acknowledging existing needs in our communities for education and resources to help parents and children thrive, both before and after birth, LSL shall also organize volunteer opportunities throughout the greater Nashville area and shall encourage law students to donate time and effort to serve our neighbors. 

 

Recognizing that such issues can evoke significant emotion, sometimes arising out of trauma, LSL expects each of its members to approach these conversations with compassion and civility.
summary: Law Students for Life at Vanderbilt Law School (LSL) is a non-partisan, non-religious student organization that seeks to foster open and informed discussion regarding legal, scientific, and philosophical approaches to the critical issue of when life...

We welcome students from all backgrounds and experiences to join us in our mission to support the homeless community in Nashville.

name: People’s Parity Project
description: The People’s Parity Project is an organization dedicated to organizing law students and attorneys to end how the legal profession-and the law itself-enables injustice. We are committed to demystifying and dismantling the coercive legal tools that allow the powerful to get away with violating workers’ rights, ripping off consumers, and shielding corporations and the courts from accountability. The purposes and objectives of this organization shall be: 1) to increase, especially among VULS students, participation in, interest in, and understanding of the purposes, scope, operation and other aspects of community organizing and direct action; 2) to provide those students interested in pursuing a career promoting social justice with opportunities to build skills and create change during law school; 3) to establish, sponsor and administer programs that afford VULS students opportunities to use their legal knowledge to serve the law school and Nashville community; and 4) to create a community forum that will provide practical information concerning the exploitive nature of the legal field and opportunities to disrupt that exploitation.
summary: The People’s Parity Project is an organization dedicated to organizing law students and attorneys to end how the legal profession-and the law itself-enables injustice. We are committed to demystifying and dismantling the coercive legal tools that allow..

name: Vanderbilt Social Justice Reporter
description:
The Vanderbilt Social Justice Reporter takes its name from the storied Race Relations Law Reporter (RRLR), a pioneering social justice journal launched at Vanderbilt in 1956 and funded by a grant from the Ford Foundation. The RRLR was published until 1972 to wide readership. During the 2022-2023 academic year, five Vanderbilt Law students—four in the Class of 2022 and one in the Class of 2023—worked to lay the groundwork for the journal as members of the New Journal Student Committee. This academic year, thirteen students—four in the Class of 2023 and nine in the Class of 2024—serve on the Social Justice Reporter Working Group.

 

The creation of the Social Justice Reporter (SJR) is an appropriate and timely response to the current historical moment and the need for fresh and responsive perspectives to inform current debates. It is also a vital component of Vanderbilt Law School’s commitment to social justice, diversity and inclusivity. Building on the legacy of the Race Relations Law Reporter, SJR provides a dedicated forum for social justice, civil rights and public interest scholarship both within Vanderbilt Law School and externally.

SJR is the only Vanderbilt Law journal with an explicit social justice mission. This publication is envisioned as a national go-to resource for social justice-oriented scholars, practitioners, students and community members, with content spanning intersectional issues of law and social equity. SJR will offer a unique mix of high-quality scholarship presented through long-form articles, short essays and opinion pieces, as well as contributions on current events and legal debates with direct and immediate policy relevance. The first round of publications are expected during the 2023-2024 academic year.

 

As a digital-only publication, one of SJR’s unique aims is to publish solutions, best practices and think pieces in a timelier manner than permitted by the traditional legal scholarship publication process. Topics related to social justice are varied, multi-faceted and constantly evolving as national attitudes about racial justice, voting rights, reproductive rights, LGBTQIA+ inclusivity, gender justice, economic and environmental inequality and other issues shift over time. As such, social justice discussion, in particular, requires timely and consistent engagement. SJR is committed to providing a forum for these discussions under the expert guidance of a Vanderbilt Law faculty panel.
summary: SJR is the only Vanderbilt Law journal with an explicit social justice mission. SJR will offer a unique mix of high-quality scholarship presented through long-form articles, short essays and opinion pieces, as well as contributions on current events.

name: Plaintiffs’ Advocates & Litigation Society
description:
The Plaintiffs’ Advocates & Litigation Society recognizes that a combination of strong institutional norms and the powerful interests of corporate donorship means law students are often exposed to only two career options: join a corporate defense firm or work for the government or a nonprofit.

The Plaintiffs’ Advocates and Litigation Society seeks to expose Vanderbilt students to a third option: become a plaintiff’s attorney and use our world-class legal education to advocate for the interests of real people against the most powerful corporate interests.

In doing so, the Plaintiffs’ Advocates & Litigation Society intends to host events focused on exposing current students to a different legal perspective, coordinate with local and national firms who share similar values, gather and provide information and resources that career services may not have on hand, and connect Vanderbilt Law students with career opportunities in an industry that is often difficult to crack.
summary: The Plaintiffs’ Advocates & Litigation Society recognizes that a combination of strong institutional norms and the powerful interests of corporate donorship means law students are often exposed to only two career options: join a corporate defense firm or

name: Vanderbilt University Museum of Art
description:
Welcome to the Vanderbilt University Museum of Art!

Scent: Connection, Confrontation, Memory

Scent is everywhere; almost everyone can smell. But how cultures and individuals use and depict scent is a testament to shared experience. In spring 2026, the Vanderbilt University Museum of Art explores art that captures the olfactory in Scent: Connection, Confrontation, Memory, an exhibition of the ways smell becomes seen and supported, and how artists from a wide range of cultures have represented its ephemeral but ever-present quality. Bringing together interactive installation, sculpture, painting, photography, and a new performance commission from Ainu artist Kanako Uzawa, Scent revels in the meanings and infinite possibilities of a very human sense, physically contained and artistically represented.

Follow us on Instagram @vanderbiltartmuseum to see all our work, exhibitions and programs. 

To visit our object study classroom or book classes, email education@vanderbilt.edu. 

Open February 14 - May 10, 2026

Tuesday to Friday, 11 a.m. to 4 p.m.
Saturday and Sunday, noon to 5 p.m.

VUMA is closed on Mondays and during Spring break.
summary: The Vanderbilt University Museum of Art

name: Graduate Leadership Institute (GLI)
description: Leadership development, though useful in the long-term, may seem like just another item to add to your already burgeoning to-do list with the many and varied demands of graduate school. Instead, we urge you to think of the Graduate Leadership Institute’s (GLI) programming as an opportunity to take a step back and be intentional in your training here at Vanderbilt. What do you need to succeed as a leader in your lab, student organization, community, internship, careers? GLI wants to provide students with the skills, tools, and self-awareness that will allow them to lead at all levels through programming that facilitates a greater understanding of personal leadership style and interactions with others, in addition to practical technical skills and business acumen that can be overlooked when strictly focusing on research skills. We are committed to training well-rounded scholars with an enhance capacity to lead.
summary: The mission of the Graduate Leadership Institute at Vanderbilt University is to provide graduate students with the tools to thrive in leadership roles at all levels and the ability to recognize and develop leadership skills in themselves and others.

name: Kappa Theta Pi
description: Kappa Theta Pi is the world's first co-educational professional technology fraternity. We work to build an active community of students with a shared interest in technology; sponsoring events aimed to provide intellectual, social, and professional resources to members. We seek to foster relationships within the local community, and with corporations; ultimately providing service and philanthropy to the local community.
summary: Kappa Theta Pi is the world's first co-ed professional technology fraternity. We work to build an active community of students with a shared interest in technology; sponsoring events aimed to provide intellectual, social, and professional resources.

name: IslandDores
description: IslandDores aims to be a vibrant and inclusive club founded with the mission to create a warm and supportive community at Vanderbilt University for students with Pacific Island heritage or those with an earnest interest in Pacific Island culture. Our primary objective is to bring together individuals from diverse Pacific Islands across the globe, providing a space where they can celebrate their unique cultures, traditions, and shared experiences and give those who want to learn more about it an opportunity. IslandDores serves to foster a strong sense of belonging and camaraderie among its members, offering a platform for open dialogue, cultural exchange, and mutual support. Through engaging events, educational initiatives, and social gatherings, we aspire to promote understanding and appreciation of the rich and diverse Pacific Island cultures present within our university. Our vision is to bridge gaps and build lifelong connections among Pacific Islanders, empowering them to embrace their identities while embracing the larger Vanderbilt community. By promoting unity, cultural enrichment, and collaboration, IslandDores seeks to leave a lasting positive impact on its members' collegiate journey and beyond.
summary: IslandDores is the only student organization on campus that caters to students who identify/come from the Pacific Islands. We plan to share and educate the Vanderbilt community about our culture and support those of similar backgrounds.

description:
It will be a space for students of Iranian heritage (as well as all other students) to unite in honor of Iranian tradition, culture, and customs. It will have an emphasis on creating a space for students to connect to their Iranian heritage away from home.

 

The Vanderbilt Iranian Students’ Association is committed to promoting an inclusive and vibrant community for students of Iranian descent at Vanderbilt University. VISA seeks to empower, connect, and celebrate Iranian students and their beautiful culture throughout campus while breaking stereotypes and promoting awareness. By connecting Iranian undergraduates, graduate students, faculty, and professionals linked to Vanderbilt and Nashville, we seek to empower VISA members socially and professionally.

name: Evidence-Based Medicine Interest Group
description:
To help students at Vanderbilt School of Medicine hone their craft of practicing evidence-based medicine, we will host sessions throughout the academic year that involve:

  1. Introducing students to the principles of evidence-based medicine, providing a framework for critically analyzing emerging primary literature.
  2. Inviting speakers from a variety of clinical specialties to discuss emerging literature as case studies for appraising research and its effect on their practice.
  3. Hosting office hours to enable students to bring their own clinical research ideas/questions/data to discuss study design and appropriate statistical methods to answer clinically relevant questions.

What not to expect: another lecture! Our sessions are highly interactive meetings where students practice discussing the pros and cons of research strategies and the strength of the evidence presented. One student will facilitate and everyone will participate!
summary: The mission of the EBMIG is to create a space for students to enhance their ability to critically evaluate medical literature, assess the quality of evidence, and apply evidence-based guidelines to clinical practice.

name: Students Interested in Gerontological Nursing
description: We are an organization through VUSN which focuses on educating students about the special concerns of older adult patients and supporting them throughout the Nashville area.
summary: We are an organization through VUSN which focuses on educating students about the special concerns of older adult patients and supporting them throughout the Nashville area.

name: Sigma Tau Delta
description: Sigma Tau Delta confers distinction for high achievement in the study of English literature while providing intellectual experiences beyond the classroom, fostering literacy in our community, and exhibiting exemplary character & scholarship. Sigma Tau Delta at Vanderbilt offers opportunities for networking, professional development, service, community, and personal growth to passionate English majors and minors.
summary: Sigma Tau Delta, the international English honor society, confers distinction for high achievement in the study of English literature.

name: Vanderbilt Child Life Student Association
description: To provide education, networking, and social support for students pursuing careers in the child life profession, or those who are pursuing related healthcare fields.
summary: To provide education, networking, and social support for students pursuing careers in the child life profession, or those who are pursuing related healthcare fields.

name: Department of Teaching and Learning Doctoral Student Association
summary: DTL DSA is a representative body consisting of doctoral students from the Department of Teaching and Learning at Vanderbilt University. Its purpose is to provide a safe space for collective, collegial empowerment and community building for DTL students.

name: Vanderbilt Out In Business
summary: Vanderbilt Out In Business (VOIB) is an undergraduate student organization which aims to provide resources, support, and education to Vanderbilt students who share an interest in business and an identity within the LGBTQ+ community.

name: Vanderbilt Photo Society
description:
Vanderbilt Photo Society (VPS) is dedicated to nurturing the passion for photography within the Vanderbilt community by offering opportunities for students to learn, create, and display photographic media. Activities include hosting workshops to refine visual editing skills, photo walks and gallery visits around the Nashville area, hosting workshops by local artists, photo contests, and culminating in a biannual photo exhibition. VPS emphasizes collaboration and peer support, and we invite you to build social connections with our creative community. Explore, create, and belong at Vanderbilt Photo Society.

Instagram: @vanderbiltphotosociety
summary: Vanderbilt Photo Society (VPS) is a forum to learn, create, and display photos. Activities include workshops by local artists, photo walks around Nashville, guest speakers, and a final exhibition. Explore, create, and belong at Vanderbilt Photo Society.

name: Vanderbilt Small Town And Rural Students
description: Vanderbilt Small Town and Rural Students’s goal is to create and maintain a supportive community of Vanderbilt undergraduate students from small towns and rural areas. This is done with the hope that these STARS will find other students who can relate to our unique experiences and perspectives! We also work with the Office of Undergraduate Admissions to increase outreach to high school students from small towns and rural areas!
summary: We aim to create and maintain a supportive community of Vanderbilt undergraduate students from small towns and rural areas. This is done with the hope that they will find other students who can relate to our unique experiences and perspectives!

name: American Chemical Society Graduate Chapter
description: The chapter plans social, informational, and networking events for graduate students and faculty in chemistry or related fields. To vote in chapter elections, you must be enrolled as a premium ACS member. However, there is no requirement to join the newsletter or attend events, so please join us!
summary: The chapter plans social, informational, and networking events for graduate students and faculty in chemistry or related fields.

name: The Access and Inclusion Project
summary: The Access and Inclusion Project is dedicated to providing a space for open and honest discussion of disability, and everyone is welcome at these discussions! We will have monthly dinners to accomplish this goal!

name: Special Education Graduate Student Association
summary: SPED GSA serves as the voice of graduate students in the Department of Special Education. This organization supports students by building a department-specific community and addressing department-specific issues and challenges.

name: Cell and Developmental Biology GSA
description: The Cell and Developmental Biology Graduate Student Association serves as a dynamic and collaborative hub for aspiring scientists in the field of cell and developmental biology. Our primary purpose is to foster a vibrant academic community where graduate students can engage in interdisciplinary discussions, share cutting-edge research findings, and develop essential skills for their careers. Through seminars, workshops, and networking events, we aim to facilitate intellectual growth, scientific collaboration, and mentorship within our department. Furthermore, we are committed to promoting outreach activities that communicate the excitement and relevance of cell and developmental biology to the broader public. Ultimately, our association strives to empower future leaders in this field and contribute to the advancement of scientific knowledge.
summary: Our primary purpose is to foster a vibrant academic community where graduate students can engage in interdisciplinary discussions, share cutting-edge research findings, and develop essential skills for their careers.

name: Graduate Women of Color
summary: The Graduate Women of Color group aims to provide professional development, support, and community for doctoral students.

name: Vanderbilt Pokemon League
description:
We are Vanderbilt University's Premier Pokemon League. Stop here for all things Pokemon! From card games to Pokemon Go, we have something for you! Make friends, battle others, and trade pokemon!

Join our discord!
summary: We're Vanderbilt's Official Pokemon Club! If you're interested in meeting other fans, learning how to play competitively, or finding a new hobby/game, we're the place for you!

name: Nightingales in the Wings
description: Nightingales in the Wings derives its name from two sources of inspiration: the small nightingale bird known for its beautiful song and the wings of a hospital or healthcare facility. Our partnerships within the healthcare sector allow our musicians to experience the intersection between music and medicine in a clinical setting. With the conservatory-level musical training of the Blair School and the support of healthcare professionals, our musicians are able to spread their wings and share something beautiful with the community.
summary: Nightingales in the Wings strives to combine the healing powers of medicine and music through performances at hospitals for patients, families and medical staff.

name: Kappa Delta Chi Sorority, Inc. at Vanderbilt
description: Kappa Delta Chi Sorority, Inc. is a Latina founded, 501(c)(7), national sorority who aims to achieve professional development, academic excellence, and graduation of all its members; an organization dedicated to community service to their local university communities with an emphasis on the Hispanic/Latino population.
summary: Kappa Delta Chi Sorority, Inc. is a Latina founded, 501(c)(7), national sorority who aims to achieve professional development, academic excellence, and graduation of all its members; an organization dedicated to community service to their local...

name: Facilities
summary: OUR VISION: To be the BEST facilities department in the country by PROVIDING OUTSTANDING, INNOVATIVE SERVICES to the community. Our departments include Business Operations, Campus Planning & Construction, VU Maintenance & Operations, and VU Real Estate.

name: Future Is Now Nashville
description:
The Future is NOW (Non-violent Options Win) Nashville is part of the Future Healers Network out of the University of Louisville (UofL). The Future is NOW Nashville is the first Future Healers chapter outside of Louisville, and it was inspired by their incredible foundation. In recent months, the Nashville community was shaken to the core by gun violence with a school shooting. Unfortunately, this is not a new problem for our city. Together with the support of the Vanderbilt Division of Acute Care Surgery; the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives (ATF) – Nashville Field Division; Nashville Metro Police; and Stronger than My Father, we strive to inspire at risk youth in Nashville to choose a violence free future.

Through compassion and mentorship, this program aims to empower young individuals to aspire for better futures for themselves and for their communities. Through longitudinal relationships and education, we hope to facilitate healing, build resilience and empowerment.

 

One Saturday per month, we will mentor children from ages 4-13 on various health care topics and wellness practices. The volunteers will act as small group activity leaders, mentoring the same set of students throughout the year. This will serve as an opportunity to give back to the Nashville community, while being an excellent opportunity for leadership and curriculum development.
summary: Through compassion and mentorship, Future is Now aims to empower youth at risk for gun violence to aspire for better futures for themselves and for their communities through longitudinal relationships and education.

name: Medical Students for a Sustainable Future at Vanderbilt University School of Medicine
description: Medical Students for a Sustainable Future (MS4SF) is dedicated to improving our planet’s health and provide the tools to effect change in the Vanderbilt medical community as well as the Nashville community through advocacy, curriculum reform, and climate change informed health care. We are a part of the national MS4SF coalition: https://ms4sf.org/
summary: We unite medical students in improving our planet’s health and provide the tools to effect change in the Vanderbilt medical community as well as the Nashville community through advocacy, curriculum reform, and climate change informed health care.

name: VUSM Street Medicine Initiative
description:
The VUSM Street Medicine Initiative strives to provide education on the intersection between homelessness and health, the role of street medicine in supporting the health of unhoused patients, and to connect students with related volunteer opportunities.

This organization is only open to current VUSM MD Students.
summary: VUSM Street Medicine Initiative strives to provide education on the intersection between homelessness and health, the role of street medicine in supporting the health of unhoused patients, and to connect students with volunteer opportunities.

name: Vanderbilt Veritas Forum
description: Intellectual and social organization bringing together students who ask big questions--and seek compelling answers!
summary: We are an open community of Vanderbilt Students who see it as our mission to ask big questions, and search for their answers. We do so through planning speaking events with recognized experts and discussions open to all students. Help us plan a Forum!

name: Vanderbilt Policy Accelerator for Political Economy and Regulation
description:
The Vanderbilt Policy Accelerator focuses on a small number of cutting-edge topics in political economy and regulation. We bring research, education, and policy proposals in these areas from infancy to maturity and periphery to centrality.

A Policy Accelerator: We cultivate, grow, and spread research, education, and policy ideas-and at a pace that aligns with the urgency of today's challenges.

Political Economy and Regulation: We are currently in the midst of a political economy revolution. From antitrust to trade, swift changes are taking place that are reshaping how policymakers and the general public think about economic policy. These changes create an opportunity to advance new paradigms, research, and policy ideas.

Projects: We are action focused. Our projects are just that--enterprises designed to achieve goals. Each project we undertake seeks to accelerate change in education, research, policy, and public debate.
