Projects support new research and ideas, build communities around project topics, and connect research to policy and public debate.
summary: The Vanderbilt Policy Accelerator focuses on a small number of cutting-edge topics in political economy and regulation. We bring research, education, and policy proposals in these areas from infancy to maturity and periphery to centrality.

name: Women and Working Moms- VU Employee Affinity Group
description: • The Women and Working Moms EAG was established in October 2022 with a mission to build a supportive and encouraging community where members can share resources and ideas, make connections across campus, and develop personally and professionally. The group seeks to serve the needs of parents and non-parents alike through social events, programs, and community outreach. For more information or to join please complete the survey here or contact Lana Hefner, Mia Clermont, or Penny Baga.
summary: We are an official VU employee affinity group. Affinity groups are newly housed in Human Resources under the direction of Joana Echols.

name: Philosophy Student Government Association
summary: This organization exists to support the teaching, research, and well-being of students pursuing graduate degrees in philosophy.

name: Vandy Quants
description: Vandy Quants helps develop interest in quantitative finance. We aim to educate the student body about what it means to be a “quant” and teach them the skills necessary to prepare them for an internship in this field. Weekly educational sessions throughout the first 8 weeks of the semester give new members the knowledge necessary to pursue more advanced projects in the field. We also host professional quant guest speakers so that members can learn firsthand from them and be able to ask them questions. Finally, we host social events in an aim to make this club feel like a tight-knit community.
summary: Vandy Quants helps develop interest in quantitative finance. We aim to educate the student body about what it means to be a “quant” and teach them the skills necessary to prepare them for an internship in this field.

name: Vanderbilt University Science Olympiad
description: Vanderbilt Undergraduate Science Olympiad (VUSO) is a dynamic and inclusive organization dedicated to fostering passion and excellence in STEM disciplines. Rooted in the principles of collaborative learning, competition, and community engagement, VUSO provides a platform for students to delve deep into science, technology, engineering, and mathematics. By hosting a series of lab-based, field, engineering, and theory events, alongside interactive workshops and expert-led sessions, VUSO bridges the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical application. Furthermore, by collaborating with high school teams and integrating their participation, VUSO ensures a sustainable model that nurtures budding scientific minds and shapes the next generation of STEM leaders. Primarly, VUSO embodies Vanderbilt's commitment to academic excellence, innovation, and community-building in the realm of science education.
summary: Vanderbilt Undergraduate Science Olympiad (VUSO) champions STEM through collaborative events, blending theory with practice. Engaging students and high schools, VUSO highlights Vanderbilt's dedication to innovative and community-focused science education

name: Student Volunteers at VUMC
description:
The goal of this organization is to create a stronger connection between the Vanderbilt undergraduate community and the VUMC volunteer program. This organization will provide students with easier access to VUMC volunteering, expedite the onboarding and orientation of new volunteers, create leadership opportunities for experienced volunteers, and increase undergraduate awareness of volunteer opportunities

Having an organization to support the VUMC volunteer program gives a platform to spread awareness for volunteering, gives structure to allow for peer leadership, and can provide student-specific information. Through an early semester informational session, students can be told about vaccination forms and human resources documents and learn how to become a volunteer. Through peer leadership, our executive team will work to create a sustainable organization which spreads undergraduate volunteer involvement. At-large organization members are given a voice to share volunteering ideas to improve the VUMC volunteer program. A volunteering network can work to manage expectations of volunteer placement progression, help schedule volunteers to fill in each others shifts, and encourages volunteers continue their involvement beyond simply "checking the box."
summary: This organization seeks to create a stronger connection between Vanderbilt undergraduate students and the VUMC volunteer program. We are building a network to support the undergraduate volunteer community while improving volunteer patient care at VUMC.

name: Basement Films
summary: A community meant to create short films produced, written and directed by us. The goal is to provide experience to filmmakers who want to showcase their artistic vision through short films, as well as to host an end-of-year student film festival.

name: Department of Biomedical Informatics Graduate Student Association
summary: The DBMI GSA serves the graduate students who are enrolled in a degree program within the department of biomedical informatics.

name: Vanderbilt Black Business Student Association
description: Vanderbilt's Black Business Student Association (BBSA) is deeply committed to amplifying access to meaningful career opportunities in the business sector for Black students. Through an array of focused initiatives, educational workshops, and impactful partnerships with industry leaders, we aim to connect our members with the vital resources and networks they need to succeed. Our goal extends beyond immediate job placements; we aim to equip our members with the skills, insights, and connections that will enable them to excel in various career fields over the long term. By providing mentorship opportunities, facilitating internship application workshops, and hosting career development seminars, we strive to create a supportive and resource-rich environment that empowers our members to reach their fullest potential. This organization is open to all Vanderbilt students.
summary: Vanderbilt BBSA is committed to amplifying access to career opportunities in business for Black students. Through focused initiatives and partnerships, we aim to connect members with resources that will help them succeed in a variety of career fields.

name: Vanderbilt University Business Exploration Club
description: https://web.groupme.com/join_group/99029609/IjboHnsT
summary: The Vanderbilt University Business Exploration Club (VUBEC) allows students to explore the business world, learn about different industries, and gain practical skills through various activities such as company visits, speaker events, and workshops.

name: Pharmacology Seminars, WIPs and JCs
summary: The page lists all seminars, WIPs and Journal Clubs of the Pharmacology Department, and provides the ability to keep track of student attendance.

name: Jewish Voice for Peace at Vanderbilt
description: Jewish Voice for Peace at Vanderbilt is an organization of Jewish students opposed to Israel's apartheid practices and colonialism. We seek to raise awareness about these injustices, shift discourse within our community, and end Vanderbilt's complicity.
summary: Jewish Voice for Peace at Vanderbilt is an organization of Jewish students opposed to Israel's apartheid practices and colonialism. We seek to raise awareness about these injustices, shift discourse within our community, and end Vanderbilt's complicity.

name: Curb Center for Art, Enterprise and Public Policy
description: The Curb Center's mission is to elevate art as a mode of inquiry, a way of understanding, and a celebration of the human spirit. Through performances, exhibitions, artist residencies, and Creative Inquiry Grants for students, faculty, and staff, we aim to amplify the presence and impact of the arts on Vanderbilt's campus.
summary: The Curb Center's mission is to elevate art as a mode of inquiry, a way of understanding, and a celebration of the human spirit.

name: Protection of Minors
summary: Protection of Minors policy at Vanderbilt University

name: Vanderbilt Cube Club
description: The Vanderbilt Cube Club is an organization of students and faculty interested in all things related to Rubik’s Cubes. This club serves as a community for cube collectors, studiers, competitive speedsolvers, and anyone with general interest in the Rubik’s Cube to meet and discuss this hobby. A focal point of this organization is to host an annual official Rubik’s Cube competition on Vanderbilt University’s campus, accessible to those at all skill levels.
summary: The Vanderbilt Cube Club is an organization of students and faculty interested in all things related to Rubik’s Cubes.

name: Vanderbilt Pride Serve
description: The purpose of this organization is to advocate for the interests and rights of the LGBTQ+ community, especially for LGBTQ+ youth, and raise awareness about the many identities under the LGBTQ+ umbrella and general LGBTQ+ issues, especially among grade school and collegiate students. The organization will be focused on advocating for issues locally in Tennessee, in other states within the United States, and at the federal level. We have three committees of volunteers: awareness, advocacy and outreach. The awareness committee primarily designs and posts graphics raising awareness of LGBTQ+ identities or issues or celebrating LGBTQ+ history, pride days/months, or intersectionality with other marginalized groups. The advocacy committee focuses on researching state and federal proposed bills concerning the LGBTQ+ community and advocate against or for them by writing to appropriate legislators/policy makers and facilitating similar efforts from others. The final committee, the outreach committee, aims to host events aiming to raise awareness of LGBTQ+ identities or issues or celebrating LGBTQ+ history, pride days/months, or intersectionality with other marginalized groups and to increase the reach of our mission.
summary: Pride Serve is a service organization aiming to advocate for the interests and rights of the LGBTQ+ community, especially for LGBTQ+ youth and raise awareness about the many identities under the LGBTQ+ umbrella and general LGBTQ+ issues.

name: Vanderbilt University Riichi Mahjong League
summary: Uniting Vandy's community with a passion for riichi mahjong. Experience the rich cultural tapestry of this intellectual game through regular engaging sessions and inclusive support for all enthusiasts.

name: The Amp
description: The Amp @ Vanderbilt University is a student-led collective of creatives passionate about music. With journalistic integrity, The Amp reports on new releases, local musicians, the state of music culture, and stories of music fandom. In addition, The Amp graphics team creates visuals to emphasize and contextualize the writing team's thoughts, hot takes, and research. Outside of these creative endeavors, The Amp also seeks to promote and aid in the development of a social music-oriented community.
summary: The Amp @ Vanderbilt University is a student-led collective of creatives passionate about music. With journalistic integrity, The Amp reports on new releases, local musicians, the state of music culture, and stories of music fandom.

name: The Carpenter Program in Religion, Gender, and Sexuality
description: The  Carpenter Program in Religion, Gender and Sexuality is located at the Divinity School and interacts with the entire Vanderbilt community through offering events and co-sponsoring programs with our campus partners. Since its founding in 1995, the Carpenter Program has empowered conversations related to the confluences of religion, gender, and sexuality. Our work on such issues as LGBTQIA+ equality and reproductive health, rights and justice, aims to be deeply intersectional, intellectually informed, ethically responsible, and practically tested. Students can participate through our co-curricular programming and our Certificate in Religion, Gender, and Sexuality Studies. For more information, contact Assistant Director Dr. Rachel A. Heath (rachel.a.heath@vanderbilt.edu).
summary: Our work on such issues as LGBTQIA+ equality and reproductive health, rights and justice, aims to be deeply intersectional, intellectually informed, ethically responsible, and practically tested.

name: Risk Management
description:
The Office of Risk and Insurance Management supports the university by providing mission-focused risk management and insurance services. We achieve this by:

  1. Understanding the educational, research, service, and business goals of the university.
  2. Using internal and higher education market data to drive trends analysis and risk management strategy and advocating for processes that address risk management.
  3. Evaluating, advising and procuring insurance coverage for the institution as a risk transfer strategy.
  4. Serving as a partner and advisor to university departments and equipping departments with risk-aware skill sets, empowering them to understand the implications in making decisions to assume, mitigate, transfer, or eliminate the impact of risk.
  5. Advising departments on preferred insurance coverage related to contractual agreements between the university and vendors, contractors, or other outside corporations.
  6. Approaching incident claims management by reviewing occurrences with department stakeholders for root causes and contributing factors, applying policy, process, and/or regulatory compliance in our response.
  7. Practicing ethical and just practices in resolution of claims. 

We hope this website assists you in your daily work. See the "Our Services" menu at the top of this page for detailed information. For general Risk and Insurance Management questions, contact us at 615-322-5155 or email riskandinsurance@vanderbilt.edu.
summary: The Office of Risk and Insurance Management supports the university by providing mission-focused risk management and insurance services.

name: Vanderbilt New Music Society
description: Our vision is a to create an environment where students feel empowered and excited to get involved with new music and create and collaborate on projects with their peers. If you want to learn more about new music, organize and participate in performance art, build a sound installation, commission and record new works, or run your own unique project, this organization is for you! We are an organizing body aimed at providing resources and support for small group projects, while regularly coordinating larger events to provide a social collaborative space for our members.
summary: NMS supports, uplifts, and engages the new music community at Vanderbilt by programming and supporting student events and projects related to contemporary art music.

name: Students Supporting Israel
description:
Students Supporting Israel (SSI) is a pro-Israel international campus movement that supports the State of Israel. 

Our mission is to be a clear and confident pro-Israel voice on college campuses and to support students in grassroots pro-Israel advocacy.
summary: Students Supporting Israel (SSI) is a pro-Israel international campus movement that supports the State of Israel.

name: Vandy Comics and Cartoons
description: Vandy Comics is a fun opportunity for students to discuss or introduce themselves to the world of comics and cartoons. We have many different kinds of events from rotating comic workshops to comic creator and cartoonist talks and our annual Comic Arts Festival. Our goal is to show the love and art for comics and encourage students to do the same no matter their skill level in art or writing.
summary: The club fosters appreciation, discussion, and engagement with comics and cartoons through activities, events, and community involvement. It addresses interest in all areas of comics and cartoons, from creating them to providing a space to discuss them.

name: Critical Pedagogies Collective
summary: We practice critical pedagogies by facilitating spaces for critical discourse, fostering multidisciplinary research collaboration between graduate students and faculty, and enhancing the academic and professional experiences of graduate students.

name: The Energy Transition Society
summary: Join the Vanderbilt Energy Transition Society to prepare yourself for careers at the forefront of sustainable innovation. Meet and greet industry experts in: Renewable electricity, EVs, Climate policy, Sustainable fuels, Recycling and more!

name: Thai Student Association
description: This organization aims to bring a fresh and vibrant cultural experience to campus. We want to highlight the diverse and rich traditions of Thailand in a way that hasn’t been explored before. Through cultural festivals, culinary events, and collaborative initiatives with other Asian and Southeast Asian groups on campus, we’re looking to contribute to a unique space for cultural exchange in the broader Vanderbilt community. Beyond that, we’re committed to reaching out to alumni and widening Vanderbilt’s outreach in Thailand, creating networking opportunities that extend across borders. Our goal is to provide a platform where students can actively engage, learn, and celebrate Thai culture in a lively and inclusive setting. We believe this approach, coupled with a global perspective and bridging connections, sets our association apart as a dynamic and enriching addition to the Vanderbilt community. Not only do we want to bring Thailand to Vanderbilt, we want to bring Vanderbilt to Thailand as well.
summary: The Thai Student Organization aims to foster a deep appreciation and understanding of Thai culture within the campus community. Our goal is to create a vibrant and inclusive space where students can actively engage with Thailand's culture and traditions.

name: Vandy Sales
description:
Who are we?

We are Vandy Sales!! Our mission is to provide members with the resources needed to learn to ‘sell yourself’ and to master the skill of sales. Through monthly skills development workshops and speakers, we want to raise awareness of the post-grad career that is sales, giving students another avenue to enter the business world. We are open to all students who want to improve their networking, persuasion, interview and ultimately sales skills!
summary: Vandy Sales aims for members to understand what exactly sales is, and that it is an imperative skill in any profession and industry. The club will provide members with the skills to succeed further in any business role, especially sales-related jobs.

name: Vanderbilt Philosophy Club
description: Vanderbilt Philosophy Club during the fall semester 2024 will continue offer exciting colloquiums. Meanwhile, we introduce the philosophy directed reading program (DRP) where students are connected to grad students specialize in their areas of interests.
summary: Vanderbilt Philosophy Club aims to help philosophy enthusiasts know each other outside of classroom and engage in philosophy in exciting ways including reading groups, colloquiums, movie nights and conferences.

name: Civil Discourse Lab
description: Freedom of speech is a bedrock principle of Vanderbilt University. The mission of the Civil Discourse Lab is to foster our commitment to this principle by providing programming that equips the Vanderbilt and larger Nashville community with the skills necessary to communicate and collaborate across differences.
summary: The mission is to foster our commitment to the principle of free speech by providing programming that equips Vanderbilt and the larger Nashville community with the skills necessary to communicate and collaborate across differences.

name: Vanderbilt for Ukraine
description: Vanderbilt for Ukraine is a student-led human rights advocacy organization, which aims to promote Ukrainian culture and history on Vanderbilt campus and beyond. Now, Ukraine is suffering from the unprovoking war started by Russia in 2014 continuing with the dull-scale invasion in 2022. Our goal is to expand Ukrainian presence and to establish ground for discussions, cultural events and meetings with prominent lectures.
summary: Vanderbilt for Ukraine is a student-led human rights advocacy organization, which aims to promote Ukrainian culture and history on Vanderbilt campus and beyond.

name: Reformed University Fellowship International
description: Reformed University Fellowship International (RUF-I) is a campus community dedicated to fostering cross-cultural friendships and fulfilling the spiritual interests and needs of international students. Grounded in the Scriptures, RUF-I seeks to promote a multicultural environment that embodies the implications of the Christian faith by showing hospitality to students from all nations, exploring faith and spirituality together with an emphasis on the Bible, and equipping students for lives of service and flourishing in their vocations, particularly guiding those who identify as Christians to deepen their faith in accordance with orthodox beliefs.
summary: RUF-I is a multicultural community for international students at Vanderbilt to build friendships, explore Christianity, and grow in faith.

name: Department of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering
description: The Department of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering offers premier, one-of-a-kind research and educational opportunities in cutting-edge topics like regenerative medicine, molecular modeling and simulation, biofuels and more.
summary: Department of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering

name: Legal Ascent
description: Legal Ascent is dedicated to all law school students seeking to connect with the legal community through rigorous outdoor activity. Our goal is to create a supportive community that reinforces legal concepts of creative thinking and problem solving through the physical challenges of completing climbing routes. Through our weekly climbing meetups, we hope to foster a dynamic, team-based support structure to guide students as they navigate law school.
summary: Legal Ascent is dedicated to all law school students seeking to connect with the legal community through rigorous outdoor activity. Our goal is to create a supportive community that reinforces legal concepts of creative thinking and problem solving...

name: Vanderbilt Undergraduate Law Review
description: Vanderbilt Undergraduate Law Review (VULR) is a non-partisan undergraduate law journal dedicated to empowering the student body to engage meaningfully with a diverse range of case law.
summary: Vanderbilt Undergraduate Law Review (VULR) is a non-partisan undergraduate law journal dedicated to empowering the student body to engage meaningfully with a diverse range of case law.

name: Master of Genetic Counseling Program
summary: Master of Genetic Counseling (M.G.C.) program educates students to become genetic counselors in genomic medicine.

name: Office of the Provost Graduate Assistantship Program
description: The Office of the Provost proudly partners with Peabody College to offer the Office of the Provost Graduate Assistantship Program. This program includes graduate assistantship and graduate area coordinator positions across several offices in the Office of the Provost and Vice Provost areas at Vanderbilt University. These assistantships, which are available to students studying on-campus, provide compensation and allow students the opportunity to connect theory learned in the classroom with practical work experience.
summary: The Office of the Provost proudly partners with Peabody College to offer the Office of the Provost Graduate Assistantship Program.

name: First View
description: The student leaders of FirstVU and the Office of Undergraduate Education offer the First View Summer Program, a 5-week online transition program dedicated to launching success at Vanderbilt, focusing on college readiness to help you excel personally, socially, professionally, and academically. FirstVU is a student organization dedicated to first-generation college students, defined as those whose in-home parents/guardians only attended a 2-year higher education institution or who have a high school education or less.
summary: First View is a programmatic initiative from the Office of Undergraduate Education to support empower first-generation students as they transition to Vanderbilt University.

name: VUSE Student Hub
description: The VUSE Student Hub supports School of Engineering undergraduate students through advising, registration assistance, academic programming, and connecting students to resources
summary: The VUSE Student Hub supports School of Engineering undergraduate students through advising, registration assistance, academic programming, and connecting students to resources.

name: Global Safety and Health
summary: The health, safety, and security of students, faculty, and staff are of primary importance to Vanderbilt. The Vanderbilt Global Safety and Health team is here to support and guide you.

name: Oliver C. Carmichael College
description:
Emily Pendergrass, Faculty Head of College

Emily Pendergrass taught elementary and middle school students for eleven years. While working alongside middle school students, she finished her Ph.D. in Language and Literacy Education at the University of Georgia. While at UGA, Emily worked extensively with the Red Clay Writing Project, part of the National Writing Project. Currently, Emily teaches courses in Reading Education where she is the also the Director of the Reading Education Specialists Program. Additionally, Emily works closely with the local public schools in literacy coaching and facilitating professional development workshops. 

The Residential College

Oliver C. Carmichael College is the newest and final addition to the collection of residential colleges in the West End Neighborhood. Opened in fall 2024, Carmichael College houses upper-division students and continues working toward the programmatic and educational goals of Residential Colleges.
summary: Oliver C. Carmichael College

name: Vanderbilt Center for Addiction Research
description: Advancements made at the Vanderbilt Center for Addiction Research (VCAR) are instrumental in uncovering the intricate mechanisms underlying the development of addiction and substance use disorder in the brain. We will list VCAR seminars, journal clubs, works in progress session as well as other center events that we will have.
summary: Advancements made at the Vanderbilt Center for Addiction Research (VCAR) are instrumental in uncovering the intricate mechanisms underlying the development of addiction and substance use disorder in the brain.

name: Division of People, Culture and Belonging
description: The Division of People, Culture and Belonging plays a pivotal role in shaping the experiences of our employees. Serving over 7,000 faculty and staff, which impacts the lives of more than 14,000 students, our team is at the heart of Vanderbilt's commitment to nurturing a community where every individual's journey is valued. This page provides opportunities for staff to engage in PCB programming and events.
summary: The Division of People, Culture and Belonging plays a pivotal role in shaping the experiences of our employees. This page provides opportunities for staff to engage in PCB programming and events.

name: Dialogue Vanderbilt
description:
Dialogue Vanderbilt is a campus-wide initiative putting into practice Vanderbilt’s enduring commitment to free expression and civil discourse, serving as a catalyst and connector for better conversations.

Vanderbilt’s transformative education supports our students' development of strong dialogue habits—equipping Vanderbilt graduates to thrive in complexity, connect across differences, and lead with courage and clarity—preparing them to succeed and drive meaningful impact in their careers and beyond. 

Fueled by our commitment to radical collaboration, Dialogue Vanderbilt works with students, faculty and administration to provide tools and programs to engage in respectful, thoughtful conversations—across differences and on any topic, helping our students develop healthy habits of dialogue, debate and deliberation.
summary: Dialogue Vanderbilt is a campus-wide initiative putting into practice Vanderbilt’s enduring commitment to free expression and civil discourse, serving as a catalyst and connector for better conversations.

name: Student Veterans of America
description:
1. Promote awareness of the challenges and opportunities faced by Veterans transitioning from military to civilian and academic life within the University, the local community, and beyond.
2. Foster a campus community that is more inclusive and supportive of Veterans, military members, and their families.
3. Advocate for the academic, professional, and personal needs of student Veterans at Vanderbilt University.
4. Provide resources, mentorship, and networking opportunities to assist Veterans in achieving their academic and career goals.
5. Collaborate with the Vanderbilt community to highlight the contributions and experiences of Veterans, enriching the diversity of the campus culture.
summary: This Vanderbilt University Chapter provides resources, funding, and support to help student Veterans achieve their goals, connecting them with the national Student Veterans of America network to further their education and career advancement.

name: Armenian Student Association
summary: The Armenian Student Association promotes cultural awareness, education on Armenian history and contemporary issues, and community support. We aim to create an environment that celebrates Armenian heritage and advocates for Armenian causes at Vanderbilt.

name: Live Learn Lead Academy
description:
The Live. Learn. Lead. Academy is a year-long, hands-on leadership program designed to empower undergraduate students by immersing them in leadership experiences within the Vanderbilt University and Nashville communities. Those selected for the program can use this opportunity for Immersion Vanderbilt, either as an immersive experience or a culminating project (or both).

The Academy features a small cohort model, bringing together students from across Vanderbilt's undergraduate schools/colleges to foster collaboration, enhance leadership practices, and build strong community networks. Students will engage in self-reflection and continuous learning to develop essential skills and insights for future success in their careers and communities.

Inspired by the hugely successful 2024 Live. Learn. Lead Symposium, which focused on conflict resolution, work-life balance, mentorship, and networking, this new leadership academy is open to all Vanderbilt undergraduate sophomores, juniors, and seniors.
summary: The Live. Learn. Lead. Academy is a year-long, hands-on leadership program designed to empower undergraduate students by immersing them in leadership experiences within the Vanderbilt University and Nashville communities.

name: The Asian American Advocacy Project
description: The Asian American Advocacy Project (TAAAP) is a student-run advocacy group dedicated to creating structural change at Vanderbilt University through collaboration with faculty and staff, creating an inclusive academic community for Asian American and Native Hawaiian/Pacific Islander (AANHPI) students at all ten of the university's schools.
summary: The Asian American Advocacy Project (TAAAP) is a student-run advocacy group dedicated to creating structural change at Vanderbilt University through collaboration with faculty and staff, creating an inclusive academic community for Asian American and Nat

name: Community Celebrations Calendar
description: The Community Celebrations Calendar helps highlight the various months dedicated to recognizing the variety of identities and perspectives within our community. This calendar resource available through Anchor Link will help students, staff, and faculty to easily note that they would like their event to be considered on the Community Celebrations Calendar, which highlights events taking place across the university.
summary: The Community Celebrations Calendar helps highlight the various months dedicated to recognizing the variety of identities and perspectives within our community.

organizationType.name: Student Affairs Department, Office, or Program

name: Chuckle Charity
description: We user humorous means for charitable ends.  Through comedic volunteerism, fundraising, and community engagement, we turn laughter into action, using humor as a means to uplift and make an impact.  We see the power laughter has to tear down barriers and bring out the best in people, and want to use that to bring out the best in the world.
summary: Raising the world's spirits with laughter and philanthropy

name: Peabody Academic Mentors
description: Our goals include helping guide students of similar academic backgrounds, by providing advice, leadership, and knowledge from our student perspective. We help guide students through their respective academic pathways by answering questions and referring students to the correct contact in the Peabody’s Office of Academic Services (OAS). Additionally, we act as ambassadors for Peabody Students and the OAS.
summary: We help guide students by providing advice, leadership, and knowledge from our student perspective. We answer questions and refer students to the correct contact in the Peabody OAS. Additionally, we act as ambassadors for Peabody Students and the OAS.

name: Plant Futures at Vanderbilt University
description:
Plant Futures at Vanderbilt University is part of a nationwide network of student organizations, with chapters at universities like Harvard, Columbia, Northwestern, University of Michigan, UCLA, and UC Berkeley, all under the umbrella of the Plant Futures Initiative.

 

At Vanderbilt, the goal of Plant Futures is to engage all students curious about the relationship between food and sustainability. We aim to achieve this through guest speaker events featuring individuals with unique perspectives on food's impact on sustainability and niche careers in the sustainable food and beverage industry. Key objectives of our organization include educating the Vanderbilt student body on the various environmental impacts of food, as well as promoting careers in food systems and sustainability that often receive little exposure on our campus.

 

Another primary goal of Plant Futures Vanderbilt is to create a thriving community of engaged and interested students who share common interests. We organize community events such as plant-based picnics and taste tests of dishes from local vegan restaurants to foster this sense of community.

 

We invite all students curious about sustainable food systems and plant-based living to join us!
summary: The Plant Futures Initiative is a national non-profit dedicated to promoting learning, career development, student activism, and community-building relating to plant-based living and sustainable food systems on college campuses nationwide.

name: The Dementia Art Therapy Alliance at Vanderbilt
description:
The Dementia Art Therapy Alliance at Vanderbilt is dedicated to enhancing the quality of life for elderly individuals suffering from neurodegenerative diseases, such as Alzheimer’s and dementia. Our mission is to leverage the therapeutic power of art to improve memory, cognitive function, and overall well-being among this vulnerable population.

We provide structured art therapy sessions designed to stimulate mental engagement, encourage creative expression, and foster social interaction. Our group members work closely with nursing homes and memory care facilities in the Nashville area to deliver personalized and impactful art experiences.

Through our programs, we aim to not only support cognitive health but also to offer companionship and joy to individuals facing the challenges of neurodegenerative conditions.
summary: The Dementia Art Therapy Alliance at Vanderbilt uses art therapy to improve the quality of life for elderly individuals with Alzheimer’s and dementia. We offer structured sessions to boost memory, cognitive function, and well-being.

name: Vanderbilt Peace of Mind
description: Peace of Mind addresses hospice understaffing in Davidson County by encouraging Vanderbilt students to volunteer, removing financial barriers with free Uber rides, and partnering with AliveHospice to streamline the volunteering process.
summary: Peace of Mind addresses hospice understaffing in Davidson County by encouraging Vanderbilt students to volunteer, removing financial barriers with free Uber rides, and partnering with AliveHospice to streamline the volunteering process.

name: Vanderbilt Sports and Society Club
summary: The Vanderbilt Sports and Society Club seeks to investigate the unique connections between sports and society, especially among Vanderbilt students and student-athletes, while elevating an understanding of how sports and society impact each other.

name: Vanderbilt Undergraduate Pre-Health Association
summary: The Vanderbilt Undergraduate Pre-Health Association (VUPHA) is a pre-professional student organization for Vanderbilt University undergraduates who are interested in pursuing a health-related career beyond the Pre-Medical track.

name: Vanderbilt Addiction Awareness Association
description:
The Addiction Awareness Association is a student-led nonprofit dedicated to uniting communities in the fight against addiction.


We raise awareness, reduce stigma, and support recovery through education, advocacy, and service.
By connecting people across disciplines and backgrounds, we work toward a future where compassion, knowledge, and action lead the way to healing and hope.
summary: AAA stands on four pillars: service, education, rehabilitation, and advocacy. We aim to foster a community that is more accepting and understanding of those battling substance use disorders. By reshaping the conversation around addiction, we seek to de-s

name: Vanderbilt Quantum Initiative
description: Vanderbilt Quantum Initiative is a student organization at the forefront of quantum technology education. We unite students across disciplines to explore and innovate in the quantum realm. Our activities span from hands-on quantum computing workshops to collaborative research projects, industry partnerships, and thought-provoking seminars. Our initiative not only educates but also inspires, connecting students with cutting-edge quantum technologies and the visionaries shaping the quantum revolution.
summary: VQI is a pioneering university organization dedicated to advancing quantum science and technology education. We bring together students from physics, computer science, engineering, and related fields to explore quantum.

name: Pre-Health Society for Vanderbilt Student Athletes
description: The Pre-Health Society for Vanderbilt Student Athletes is a student-led organization dedicated to supporting student-athletes pursuing careers in the healthcare industry. Recognizing the unique challenges faced by student-athletes, this organization provides a tailored community that fosters both academic and professional development. Through monthly meetings, guest speakers, and workshops, members gain valuable insights into healthcare careers, medical school applications, and networking opportunities. The society also emphasizes community engagement through volunteer activities at local healthcare facilities, giving student-athletes hands-on experience while contributing to the surrounding community.
summary: The Pre-Health Society for Vanderbilt Student Athletes supports student-athletes pursuing healthcare careers by providing a tailored community that fosters academic and professional growth through meetings, workshops, guest speakers, and volunteering.

name: Cal Turner Program for Moral Leadership
description: The Cal Turner Program for Moral Leadership engages the Vanderbilt professional school communities and beyond in questions of moral and ethical leadership, skill-building, and collaborative, interprofessional and cross-sector engagement with pressing moral issues.
summary: The Cal Turner Program engages the Vanderbilt professional school communities and beyond in questions of moral and ethical leadership, skill-building, and collaborative, interprofessional and cross-sector engagement with pressing moral issues.

name: Vanderbilt Entertainment Club
summary: A collaborative community for aspiring entertainment professionals to build their careers and creative networks.

name: Mongolian Student Association
description: The Vanderbilt University Mongolian Student Association is a cultural organization dedicated to promoting and representing Mongolian culture on campus and beyond. Founded by a group of Mongolian students, our goal is to share our rich heritage, traditions, and history with the Vanderbilt community. While we began as a space for Mongolian students to connect, we are open to everyone interested in learning about our culture. We host a range of events, workshops, and activities that showcase the diverse aspects of Mongolia—from traditional arts, music, and dance to language, cuisine, and history. Through our efforts, we strive to create an inclusive community where people from all backgrounds can come together to engage with and appreciate Mongolia's unique cultural heritage.
summary: The Vanderbilt University Mongolian Student Association promotes Mongolian culture through events and activities, creating an inclusive community for all to connect and appreciate this unique heritage.

name: Neurotech @ Vanderbilt
summary: Neurotech @ Vanderbilt exposes students to neurotechnology through lectures and hands-on experiences. We have guest lecturers from academia and industry explain their work. We also compete in an annual neurotechnology competition.

name: VALIANT Attempt
description: This is an organization for the undergraduate and graduate affiliates of VALIANT. We aim to provide our community with professional and social events to encourage collaboration and friendship.
summary: This is an organization for the undergraduate and graduate affiliates of VALIANT. We aim to provide our community with professional and social events to encourage collaboration and friendship.

name: Vanderbilt Bioinformatics and Technology Club
description: The Vanderbilt Bioinformatics and Technology (VBiT) Club strives to foster a vibrant community of students passionate about the intersection of biology, informatics, and technology. We hope to create a space where students can actively think about and openly discuss how technology can combine with biology and be incorporated into healthcare to further enhance patient care and treatment quality. We hope to spark diverse perspectives and advancing understanding in bioinformatics and biotechnology on the Vanderbilt campus through a variety of programmings including informational workshops, guest speaker events with industry leaders, lab tours, mentorship programs, informative newsletter, etc. We are committed to equip our members with a well-rounded perspective on bioinformatics & technology and its application that they will bring forth towards their future careers.
summary: VBiT strives to highlight current issues within the bioinformatics and technology field by offering a variety of networking and informative programs. We hope to spark conversations around how technology can enhance healthcare quality and efficiency.

name: PANJ
description: Our organization is dedicated to uniting and empowering Punjabi students at Vanderbilt University. We celebrate and promote Punjabi culture, traditions, and identity through a variety of engaging events and initiatives. By hosting cultural festivals, educational workshops, and community service activities, we aim to enrich the campus experience, increase Punjabi representation, and create a supportive community. Our mission is to foster a deeper understanding of Punjabi heritage while encouraging more Punjabi students to consider Vanderbilt as a welcoming and vibrant place to study
summary: PANJ unites Punjabi students at Vanderbilt, celebrating and promoting Punjabi culture, traditions, and identity through educational events, cultural festivals, and community service.

name: National Society of Black Women in Medicine
description: The National Society of Black Women in Medicine is committed to supporting current and future healthcare professionals and scientists in efforts to combat educational and professional racial inequities by promoting attainability through educational resources, unity, and mentorship.
summary: The National Society of Black Women in Medicine is a multi-disciplined organization that works to increase the recruitment and retention of Black women pursuing broad careers in medicine.

name: Tech Law Society
description: The VLS Tech Law Society brings together students who share an interest in technology law—how the law promotes technological innovation, limits technological abuse, and responds to technological change—and the application of technology to the study and practice of law.
summary: Tech Law Society seeks to raise student awareness of the legal issues associated with technology law (e.g, AI/ML, cryptocurrency, blockchain, IoT, FinTech, cybersecurity, data security, clean energy technology, biotechnology, healthcare technology, etc.)

name: VSG Giving Tree
description: Angel Tree is a yearly philanthropic initiative with community partners to “adopt” a local child and give holiday presents to children who may not otherwise receive any. Hosted by Vanderbilt Student Government.
summary: Angel Tree is a yearly philanthropic initiative with community partners to “adopt” a local child and give holiday presents to children who may not otherwise receive any. Hosted by Vanderbilt Student Government.

name: BridgeVU
description: BridgeVU is a dynamic student organization dedicated to fostering free, respectful, and solution-oriented dialogue across the political spectrum. We bring together students from all backgrounds and affiliations to engage in open, empathetic conversations about significant issues. Through debates, panel discussions, and workshops, BridgeVU provides a unique platform for YOUR perspective to be heard, understood, and valued. Join us to bridge divides, challenge your thinking, and contribute to a more thoughtful campus community.
summary: A platform that unites students from all political backgrounds to engage in free, respectful, solution-oriented dialogue through debates, discussions, and workshops, ultimately fostering a more inclusive and thoughtful campus community.

name: Vanderbilt Pediatric Association
description: The goals of VPA are to promote awareness and education of the Pediatric Field amongst its members and the Vanderbilt Community at large, to form strong links and opportunities among students and Faculty interested in Pediatrics, and to keep the Vanderbilt Community informed on events and issues that affect the Pediatric Field. Our goal is to meet that need by providing educational resources, connecting students to opportunities, and spreading awareness. The organization will provide students with easier access to educational opportunities to learn about the Pediatric field and volunteer opportunities for involvement by expediting the search for them as well as providing connections with professions. Members can learn through listening in on speakers, round table discussion groups, as well as having access to shadowing and research programs Vanderbilt has. VPA will promote a space where students can have enriched conversations about pediatric advocacy and topics associated with childrens’ mental health and child immunizations. Other members can have easier access to pediatric volunteer opportunities at the Children’s Hospital so they can apply what they learn through this organization in an indirect or direct way of service.
summary: The goals of VPA are to promote awareness and education of the Pediatric Field amongst its members and to keep the Vanderbilt Community informed on events and issues that affect the Pediatric Field through pediatric opportunities and mentorship.

name: MHS- M.A. Masters in Social Foundations of Health
description: The M.A. in the Social Foundations of Health provides students with the interdisciplinary knowledge and skills that future health care professionals require. The field of contemporary health care is increasingly shaped by the realization that social, environmental, personal, and structural factors are as essential as basic biological processes to understanding patient’s illness and suffering. Social and cultural aspects of illness and health are a growing component of pre-health education. The program is aimed at students who want to gain research experience, enhance their interdisciplinary training, and strengthen their applications before entering the job market, medical school, professional school, or doctoral programs.
summary: The M.A. in the Social Foundations of Health provides students with the interdisciplinary knowledge and skills that future health care professionals require.

name: Black Atlantic Speakers Series
description: In 2002, with the support of the Robert Penn Warren Center for the Humanities, Jane Landers and Shawn Goudie, Department of English, launched the interdisciplinary Circum-Atlantic Studies Seminar (CASS). CASS participants from diverse constituencies on campus and invited speakers discuss the latest scholarship on the Atlantic World, a field that links Africa, Europe, the Americas, and the Caribbean. In 2007, in commemoration of the 200th anniversary of the British abolition of slavery, CASS launched a new Black Atlantic Speakers Series. That series has since hosted some of the most renowned scholars in the field to speak about their research, publications, and digital projects for Black History month. Celso Castilho joined Landers as co-director of CASS from 2018-2020 and in 2020 Daniel Genkins became the new co-director of the seminar. The Slave Societies Digital Archive assumed primary sponsorship of CASS and the Black Atlantic Speakers Series in 2023. Over the years, both programs have received support from the Department of History, the Jean and Alexander Heard Libraries, the Center for Latin American, Caribbean and Latinx Studies, the Department of African American and Diaspora Studies, The Robert Penn Warren Center for the Humanities, the Bishop Johnson Black Cultural Center, and the Graduate School VU-EDGE Program.
summary: This series has hosted some of the most renowned scholars in the field to speak about their research, publications, and digital projects for Black History month.

name: Canta Canta
description: Canta Canta is a student-led nonprofit that designs and distributes handmade interactive art books for pediatric patients, especially children from under-resources, uninsured, and immigrant communities. Created by Vanderbilt students, our books offer moments of joy, creativity, and connection to children receiving care in clinics across Tennessee and at partner hospitals nationally and internationally.
summary: Canta Canta is a student-led Vanderbilt nonprofit that creates and distributes handmade interactive art books to pediatric patients, especially from under-resourced, uninsured, and immigrant communities.

name: Baseball Analytics Association
description: The Baseball Analytics Association will acquaint its members with the applications of statistical and computational tools in baseball analysis. Activities include educational meetings, general discussions, and project work/presentation. Familiarity with statistics, programming, and the game of baseball is by no means required but may be helpful.
summary: The Baseball Analytics Association will acquaint its members with the applications of statistical and computational tools in baseball analysis. Activities include educational meetings, general discussions, and project work/presentation.

name: Athletes In Business
description: Athletes In Business at Vanderbilt University empowers student-athletes to successfully transition from the field or court to the business world. Our mission is to provide a comprehensive platform that bridges the gap between athletic commitment and business awareness through guest speakers, interactive workshops, and unique student-athlete networking opportunities. We strive to equip our members with the knowledge, skills, and connections necessary to excel in the business world, fostering a community of future leaders who are as competitive in their professional careers as they are in their sports.
summary: The purpose of the Athletes in Business Organization is to equip student-athletes with the skills and connections needed to transition from sports to the professional world and leverage their unique experiences for successful careers beyond the field or

name: IMA Vanderbilt
description:
For more than 100 years, The Institute of Management Accountants has been a champion of management accounting and finance professionals.

The organization was founded in Buffalo, N.Y., in 1919 as the National Association of Cost Accountants (NACA) to promote knowledge and professionalism among cost accountants and foster a wider understanding of their role in management.

In 1957, our name was changed to the National Association of Accountants (NAA), beginning an era in which we would later develop our hallmark credential, the CMA®.

In 1991, the organization's name was again changed to the Institute of Management Accountants (IMA), signifying our broader role as the association for accountants and financial professionals in business.

Today we are known by the shorthand—IMA—and continue the legacy of professional excellence begun nearly a century ago. “100 Years and Counting: ,” by: Bud Kulesza, CMA, CFM, 1999-2000 IMA Chair and John B. Pollara, CMA, 2007-2008 IMA Chair.
summary: For more than 100 years, The Institute of Management Accountants has been a champion of management accounting and finance professionals. Vanderbilt's chapter will give students the opportunity to become certified managerial accountants.

name: Vanderbilt Tumor Board Program
description:
The goals of the Vanderbilt Tumor Board Program are as follows:

  1. To provide undergraduate students the opportunity to shadow and attend tumor board meetings.
  2. To expose students to the clinical discussions and decision-making processes of healthcare professionals in oncology.
  3. To host biweekly lectures on topics such as cancer biology, treatment options, and recent advancements in oncology.
  4. To encourage students' interest in oncology and related fields of study and medical careers.
  5. To foster a strong community amongst pre-health students within the Vanderbilt undergraduate student body.
summary: The Vanderbilt Tumor Board Program aims to expose pre-health students to realistic operations of physicians treating patients with cancer through tumor board shadowing complemented by biweekly lectures.

name: Line Dancing Club
description:
Beginner line dancing lessons taught by professional instructors from Turn Their Heads now on campus for free! Join us for a study break- No dance experience necessary.

Join our Insta + GroupMe: https://linktr.ee/vandylinedancing
summary: Free line dancing lessons weekly on campus, no dance experience necessary!

name: PDP Student Activites Board
summary: The VUSM Professional Degree Program students of the Vanderbilt community coming together for professional and social events and opportunities.

name: VUSM-MD International Medical Student Society
description: The VUSM International Student Society supports non-U.S. citizen medical students through tailored mentorship, community building, and professional development. Please note this organization is only open to current VUSM MD Students.
summary: The VUSM International Student Society supports non-U.S. citizen medical students through tailored mentorship, community building, and professional development. Please note this organization is only open to current VUSM MD Students.

name: The Society of Vanderbilt Mathematics Graduate Students
summary:
This is an organization whose purpose it to foster community and promote professional
engagement among the Graduate Mathematics Students and anyone else at
Vanderbilt University who has passion for graduate level mathematics.

name: Vanderbilt Socio-Historical Society
description:
The objectives and purpose of this organization shall be:

 

  1. To increase Vanderbilt student engagement and awareness of the disciplinary intersections between sociology and history.
  2. To cultivate an environment whereby students and professors studying the humanities and sciences may affirm their commitment to a transdisciplinary perspective in the study of the world around them.
  3. To provide opportunities for Vanderbilt undergraduate students to engage with topical research across the various disciplines employed in socio-historical study.
  4. To provide a place for discussion to occur between students and researchers engaged in socio-historical research.
  5. To allow Vanderbilt students to meet and network with historians, sociologists and other scholars in the Nashville area and beyond.
  6. To foster a culture of intellectual curiosity and historically based connections in studying the world around them.

 

The core creed of the Vanderbilt Socio-Historical society is to enable the cultivation and development of fruitful and productive human connections, activity and thought without disciplinary or physical restrictions.

To provide the Vanderbilt, Nashville, and greater Tennessee communities with spaces for courteous and unconstrained intellectual discussions across disciplines.

Accomplishing its goals via trips to spaces, places, and institutions that hold significance or meaning in contributing toward advancing the collective human experience whether social, cultural, historical, or architectural— the society fundamentally commits itself to the ideals of pluralism in all of its undertakings, especially intellectually.
summary: Guest lectures from topical academics, social events, opportunities to engage with graduate research, and guided visits to historical, architectural, and environmental spaces of historic and cultural importance.

name: Leveraging Emerging Voices in Economics Research
description: We seek to develop undergraduate students’ curiosity and capability for economics by fostering a close-knit community, providing hands-on opportunities for skill-building, and promoting connections among students, faculty, and alumni.
summary: Lever supports every 'Dore interested in learning about economics or diving into research.

name: Vanderbilt Partners for Parkinson’s Program
description: Parkinson’s disease is a chronic neurodegenerative disorder that affects movement, coordination, and emotional well-being. With nearly 90,000 new cases being diagnosed every year in the United States, the need for increased support and understanding is greater than ever. The Partners for Parkinson’s Program seeks to address the significant isolation and social challenges often faced by individuals living with Parkinson’s.
summary: The Partners for Parkinson’s Program fosters meaningful connections between students and individuals with Parkinson’s, aiming to reduce social isolation while providing volunteers with a deeper understanding of neurological movement disorders.

name: Phi Delta Epsilon
description:
Phi Delta Epsilon (PhiDE) is a professional medical fraternity at Vanderbilt University, dedicated to supporting undergraduate students aspiring to pursue careers in medicine. PhiDE provides a unique combination of academic resources, leadership opportunities, social events, and philanthropic initiatives to enhance the personal and professional journeys of its members.

A cornerstone of PhiDE is its commitment to service, with members actively participating in meaningful philanthropic efforts that benefit the broader community. The fraternity also fosters academic excellence and personal growth through mentorship, collaboration, and leadership development.

Beyond academics, PhiDE creates a vibrant, close-knit community where members form strong connections with like-minded peers. Biweekly meetings, engaging social events, and collaborative projects foster camaraderie and provide a supportive environment that extends beyond the classroom.

PhiDE empowers its members with tools and opportunities to succeed academically, develop professionally, and build lifelong friendships..
summary: Phi Delta Epsilon (PhiDE) is a medical fraternity dedicated to supporting pre-medical students. By offering philanthropy events, social opportunities, and a strong sense of community, PhiDE fosters academic success, camaraderie, and professional growth.

name: Order on the Court
description: Order on the Court is a vibrant community dedicated to bringing fun, fitness, and friendly competition to the VLS community through the exciting sport of pickleball! Whether you’re a seasoned player or just curious about the game, we offer a variety of activities for all skill levels. Our programming includes casual play sessions, fun tournaments, and social events designed to keep things light-hearted and enjoyable. We aim to create a welcoming space where students can stay active, improve their game, and build lasting friendships. With pickleball’s easy-to-learn, low-impact nature, our club is perfect for anyone looking to try something new and get involved in a fast-growing sport. Join our GroupMe!
summary: Order on the Court offers students the opportunity to play, learn, and socialize through the sport of pickleball. It provides weekly casual play sessions, local tournaments, and social events for all skill levels.

name: Trail Lawyers
description: Trail Lawyers is a place for all Vanderbilt Law School students who want to explore and experience the outdoors to gather and spend that time together. Trail Lawyers seeks to create a place for all students and faculty who wish to experience the outdoors, regardless of skill or experience level. Activities can include hiking, camping, fishing, birding, and more.
summary: Trail Lawyers seeks to create a place for all students and faculty who wish to experience the outdoors, regardless of skill or experience level. Activities can include hiking, camping, fishing, birding, and more.

name: Nu Rho Psi
description: Nu Rho Psi is a student organization dedicated to discovery through collaboration and creating opportunities for students to become involved individuals. Our organization draws its founding principles from the Society for Neuroscience to advance the understanding of the nervous system, provide member development opportunities and educational resources, and promote general education. We aim to nurture the intellectual and ethical strength of our members and generate a collaborative network for both the present and the future.
summary: The mission of Nu Rho Psi is to encourage professional interest and excellence in scholarship, particularly in neuroscience as well as promote career development in neuroscience and related fields.

name: American Lung Cancer Screening Initiative
summary: ALCSI raises awareness of lung cancer screening in Vanderbilt, Nashville, and Tennessee; connecting people to local screening programs; and embedding awareness through community outreach, health education, and policy/advocacy change.

name: Lambda Phi Epsilon
description: Lambda Phi Epsilon (ΛΦΕ) is the world’s largest Asian-interest fraternity, founded on February 25, 1981 at UCLA to guide men on a lifelong journey of authenticity, leadership, and cultural pride. Building on that 44-year legacy, the Vanderbilt University Associate Chapter was officially chartered on March 23, 2025 by ten founding members, marking Lambda Phi Epsilon’s expansion into the Southeast region and bringing its pillars of brotherhood, scholarship, service, and cultural engagement to campus life.
summary: Vanderbilt University Associate Chapter was officially chartered on March 23, 2025, marking Lambda Phi Epsilon’s expansion into the Southeast region and bringing its pillars of brotherhood, scholarship, service, and cultural engagement to campus life.

name: The Sexual Health and Wellness Collaborative
summary: SHWC provides Peabody graduate students with comprehensive, inclusive, and accurate sex education information, enabling them to better serve their future students by teaching and promoting sexual health, consent, communication, and well-being.

name: Arts and Sciences: Beyond The Core
description:
The A&S College Core is the foundation for undergraduate study for College of Arts and Science. The College Core curriculum is flexible and intuitive, offering students common intellectual experiences, a firm grounding in the arts and the sciences, opportunities to explore their interests, and meaningful pathways through their education. Click here for full details.

The First-Year Core consists of two seminars, CORE 1010 (taken in the fall) and CORE 1020 (taken in the spring) worth four credit hours, even though the amount of time spent in the classroom and doing coursework is equivalent to three-credit-hours. So where does the fourth credit hour come from? It comes from the year-long “Beyond the Core” experience.

Beyond the Core is designed to help students explore their academic goals and intellectual passions. Students will explore resources and opportunities across campus and engage in an intentional set of co-curricular activities to support their personal and intellectual growth.
summary: The A&S College Core is the foundation for undergraduate study for College of Arts and Science. "Beyond the Core" is designed to help students explore their academic goals and intellectual passions.

name: School of Engineering
description: The Vanderbilt School of Engineering is dedicated to creating a welcoming, connected community where students, faculty, and staff thrive together.
summary: The Vanderbilt School of Engineering is dedicated to creating a welcoming, connected community where students, faculty, and staff thrive together.

name: Nucleate Vanderbilt
description: Nucleate Vanderbilt is a premier science-meets-business organization on a mission to educate and empower the next generation of life sciences leaders and entrepreneurs. We’re a vibrant community at the intersection of science, entrepreneurship, and venture capital, supporting students through skill-building workshops, speaker panels, networking events, and pitch competitions. Our members gain exposure to a wide range of career paths—including consulting, industry R&D, policy, and business development—but our primary focus is on launching students into biotech and life sciences entrepreneurship, venture capital, and investing. Our undergraduate program, Nucleate Dojo Explore, features a dynamic seminar series that introduces students to the biotech ecosystem and provides the essential tools for entrepreneurial success.
summary: Nucleate Vanderbilt is a premier science-meets-business organization on a mission to educate and empower the next generation of life sciences leaders and entrepreneurs.

name: Institute for the Advancement of Higher Education
description: The Institute for the Advancement of Higher Education (AdvancED) leads cutting-edge, research-backed innovations in pedagogy and student experience. Drawing on the talent, resources, and expertise of the Office of Education Design and Development and the Office of Learning Innovation, AdvancED exists to support and propel Vanderbilt’s faculty to design and lead world-class learning experiences.
summary: The Institute for the Advancement of Higher Education (AdvancED) leads cutting-edge, research-backed innovations in pedagogy and student experience.

name: Housing and Relocation
summary: This program is a great resource for off-campus housing needs.

name: Center for Latin American, Caribbean and Latinx Studies
summary: Our Center offers a variety of programs and events for students throughout the academic year, and we would like to use Anchorlink as a resource to promote our events and remain connected with the campus community.

name: Culture, Advocacy, and Leadership
description:
The Program in Culture, Advocacy, and Leadership (CAL) equips and inspires students to understand and engage with communities in the United States, the Americas, and the world. The CAL curriculum prepares students for civic engagement and public leadership. Through real-world experiences and innovative coursework, students develop the knowledge, values, and skills they need to contribute to a better future.

The name Culture, Advocacy, and Leadership signals the key components of the program and the competencies students need regardless of what they pursue after graduation.

  • Culture speaks to the importance of understanding history, people, and institutions, which need to inform any effort to make a difference in the world.
  • Advocacy speaks to the public-facing, community-engaged projects students will undertake as they develop a voice and a vision all their own.
  • Leadership speaks to the ideas, experiences, and skills that students must draw on when uniting people to tackle common problems.

The CAL major is both structured and individualized. All students take required foundational courses, but they also customize the major through courses they choose and real-world experiences related to their passions. Discover ways to customize your major.
summary: The Program in Culture, Advocacy, and Leadership (CAL) equips and inspires students to understand and engage with communities in the United States, the Americas, and the world.

name: Vanderbilt University Institute of National Security
description: In 2023, Vanderbilt began envisioning an Institute of National Security as part of the ambitious Discovery Vanderbilt, established to pursue vital solutions to the world's greatest challenges. Building on the university's ingrained culture of user-inspired, solutions-oriented research and innovation as well as connections to national security professionals across the United States Armed Forces, the intelligence community, and other entities, the Institute of National Security officially launched in 2024 under the leadership of founding director, General Paul M. Nakasone, with the goal of becoming the most trusted university ecosystem for national security solutions. We are undertaking this bold initiative at a time when there is urgency around the globe for the kind of radical collaboration that Vanderbilt University can uniquely deploy into national security settings to keep our nation safe and the world secure.
summary: In 2023, Vanderbilt began envisioning an Institute of National Security as part of the ambitious Discovery Vanderbilt, established to pursue vital solutions to the world's greatest challenges.

name: Legal Studies & Pre-Law Advising
description: The Legal Studies team at Vanderbilt Law School manages the undergraduate Legal Studies minor, the Master of Legal Studies program, the international LL.M. program, and the university's Pre-Law Advising office.
summary: The Legal Studies team at Vanderbilt Law School manages the undergraduate Legal Studies minor, the Master of Legal Studies program, the international LL.M. program, and the university's Pre-Law Advising office.

name: Women, Law, and Policy Program
summary: The Women, Law, and Policy Program aims to explore the ways in which law and policy shape our experiences in leadership, in the workplace and across other spheres.

name: College of Connected Computing
description: To provide more students with the skills and credentials needed in a society shaped by technology, Vanderbilt is establishing the College of Connected Computing, the university’s first new college in 40 years. The new, interdisciplinary college will advance breakthrough discoveries and strengthen computing education for every one of our students through a “computing for all” approach.
summary: To provide more students with the skills and credentials needed in a society shaped by technology, Vanderbilt is establishing the College of Connected Computing, the university’s first new college in 40 years.

name: Community Engaged Nursing Scholars
summary: Community Engaged Nursing Scholars (CENS) is a student-led organization at the Vanderbilt University School of Nursing that connects students with the Nashville community through service, education, advocacy, and partnerships focused on community engagem

name: Fashion Law Society
description: The purpose of this organization is to provide students interested in the intersection of law and the luxury goods market with an opportunity to explore career paths and creative opportunities within this intersection. Fashion Law is a practice composed of intellectual property, business, finance, international trade, labor, technology, regulation, and sustainability and ethics. Due to the distinct aspects of the fashion industry and legal practice, the Fashion Law Society intends to expand knowledge of career opportunities within the industry and discuss issues confronting the fashion industry through a legal lens. The Fashion Law Society seeks to accomplish its purpose by sponsoring activities, inviting guest speakers, and fostering meaningful conversations that highlight the dynamic and unique field of Fashion Law.
summary: Fashion reflects cultural expression, and the Fashion Law Society’s seeks to provide a space where members can explore their interest in fashion and artistic creativity can enhance their law school experience and formative years in the legal field.

name: Multilingual Health Science Student Organization
description: MHSSO is an organization dedicated to empowering future healthcare professionals at VU to develop multilingual proficiency and cultural humility for compassionate patient-centered care through collaboration, education, and community engagement.
summary: MHSSO is an organization dedicated to empowering future healthcare professionals at VU to develop multilingual proficiency and cultural humility for compassionate patient-centered care through collaboration, education, and community engagement.

name: Vanderbilt Owen Soccer Club
description:
The Vanderbilt Owen Soccer Club is a student organization designed specifically for the Owen Graduate School of Management community. The club combines recreational soccer with structured discussion and social engagement centered on the sport.

Unlike traditional intramural or pickup teams, the club is open to students of all interest levels, including players, fans, and those interested in strategy, leadership, and team dynamics. Activities include group watch parties for major matches, informal discussions on tactics and decision-making, and optional small-sided games against other Vanderbilt graduate schools.

Non-playing roles such as organization, analysis, and event coordination ensure broad participation. The club’s goal is to foster an inclusive, low-pressure environment that builds community while using soccer as a platform for learning and connection.
summary: The Vanderbilt Owen Soccer Club brings together Owen MBA students interested in soccer through match viewing, discussion, and optional play, emphasizing strategy, leadership, and community rather than competitive performance.

name: Vanderbilt University School of Nursing Student Nurses' Association
description:
The Vanderbilt University School of Nursing Student Nurses’ Association will expand its mission by intentionally creating structures that empower student leaders to propose, pilot, and implement new ideas aligned with nursing education, advocacy, and community health. The organization will support innovation through committee-led initiatives, project-based leadership roles, and open forums that encourage student-driven programming.

New ideas and initiatives will be evaluated based on alignment with the organization’s mission, NSNA core values, and identified student needs. By maintaining flexible programming, fostering interdisciplinary collaboration, and encouraging leadership at all levels, the organization will remain responsive to emerging issues in nursing education and health care while cultivating future nurse leaders.
summary: Advancing nursing education, professional development, and advocacy. VUSN SNA supports leadership, ethical practice, interdisciplinary collaboration, and student engagement in education standards, policy, and community health.

name: WELL Nurses: Students for Wellness, Equity, Longevity & Leadership
description: WELL Nurses: Students for Wellness, Equity, Longevity & Leadership is a student organization at Vanderbilt University School of Nursing (VUSN) dedicated to advancing holistic health and well-being through evidence-based practice, cultural appreciation and connectedness, and wellness justice. We support student nurses in integrating holistic health and wellness practices into their personal lives to promote professional longevity. Our organization supplements VUSN’s holistic health and wellness offerings through continuing education opportunities, providing resources to the greater Vanderbilt community, and leading initiatives that cultivate healthier academic, clinical, and workplace cultures. We advocate for equitable access to holistic health and wellness resources for all individuals on and off campus, with a commitment to supporting vulnerable communities.
summary: WELL Nurses is a student organization at VUSN dedicated to advancing holistic health and well-being through evidence-based practice, cultural appreciation and connectedness, and wellness justice.

name: Kurdish Cultural Association
summary: The Kurdish Cultural Association promotes Kurdish culture, history, and community through events, education, and service.

name: The Diabetes Link
summary: The Diabetes Link is a national nonprofit empowering young adults with type 1 and type 2 diabetes through peer support, expert resources, and leadership opportunities, building community and practical life skills from college through early career.

name: The Neurodiversity Alliance
description: The Neurodiversity Alliance is an organization dedicated to building an inclusive community for neurodivergent students and allies at Vanderbilt University. The organization focuses on peer-to-peer support, leadership development, and education that promotes understanding of neurodiversity as a natural and valuable form of human diversity. Through campus programming, advocacy initiatives, and collaboration with a national nonprofit network, the Neurodiversity Alliance provides students with opportunities to engage in meaningful dialogue, develop leadership skills, and access resources such as mentoring frameworks, training programs, scholarships, and national events. By connecting individual student experiences with broader advocacy efforts, the organization aims to empower neurodivergent students while fostering a more informed and supportive campus environment.
