summary: The Neurodiversity Alliance supports neurodivergent students and allies through peer support, leadership development, and advocacy, while connecting Vanderbilt students to a national network advancing neurodiversity awareness and inclusion.

name: Vanderbilt Freedom Initiative
description:
We are a chapter affiliated with the International Justice Mission college chapter network but are taking an independent name for publicity purposes. We align with every aspect of IJM’s mission but are adding domestic efforts to expand our reach to educate, equip, and empower the Vanderbilt community and the greater Nashville area. The Vanderbilt Freedom Initiative will abide by International Justice Mission’s college chapters’ three pillars: engaging in advocacy to strengthen the United States’ response to modern day slavery, fundraising toward global rescue efforts, and committing to earnest prayer for injustice.

Partnership with International Justice Mission:

IJM is a global organization that partners with local authorities to combat trafficking and end modern day slavery by rescuing and restoring victims, holding perpetrators accountable, and helping strengthen public justice systems. According to the Global Slavery Index, as of 2025, 50 million people are victims of modern-day slavery globally. IJM actively works to rescue victims of these three types of human trafficking:

 

  1. Labor Trafficking uses deception, threats or violence to coerce someone to work for little to no pay. Although slavery has been outlawed in nearly every country, millions of men, women, and children are working as slaves in brick kilns, rice mills, garment factories, fishing operations, and many other industries. Labor trafficking is a violent crime. Physical and sexual assault are rampant. In many cases, IJM has documented people who have been beaten, gang raped, locked in tiny rooms, starved, and even killed. Victims who try and escape commonly report being tracked down, beaten, and returned to the facility.
  2. Sex Trafficking is a form of modern slavery in which someone deceives and sexually exploits another person for a profit. Sex trafficking includes exploitation in establishments (brothels), private networks (homes), and on the internet (online sexual exploitation). The largest victim demographic for sex trafficking is women and children.
  3. Online Sexual Exploitation is a form of sex trafficking. It is the livestreaming of sexual exploitation of children over the internet. Customers anywhere in the world search online and wire a secure payment to an adult who sets up the live stream.

 

Vanderbilt Specific Mission:

The primary purpose of this club is to educate, equip, and empower the entire Vanderbilt community to fight against human trafficking. Our hope is that students, professors, administrators, and staff will engage with our mission and answer the call to be agents of change. Most of our activities will be hands on and will include but are not limited: panels, volunteering and lobbying events, seminars, film screenings, and fundraising events. We believe Vanderbilt’s campus does not actively address the fight against human trafficking and our hope is to encourage and inspire the Vanderbilt community to raise awareness about the global injustice of human trafficking. Our hope is that the Vanderbilt Freedom Initiative will integrate human trafficking into the conversations of all communities on campus and provide the Vanderbilt community with the resources needed to combat human trafficking.
summary: Vanderbilt Freedom Initiative will educate, equip, and empower the Vanderbilt community to fight against human trafficking through our 3 pillars: advocacy to strengthen the response to trafficking, fundraising for rescue efforts, and prayer for injustice

name: Vanderbilt University Women's Rugby Football Club
description:
The Vanderbilt University Women's Rugby Football Club was first started in 1978 and remains an important part of Vandy Rugby's story, but unfortunately disbanded in the early 2000s. This is not a new team, but a revival. We are an inclusive women's rugby team seeking to provide teamwork, resilience, and competitive-spirit to the Vanderbilt community. 

As a team, we are dedicated to the core tenet of rugby: anyone can play. As such, our mission is to hone the skills of experienced players and to share our love of the sport with new teammates who may have no experience to begin with. 

The skills learned on our team will not only make a player superior on the pitch, but will translate well off the field. Sportsmanship and communication are essential in many areas of life, but are hard to develop in a vacuum. Here, these skills are sharpened along with forging a deep solidarity that will last long after graduation.
summary: An inclusive women's rugby team to support both experienced and new players alike.

name: Vanderbilt University Club Softball Team
description: VUCS is an organization to provide students with a space to learn, hone, and advance their softball skills, as well as to play on an official softball team in tournaments, games, and scrimmages. The club plans to hold practices twice a week and attend a few weekend tournaments per semester.
summary: A Vanderbilt University fast-pitch softball team.

name: Vandy Paws & Purpose
description: Our mission is to promote animal welfare, advocacy, and student well-being through volunteering with local Nashville animal shelters and related campus programs. We strive to support shelter missions through direct care, fundraising, and socialization. Our volunteers benefit from opportunities to interact with rescue animals in a healing way through meaningful care and empathy, play with adorable animals in need, and participate in campus-wide drives and events.
summary: Our mission is to promote animal welfare, advocacy, and student well-being through volunteering with Nashville animal shelters and campus programming. We strive to support rescue missions through dog and cat care, socialization, and community awareness.

name: Venezuelans in Vanderbilt Association
description: We are a group of Vanderbilt scholars devoted to supporting and promoting Venezuelan culture and studies inside and outside our campus.
summary: VIVA's mission is to become an interdisciplinary group of Vanderbilt scholars who support and promote Venezuelan culture and studies on campus.

name: School for Science and Math at Vanderbilt
description: Located on Vanderbilt’s campus, the SSMV is a program for high school students (grades 9-12). Participants attend courses on campus one day per week in addition to their regular high school schedule. The four-year curriculum focuses on interdisciplinary science and math exploration with an emphasis on project-based learning where students get the opportunity to engage in scientific reasoning and practice. While the SSMV has permanent staff and postdoctoral scholars, it is further supported by Vanderbilt faculty and students that share their expertise with students in the program. Because participants miss their regular high school courses once a week, they often request tutors during our afternoon study halls (M-Th 2-4:30).
summary: School for Science and Math at Vanderbilt

name: Vanderbilt Learning On Ball
description: Vanderbilt LOB is focused on providing high school students in the Metro Nashville area with personalized mentorship in preparation for college, through the shared love of sports.
summary: Vanderbilt LOB is focused on providing high school students in the Metro Nashville area with personalized mentorship in preparation for college, through the shared love of sports.

name: Engine for Art, Democracy & Justice
description: The Engine for Art, Democracy & Justice (or EADJ for short) is a research initiative started by Dr. María Magdalena Campos-Pons, an internationally renowned artist and the Cornelius Vanderbilt Endowed Chair & Professor of Fine Arts. It is a trans-institutional research initiative and an ongoing collaboration between Fisk University, the Frist Art Museum, Millions of Conversations, and Vanderbilt University that explores creative approaches to living together in the American South and Global South.
summary: The Engine for Art, Democracy & Justice (or EADJ for short) is a research initiative started by Dr. María Magdalena Campos-Pons, an internationally renowned artist and the Cornelius Vanderbilt Endowed Chair & Professor of Fine Arts.
